subject: May God give us courage to defend the truth
body:
There’s a sense right now—you can feel it—that something is shifting.

| | | [](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zidqk5lb6ejnum755gu86rvj907bme2159fqei6o_rp22sh2s8i6eor1cjj6ap32c1h2bs)

## ****
Dear Friend of Freedom,

There's a sense right now--you can feel it--that something is shifting.

People are weary of censorship, weary of hostility to faith, weary of watching freedom bow to ideology.

Some call it a "vibe shift."

But a vibe shift alone will not sustain a nation.

A cultural moment is fragile. It doesn't pass laws, build institutions, or defend the next generation's rights.

That's why Alliance Defending Freedom exists--[to turn conviction into action and ensure that liberty endures in law.](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2z91ama5pa68r7heioq3to5aprug0ltpnd4o835so_rp22sh2s8i6eor1cjj6ap32c1h2bs)

**America now stands alone in the Western world, still upholding robust protections for religious liberty and free expression.**

* In the UK and Europe, Christians have been arrested for praying silently outside abortion facilities and criminally prosecuted for posting Bible verses on social media.

* In Mexico, current and former congressmen have been convicted of "gender based political violence" for speaking biological truth.

* And here at home, our freedoms often hang by a single vote at the Supreme Court.

**Freedom is not self-perpetuating. It must be defended --case by case, precedent by precedent, generation by generation.**

Your gift today helps ADF defend that freedom in the courtroom, protect those silenced for their faith, and secure lasting victories that no administration can undo.

# **[Judge Boasberg reverses course on Jan. 6 defendants pardoned by Trump](http://trk.libertyledger._c2V5aWQ9In0/_R0RteUQAQ9y52Dz34WwBQ)**
---

| Peter, before I hit the road again to meet with people where they are all across CO-04, I wanted to give you a quick update on this race. Lauren Boebert just passed the biggest wealth transfer from the working class to billionaires in U.S. history. She voted to gut health care access, slash food assistance, defund education and shutter rural hospitals across Colorado.
And then? She walked away from the communities that will feel the pain the most. She skips town halls. She bails on key votes. She won't meet with the people who she was elected to serve.
That's not leadership. That's cowardice. It's not how I was raised. And it's not how I intend to lead.
I'm running for Congress because I believe leadership means showing up, listening and standing up for the people you serve. That's why I'm on the road every day, listening to what folks in this district have to say and what changes they need Congress to make. But while I'm talking with folks about good-paying jobs, clean energy and standing up to corporate greed, Lauren Boebert's allies are ready to dump millions into this race to attack us and protect her seat. If we want to flip this district and bring pro-worker leadership to Washington, we need to make sure we have the resources to build momentum, expand our reach and prove that our grassroots campaign can take on Boebert's billionaire-funded machine. That's why I'm raising $12,000 before midnight tonight - and why I need you with me.
[Will you chip in $25 before I hit the road again so that I can build up the momentum I need to win CO-04 and flip the House blue?](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001gvtpWJK-0TeXlrQS5E9E1Tr8QneasNPJjNxeBShZw9r2a4akFDNLkbzFFpm4JSY4-yPinjJt4fjcrqCn4LkPZsLFdVCl90j1NyKdTKHrjH2DdFdZsyhyVjMZXb0UA-sNjFSTVHrj4y2reIJg2cbO2dw_2XskojVRyDV3pxUL0DFHZeVv0ZlZS92Uq8W7c-VFgii40Z-e8ePBZGCCpfRo4Z61LKaRViOXkHnr5OnXcoE=&c=9QYmlM3s6lx1ob6YdP1ZMj6Zs_xGBnIBT_fKS9YhsS8qXllTIrjGSQ==&ch=Cuv7cQWd3SFhMNCjFIECwL_HcZaSSb8MiOADkWIq2XTKU6VuUFs1yA==)

| | Patriot, Did you know the U.S. government has the legal power to **change the price of gold** with the stroke of a pen? [Let me share what Washington insiders are **quietly discussing**...](https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=cb11253c935ad54c14811f68146e82925f3154c03e357e863536565a0094cbbccdf326dcaf0c3a3bda8927ebb58b759758c3304d02269f1f4e8668fa8030b88e) My father's administration is exploring a powerful economic tool: the **Gold Reserve Act** (U.S. Code Title 31, Section 5117).This law gives the Treasury the authority to **revalue America's gold reserves** on the national balance sheet from their outdated book value of $42 to current market prices. Why would my father's administration do this? It would...
1. Immediately strengthen America's financial position
2. Signal gold's renewed importance in our monetary system
3. Prepare America for a new era of sound money principles

But here's what the mainstream media won't tell you:
---
| | **This Revaluation Would Create a Perfect Storm for Gold Investors**
---
**This Revaluation Would Create a Perfect Storm for Gold Investors**
| | | When the government officially acknowledges gold's true value, it elevates gold's status as a monetary asset across the entire financial system. The resulting demand could cause a **surge in gold prices.** The potential upside for gold investors is substantial. And luckily, **there's a simple way to position yourself to benefit...** A **Gold IRA** is a tax-advantaged retirement account that holds _**physical gold**_ instead of paper assets. **[Click here to request your FREE Info Kit on Gold IRAs](https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=cb11253c935ad54c3ad2b876fdb2514bcaba4bc5dd963fa8e741dcdbef4c1ec99effb28cf40e62f691684760158be0077b4a52064a01f10b615f9bf80ff24f2a)** from my trusted friends at Birch Gold Group.

---
| | This comprehensive guide reveals how a Gold IRA works and why it's the **perfect vehicle to benefit from the coming gold revaluation**. Plus, it shows you, step by step, how to legally make this move without paying taxes or penalties. **[Get your copy of this free info kit now](https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=cb11253c935ad54c3ad2b876fdb2514bcaba4bc5dd963fa8e741dcdbef4c1ec99effb28cf40e62f691684760158be0077b4a52064a01f10b615f9bf80ff24f2a)** and implement these strategic moves BEFORE this wealth-multiplying policy becomes public knowledge!

Donald Trump Jr.
Birch Gold Group **Last Thing:** Wall Street insiders are already quietly increasing their gold positions in anticipation of this policy shift. **Good Morning!** On this day in 1848, President James K. Polk confirmed the discovery of gold in California in an address to Congress, sparking the [California Gold Rush in 1849](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHOpCAcB58GO4z8RdSC4hq779orDcJP5RbBALubffvLXXJP8JXTTCbz62v9Mj74ePw0F3Td0xrxLgG1Iq8mpnoi3zAWDiYge2sePj9W6mjggng3rM748Fll4_QOEPoGWoxKzj1Nfd-c2tJujOrJCqmGSdJEZpZQJPZ-dsINjdd_bYK6QYhuFKJV27bJ3U1Kjd0sR8VkB-dryu3v5GM9sYdPeiG3Nl1N0Getd2H9D0YLo-XyISBbxIrcpnwwWuoJfmcU7xDth9HyH6o3gZcbsCcKo8XB4tqQ-cDFJCe-p2euJzcxPk3gF0oxB7hN8cgopcna3W8fgi9qZLI7LuPDv6b6rZEvTX8CAAD___jTiR4). ## US Brokers Peace Deal for Rwanda, Congo
President Trump hosted the leaders of Rwanda and the Democratic Republic of Congo on Thursday as they signed a [U.S.-brokered peace agreement](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1ugzAUxfGnMVvQ9bUxyeChUsWWrh2RPy5wi4ORYwXl7Sv6Cp2P9JPO__s-3h0n3uYv9yBbpzxudDwT1UpldFuuC5WdXKBILlHh4FYu64iA3UXiBboxOk7vUTfRTkRIqiEre6NvCq9KNYsNRnYgIULwk9SKrpoC9B46FSB0UjdsT00idFJCL2VrvPd6ildjerjp3ggNFLnm0v5k3upCU3rnF5U25EeT7FLr_hTqQ-AgcDiOo_U-nJvA4TwjcEj8IoFDuKktrTivb1WFGnY3k1Cf2BQb94NT4qfphYb54Tj94fVfRV4WfwMAAP__e9ZytQ) aimed at ending a 30-year conflict. The deal, dubbed the Washington Accords, was signed at the [newly renamed Donald J. Trump Institute](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO3CAURb8Gd4zgYWNPQTFRZCmKNkVSpLSweaxfFoMFeK35-8jzC9td6UpX95y_b9ObpUDx_Zfd0FSfpohnCVgr5snGVFfMO9oFHdqAmRb7QfljAgEdl8BFNzlL4Tm1jTMeEVA1aGSv27uCQalmNVZ71E53Cmd3X2anAAYhpL93PQztgA2Za02C6KQUvZQ3Pc9z692gdS_uba9ZK9BRTfn2L1GsK_rwTJ-Yb0vammDWWvfC1IPByGA8z_O2lXh1DEaM_CgMxguKwbinQJWWK9Z8bDvPGO2GhR-FUyyV6lGRJ89fyNz6ipmvtBUMnsFoM3885O8fP5-aqTEt5Jj6_u2Sh2f5g3lvsnH7SSFQ0T1rxftmKbxu1i-5_TTwPwAA___6kI6k) of Peace and includes a ceasefire, disarmament of militias, refugee returns, and accountability for war crimes. Presidents Paul Kagame of Rwanda and Felix Tshisekedi of the Democratic Republic of Congo endorsed the pact [despite ongoing fighting](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkbGunDAQRb_GdLPCBsy-gmKjBEWRXoqkSIkGzxicNTayvYv27yP2F1JNcaW5Ouf--Zw-0XkXlp-48VBsnAIf2XMpnCYMsaycdkbDxOg5OYN3l-6TqlUHUkHdTYTOv6a2osEyK24qHmSv249GXZumWgdq676W-tob2dmOe8bWWk2zMmQ0Glu54fwmVd1JWfdSXvQ8z62lq9Z9_dH2WrQ1kysxXf5GF8rK1r_ik9PFxK3yw1rKnkVzE2oUajyO47LlcGZCjRzgkYUaT6gzjMmTUGNJj22HOWIuGVZGyhAtpAMDIWAgMDEsEQ7nPcwM62NZXFhgjZ7eFwNlwAxl5Rdkt5wtMKd458QEb19wChNqxAS3m_z1Y_z-TTRjNI5E8_XLKZyP_JvTXqWB9rPJZd2Ltl42dP6NVv5rj-eg_gUAAP___BSh6w) reported the same day. Trump declared the leaders “great” and predicted swift progress. The deal opens the region's vast critical mineral reserves to American companies, countering Chinese dominance in resources like cobalt, copper, and gold.
## FBI Arrests Man in DC Pipe Bomb Case
Investigators identified and arrested a suspect in a [long-running D.C. pipe bomb case](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DuOpDAYBODTmAzktyFwsAlZb7oh8uOn-bcNRranUd9-xFxhspJK-qSqf4_l4TDh8fzrdrBtzcsBV03QGpTFHbltUE5wASK4BAWDe2F5LZxy1TPeU7VEh-mzyC7aFYCD6MAyo-Uk-ChEt1mpR-aCopMxY9BKKyFV8JOTUoTg49ihvTXGqWKMGsYG7b2Xaxy1NnSSRhNJIWLLZfif8WgbrOmT31CGkPcu2a21sxLxh_CZ8Pm6riH4eq-4e8LnOxI-n3hC7_Pu-_pVTwitd6VAbYTPXbHxvDAlrNoQSZ-7w_Sjt19d8rb8OwAA__-9HXT8) discovered on Jan. 6, 2021, though they say there's no confirmed connection linking it the unrest at the Capitol that day. The FBI says [30-year-old Brian Cole Jr.](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O3CAUhZ8Gd3dkwMbjgmKiyEWkTZEUKS1-Luu7i8ECdqzN00feV0h3pCMdne_787K-GIqUXn-aHXULeU141oitYVlNym3DcqBx6NFELOTMO5X3VfRiBC6gH1dvKH6uQ-d1QBQoO9R8UsMsxV3KbtNCSmODmx36cZJ8xlk4b-0d5RSU4kNH-lrjoh857yfOb8paOwR_V2rq52FSbOjRU8vl9pYptQ1D_MxPLDeX9y7qrbWjMvlgYmFiOc_zttd0dUwsmOCjMrFcUEwsX_ncMlAFW8gkcDkiBEtAHlOjQFihftQDXQNK8GYSKPAODjoQbN4tOFORicUUeDz4rx_x75PJJTvyTH7_donEs_7GcnRF--OkGKmqiQ39624ofl1u_-X5qcW_AAAA__-VlJJf) of Virginia was linked to the two explosive devices through cellphone data, license-plate records, and purchases of bomb-making materials dating back several years. The bombs never detonated, but officials say they posed a serious threat and diverted law enforcement resources. Prosecutors haven't outlined a clear motive, leaving major questions about intent and whether he acted alone. [See the suspect's photo here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OpDAQhJ_GZD3CPwNDQHAJ2V56IWrbzdI7xkZtz67m7U_cK1xcqk9V35-P9QM5cf78jQfNbStrpp-aqDWSFXNpO8lJGCgSJhIO-GR5rqY3d9AG-vsakdN7dV2cNyJDtqNZj4ObrHlY2-3zfbA-jDpMFCacnHWTt0E_SD88DSPajueLpk1_17oftb4N3nu3xccwjP3kxkG5niK3IrevwrnttKV3-Sa5hXJ0ad5bO6uyv5RZlFny-yy1XZEyywVWZtFGmaV3yiyvCtc_ZRYM4VUpQgxw8kngy-FJwAtjhlASwZdAhQ0DwSsfWJ8UYSsCG0tt0PggwK2RAIpQbcAZhOpLrkaEcy-tKLN0Msfzh1PiOozK9Z8Hcvo3vP2X7e_Z_A0AAP__5UuUGw).
## Pantone Chooses Off-White as 2026 Color
The Pantone Color Institute has announced [Cloud Dancer](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL9u6yAUh58Gb1hwsLE9MNzFW-7a0TqGQ0xDwMIkVt6-StWtY-ffH336Pi7LBUMM6fof72Sqz0ui84hUK5UFU64blZ3QkiOMVILFWyi3BQT0XAIX_eIwxNfSNc54IiDVkJGD7iYFo1LNZhAHZR2MEnwvPNGKo1r1oKmb_OjU1ATzfpMgeinFIGWr13XtvBu1HsTUDZp1glyoubSfOaS6kY-v_KTS2nxvotlq3Q-m_jGYGcznebY7ppoTvXMGM5YabKSDwWxzzIVnz-tG_EVYGMxbPnnN_HEQ_9nxXzUOAjS3MT8cd5gsFR4S30t2D1uPphi3nyHGcOiBdeJ6xxC_4eqfjD4NfAUAAP__VNGMZA), a soft, luminous white, as its 2026 Color of the Year. It's the first time the global color authority has chosen a white hue for the designation since the tradition began in 1999. Described as a "billowy, balanced white," the shade, PANTONE 11-4201, is intended to be a "[whisper of calm in a noisy world](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykUD1vwyAU_DX2hgUPG-KBoUu2dO1oPeA5psFgYRIr_75yt84d70Onu_u6TTcMMaT7J65k6pynRMceqVYqE6ZcFyoboSNPGKkEh49QHhNwGJgAxofJY4jvqW-9mYmAZEtGaNWPEi5StovheiQEyxVaqZFbPUrpL2IgsCQcDW0wZ5oAPgjBtRCdstb2s78opfnYa9X0nHyouXTfOaS60Bzf-UWlc3lto1lq3fZGfjRwbeB6HEeXrDtXnHoD1y1vzD1jfRb6i9hpOilMNSdiCVfaGXBQzOWYC3sTFlZcQui1VKotxm9HiDHsSjc9v68Y4m-J-q_nXgZ-AgAA___tW4KR)," reflecting a cultural desire for quiet reflection and a sense of serenity in a frenetic society. Executive director Leatrice Eiseman called the discrete shade a "[promise of clarity](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFy6yAURL8GdXjggpBVULyGzq9NqbkSF4sYgwYRO_r7jPILaXdnzuyej9t0w5hivv_HJ9kWypTpvSdqjeqEubSV6ka4kCdMVOOCj1gfEwjouQQu-sljTMekO28DEZDqyMrB6FHBValutXMYterxSkoYPxpAr4TBEWGAxROaLtqTJkH0UopByouZ51kHfzVmEKMeDNOCfGylXj5LzG2lkI7yonpZyrNLdm1t25n6x8AxcPnYyt7OioE7wQycBAZOaAYuxUB7OxIxcBvmVjLtHAQYvpRUKi-Bt5X4QVh5pdf52XPMnsfG3zElXinhNz_KFwPXVeu3M427GZgW9yfG9Duq_cnky8JPAAAA__-qVoZB) needed in a time of transformation, succeeding last year's warm brown, Mocha Mousse.

The deal, dubbed the Washington Accords, was signed at the of Peace and includes a ceasefire, disarmament of militias, refugee returns, and accountability for war crimes. Presidents Paul Kagame of Rwanda and Felix Tshisekedi of the Democratic Republic of Congo endorsed the pact reported the same day. Trump declared the leaders “great” and predicted swift progress. The deal opens the region's vast critical mineral reserves to American companies, countering Chinese dominance in resources like cobalt, copper, and gold. FBI Arrests Man in DC Pipe Bomb Case Investigators identified and arrested a suspect in a discovered on Jan. 6, 2021, though they say there's no confirmed connection linking it the unrest at the Capitol that day. The FBI says of Virginia was linked to the two explosive devices through cellphone data, license-plate records, and purchases of bomb-making materials dating back several years. The bombs never detonated, but officials say they posed a serious threat and diverted law enforcement resources. Prosecutors haven't outlined a clear motive, leaving major questions about intent and whether he acted alone. . Pantone Chooses Off-White as 2026 Color The Pantone Color Institute has announced , a soft, luminous white, as its 2026 Color of the Year. It's the first time the global color authority has chosen a white hue for the designation since the tradition began in 1999. Described as a "billowy, balanced white," the shade, PANTONE 11-4201, is intended to be a "," reflecting a cultural desire for quiet reflection and a sense of serenity in a frenetic society. So Far](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3159258&post_id=180316647&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwMzE2NjQ3LCJpYXQiOjE3NjQ5MzM0MTYsImV4cCI6MTc2NzUyNTQxNiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMxNTkyNTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.1Alp4JAAw080ej8TFE_ZZxXEs4HwHGCJ_cPA6GkVPaI)

Welcome to The Angry Democrat
Don't recognize this sender? [Unsubscribe with one click](https://substack..VXzV4KfVcmd6poIr5rVIA5rurRvtlcfthzy549-dbpI?)
The Angry Democrat recently imported your email address from another platform to Substack. You'll now receive their posts via email or the [Substack app](https://substack.com/redirect/5178e794-2ab8-4a04-bbea-21f2ac7e82e3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). To set up your profile and discover more on Substack, [click here](https://substack.com/redirect/8b6ba7f7-f44f-47a8-8e87-de283739be77?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
**Understanding the New Ohio 7th District**
This is not really an opinion piece. It is more of a breakdown of what is happening in Ohio's 7th Congressional District. Obviously, this is a district I used to know extremely well, until the maps were changed again. And with every redraw, the political math changes too.
This time, the district leans a little more Republican than before. Even so, Democrats see blood in the water for incumbent Max Miller. They believe 2026 could be a national wave year, and that even districts leaning red might be competitive. That includes Ohio 7.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/6670bb80-6226-46f3-b4fe-41ce30033100?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
**How the District Used to Look**
When I ran, Ohio 7 included part of Cuyahoga County, all of Medina County, all of Wayne County, and a sliver of Holmes County. A diverse spread of suburban and rural regions, from Brunswick to Wooster to Medina. In that configuration, I lost by about 10 points, which was not surprising given the partisan makeup at the time.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3fff1ebe-c35c-4ec2-8eb7-90853eb2387f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Ohio 7th 2024
The new Ohio 7 still keeps the same general region in Cuyahoga County. It still includes all of Medina County. But instead of all of Wayne County, the district now includes only about half of it, splitting Wooster right down the middle. Then, for the first time, it jumps south and picks up Ashland County.
That Ashland addition and the loss of the more Democratic areas of Wayne County shift the district more conservative. It is not a dramatic shift, but it is enough to make the race even more of a climb for Democrats.
And that is the reality. There is no point sugarcoating it.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c42e2966-af03-4a0e-959a-0313caf951ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Ohio 7th 2026
* * *
**The Candidates Who Have Declared**
There are several Democrats already lined up to challenge Max Miller. With a competitive primary shaping up, voters will get a real chance to hear from a range of voices, platforms, and campaign styles. That is healthy. Primaries create contrast. They sharpen candidates. They force real conversation about priorities.
And for a district that is not naturally blue, Democrats absolutely need the strongest possible nominee.
Let's meet the candidates:

**[Brian Poindexter](https://substack.com/redirect/58eb8da6-b99c-42a6-8fd3-4e6e87a3d5a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/69065201-4278-47c9-9815-004454ff3e4a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
A union ironworker from Cleveland (Local 17), apprenticeship instructor, former labor organizer, and Brook Park councilman. Poindexter frames his campaign around giving working families real representation in Washington, arguing that workers are consistently overlooked while corporations and the wealthy get preferential treatment. He positions himself as a hands-on, calloused-knuckle advocate for the overworked and underpaid, running on a working-class, pro-labor, "seat at the table" message.
**[Ed FitzGerald](https://substack.com/redirect/f844b046-6863-4658-9dec-1359766cb410?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a3b7f261-c7cc-416e-917e-f90d94a5ece8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
A former Lakewood Mayor, former Cuyahoga County Executive, and ex-FBI Special Agent who investigated corruption and organized crime. FitzGerald highlights a long record of government reform, transparency, and efficient local governance. After a decade as a small-business owner, he says he's running because he's disgusted with the state of Washington and wants to challenge Max Miller directly. He leans heavily on experience, corruption-fighting credentials, and a promise to restore good government.
**[Scott Schulz](https://substack.com/redirect/feb0440d-8dff-4553-91ce-ce39077d2b7f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e2ad8d8f-7878-42c0-8d6f-36b542b673d5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
An educator and university admissions leader with deep ties to Northeast Ohio schools. Schulz has helped thousands of first-generation students access higher education and currently serves on the Bay Village Board of Education, where he navigated pandemic challenges and secured school funding. He presents himself as a bipartisan problem-solver focused on education, jobs, inflation, and healthcare. A family-oriented candidate with military-friendly credentials, Schulz emphasizes integrity, pragmatism, and community-driven leadership.
**[Michael Eisner (Cancer Survivor& Justice Advocate)](https://substack.com/redirect/c73d5471-f70f-4933-9142-8659ba2955e0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a7846092-d710-4948-a3ca-5efae469c122?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
A justice-focused candidate who has spent his career representing people who've been wronged. A cancer survivor, he says the care he received inspired him to fight for accessible, compassionate healthcare and a fairer version of the American Dream. He highlights his blended family, personal resilience, and a commitment to confronting Max Miller. He frames his run around compassion, fairness, and standing up for ordinary people.
* * *
**My Thoughts on the Race**
People have already asked for my opinions on these candidates and how I think the primary will shake out. The truth is, offering opinions this early would be presumptuous. Nobody has started debating yet. Nobody has been tested. Nobody has faced real scrutiny. Campaigns reveal things about candidates that no resume ever will.
I have my predictions. I have my early reads on who has strengths and who faces headwinds. But I want to give these candidates the space to campaign before I give my full analysis.
Closer to the primary, I will give my full predictions on how the field stacks up. Until then, I am keeping my powder dry.
I am hopeful we see surprises. I am hopeful we see strong campaigns. I am hopeful we see candidates who talk about the issues that actually matter to working families. And I am hopeful that Ohio 7 shows what so many other districts across the country are showing in 2026. A Democratic electorate hungry for something new.
Whatever happens, this is a race worth watching. And I will be watching it closely.
* * *

It is not a dramatic shift, but it is enough to make the race even more of a climb for Democrats. And that is the reality. There is no point sugarcoating it. | | ---|---|--- Ohio 7th 2026 The Candidates Who Have Declared There are several Democrats already lined up to challenge Max Miller. With a competitive primary shaping up, voters will get a real chance to hear from a range of voices, platforms, and campaign styles. That is healthy. Primaries create contrast. They sharpen candidates. They force real conversation about priorities. And for a district that is not naturally blue, Democrats absolutely need the strongest possible nominee. Let's meet the candidates: | | ---|---|--- A union ironworker from Cleveland (Local 17), apprenticeship instructor, former labor organizer, and Brook Park councilman. Poindexter frames his campaign around giving working families real representation in Washington, arguing that workers are consistently overlooked while corporations and the wealthy get preferential treatment. He positions himself as a hands-on, calloused-knuckle advocate for the overworked and underpaid, running on a working-class, pro-labor, "seat at the table" message. | | ---|---|--- A former Lakewood Mayor, former Cuyahoga County Executive, and ex-FBI Special Agent who investigated corruption and organized crime. FitzGerald highlights a long record of government reform, transparency, and efficient local governance. After a decade as a small-business owner, he says he's running because he's disgusted with the state of Washington and wants to challenge Max Miller directly. He leans heavily on experience, corruption-fighting credentials, and a promise to restore good government. | | ---|---|--- An educator and university admissions leader with deep ties to Northeast Ohio schools. Schulz has helped thousands of first-generation students access higher education and currently serves on the Bay Village Board of Education, where he navigated pandemic challenges and secured school funding. He presents himself as a bipartisan problem-solver focused on education, jobs, inflation, and healthcare. A family-oriented candidate with military-friendly credentials, Schulz emphasizes integrity, pragmatism, and community-driven leadership. | | ---|---|--- A justice-focused candidate who has spent his career representing people who've been wronged. A cancer survivor, he says the care he received inspired him to fight for accessible, compassionate healthcare and a fairer version of the American Dream. He highlights his blended family, personal resilience, and a commitment to confronting Max Miller. He frames his run around compassion, fairness, and standing up for ordinary people. My Thoughts on the Race People have already asked for my opinions on these candidates and how I think the primary will shake out. The truth is, offering opinions this early would be presumptuous. Nobody has started debating yet. Nobody has been tested. Nobody has faced real scrutiny. Campaigns reveal things about candidates that no resume ever will. I have my predictions. I have my early reads on who has strengths and who faces headwinds. But I want to give these candidates the space to campaign before I give my full analysis. Closer to the primary, I will give my full predictions on how the field stacks up. Until then, I am keeping my powder dry. I am hopeful we see surprises. I am hopeful we see strong campaigns. I am hopeful we see candidates who talk about the issues that actually matter to working families. And I am hopeful that Ohio 7 shows what so many other districts across the country are showing in 2026. A Democratic electorate hungry for something new. Whatever happens, this is a race worth watching. And I will be watching it closely. Are you an ANGRY DEMOCRAT? If so, the please share with other Angry Dems. Join otherANGRY DEMOCRATS** by support Matt's work. Subscribe and Share! | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Matt Diemer 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

body:

| | | [](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJykUcuO2zAM_Br5lkCibCc5-BCs4aJAt9hTezQYiusI1QuS7KJ_X7hwvqAHAiRnMOSQP9_nd7TOhuU7eh5os3Muc3kyoWcX4y8bls-Yn-zSn7guM0joTgpOstt5IabMj5jDrBozkOk1NDyoS9_edC-1ap7DlW4aW93S9SpbZej66C7UyovhG5DER2OHXVOB3EOCOtNn25lO0k1JyUgX0co1GaxczhRD4bxhtRvbEOKexXCm6Bs3PGtNRei7gEnAtNiNz8d2O0HAdFQCJkyJ0RWhp0JCj2_3b0pC9-VDQI--Cj12vYB-KUKPyAL6tfq5xDUTCz2mHEtiqjYsB-TZ2NULPbJH644moU9olyD0SOg4GMwvJIbKYR_zcT-9_fh6eh2gyYNJv61ztvS77WWX--eu_s9ntgH-BgAA__89oqDI)

Our Network Clinics across the nation provide **free ultrasounds** , **holistic** **compassionate care** , and the **gospel of Jesus Christ** to women in crisis. Because when a mother sees her baby for the first time, when she hears that heartbeat, everything changes. In fact, 81% of women who receive an ultrasound, can see their baby and hear the heartbeat, will choose life for their babies. And your **$28 gift provides one life-saving ultrasound** and gives her the chance to meet her baby and hear of God's love for her. Kit Dahlstrom Nancy's Husband and #1 Supporter P.S. - Please don't wait. ## Patrick Proposes $1000 for Every Newborn
Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick [announced plans](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGypCAURL9GMyxABA0INjGbTTe0QK4jtQhTcJWZv3_l_MILOus6XX3-PZaH8cHH519zgMb3lpYItQRAhLyw_1vKcEH-RKg25WgN7jmlY-GUD4RxQofFGR8-i2idttRNrAXNlBRTL-k4tbue6CiM5VIOIAantlVuFCyTbh2VEsK2Xt8wxukdxmU3WDX2Qg0jc31vrWkEBecx5Q53QHibsoVPuiB3azraoHfEV2n6Pw2fGz7XWrstvZU5C_p4Vxo-358aPmM-jxcx65rOiIV8WaSmMzji0mkDEB83E5EUc_n4LG3W7lV9CL5I1Qj6PIwP31X8natL858AAAD__1OngHw) to create a state program that would give every Texas newborn $1,000 for stock market investment. The proposed [New Little Texan Savings Fund](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0Ltu7CAQxvGnwd2umAFfKCiOdORuo3QprcHAGi02KzzZy9tHziuk-Jqv-BX_r8t0oZTTdv2gNVh-xTJt4bnnwBzqBLdYaniE-t7C05W6OeKllrJOKLE9AZ5kO3lK-T3pxlsnvYEmWOg7bVQnB9MsFgYd9eBbR_0QIc4q9s4AzN4rRTSbJtkDA5THALtz6_pB6b4dwCvlHAktg09c6pmXwOFFe8zv8gj1PJe1yXZhvu9C_RM4Chxfxytw_E_bJ3FN803guDPx9y5wBGM61KCkRAPaSAmmqdbfnynntHe90PK6Usq_NP8tyMPiTwAAAP__rEZyIA) would cost about $400 million a year and would require a constitutional amendment to make it permanent. The idea builds on a [new federal program](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OozAcxJ8GOiN_YlK4uIYu116J_v4gWDF2zjZxePsVW22_xUgj_aTRzPy7L3fwwcfHX9idqp81LdG1ElytLi_kuabs3i6f0TWdctRQt5zSvlBMBSIUYbFY8OFceG-VxvZGeqeIHPmNjXi69ZuaOOhxFMIClVKwVVAsDQHDpNF6ZbL36gojFF8idByElhPjUkzEMqY1dBw762vKQ91cdR8oazjT2-XBpL0Paqv1VTr2p6NzR-fW2hC1uVZcvKOzPoqPrpQfFl24o3PNx_5CYEw6Yi1Igz7R_wOCX09EMMboGVND2USgXBIm-qzsq_kQfBllx_FjBx--a9TfnfdW9CsAAP__MduElA), signed into law under President Trump, that invests $1,000 for U.S.-born babies between 2025 and 2028, with funds accessible when the child turns 18. Patrick says the program would [teach financial literacy](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O4yAUhZ8Gd0Tmz-CCYhu62XZL6wLXCQqGCDP25O1XzitMcapP-nTO-fe1fEHKqdz_woa2_6x1KXjuGXvHtrDnWhse2N4FT19b8dAfrdZt4SNXlHE6qiVCyu9FDtH6Mc5sQMv0JGcxjWYeHhZWkBOTSrN5BaH0NGsZNDPgQ5hmIYZkLxnj4xXGp5vy2giplWFRCO-ByBFj6rXd-gM7_sC-5nc9sN1C3YZsH72_diL-EO4Id-d53p7HN16QcAetp5CRcHftItzlGiAT7j4iwl2EQl_QWwpPWvCkOfWekV647HSHI5X7TtfvEgl3fJqpDKNQSniqlZJUagRqFKzUGK-MYQHljEOz8XWmnNM-aSLH-wYpf-r23518WP4_AAD__6sRjpQ) and build long-term wealth for young Texans.
## Power Grid Could Face Winter Strain
A new [reliability report warns](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOtCAURp8GOyZwQdGC4m_s5m-3NCDXkSyKgbvjzNtvnFfY4qtOcvLlfN2nu4sp7o__bkNLryVPO541IRGWSX4vueATy3vH0-eye0dryXmbQEDLJXDRTsHF9J50E6wXYZANWmk6PahO9EOz2l7pWYcO1YKDMgs4r6WXi-9606J0son2kkkQ1yR0t9abXmnT9jIo5b1jWmCIlMuNViR8ubqkd35iuc15a5JdiY7K1D8GI4PR-VmqizAYj1yJwbjjyQseuRCvaz4rD44cn3EnLJXP-ScFXqm4uPOPnj9KDPyMF2cwyh60EcowGJtiw3HGlGLtDNPisbmYPjfob_GeFn4DAAD__8_Rg8A) that the surge in new data centers statewide raises concerns about blackouts for Texas's power grid this winter. The North American Electric Reliability Corporation cautions that nearly 100 registered data centers are now in operation, up from just 13 prior to 2021. The facilities [consume large quantities](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0Duu8yAQBeDV4A4LBj_iguJv3OVvb2nxGGIUbCI8Ccnur5wt3GKkkY70Hen8XJeriSnut_9mQ03vkJcd65GQCMsi7yEXfGH57FhtLrs1tJactwUE9FwCF_3iTUyfpWu8tsJPskEtx6Gb1CAuU7NqpbA3GIJ1ynrrwgR-6odxnIIYnAfZRH1iEsR5Eoa2t-NFdWN_kV4paw3rBPpIubS0IuHbHCF98gtL6_LWJL0SPQ6m_jGYGcy11tbt1p0hg_m0GcxSnj8wmL8A94YMd7gTFm4ix-IycZuMu-cn8UeuWHh-krlhu9KWmqL9o8aU4jGMrBO3zcT0rae_jfbS8BsAAP__spGBIw) of electricity around the clock, potentially shifting peak demand times. ERCOT officials project just a 1% outage risk for January. Many Texans, though, remain skeptical, recalling that the power grid's failure during [Winter Storm Uri](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykULuOqzAQ_RrTgezBvAoXt6HLbbdENh6CtX5Ew4gk-_Ur8gtbnOa8dHS-bsvNhhjy_b9NaPi1lSXj84jIjLSo760QnkjvjE9XKDvLO5WSFpDQ1Qpq2S3ehvhedOWNk35SFRo19HpqezlO1W462SFAL9fRt71W2m_ST-g0wDYNsLoqmKtMgbygoG86N4ytHrpR-bZ1zgot0Qcu1PCOjC97bPFdTqRmLamKZmd-HKL9J2AWMGM-A5WcMLNNSGG1TaG7gPmTFDCvmBlJwGyJwxrx4njH-qPXGyH-YM0hYQwZ67LVeGLmy1WR8Y9niDEc_SC0vCcb4mcE_-2608BvAAAA__9qUoTU) in February 2021 left 4.5 million people without power for several days.
## Austin First Responders Get Bulletproof Vests
Austin firefighters and paramedics now carry bulletproof vests after nearly 300 [were distributed citywide](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2SrCAUhJ9GMyz-RA0IbmI2N93QguEwUoviwhmZefstdx9hg066vuqu7o_bcjMhhv3x32yg8eXTskMtERAhL-zTpwwn5PcO1aa8W4NrTmlbOOU9YZzQfnEmxPciW6ctdRNrQbNByUkoOk7tqrnwyt77u5y494rJ3rIRvABlYWRGsjboK4xxeolx1fV2GIUc-pE5Iaw1jaTgAqbc4QoIL1N8fKcTcndPWxv1iniURvxr-Nzwudba-fQazLNg2C-k4fO1qeHzr0d8yODDY0XIhdQQI9lTJRYIfD3DcYAj9hkj4JFT8uSEgqXN2h0XGooaGkkfmwnxpx__9tqp-XcAAAD__7hRhJk) this week. The lightweight steel plate carriers protect against pistol and most rifle rounds, marking the first time every Austin firefighter has ballistic protection. Austin Fire Department Captain Russ Howard said the vests [help crews concentrate](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L_OpCAUBfCnwY4JIP4rKLaxm223NCDX8WYQDFx15u03fq_wFac6yS8n599zeloMGF9_7QaGPkuaIlwlABHkSb6XlOGE_I1wuZSjs7TmlLZJCdVwqbhoJm8xfCddeeOEH2QFRnatHupW9EO1mt75HsC5dulqOQxCyd7a2ekWBqu1Xio0NyaVuCNV-2hc19e6a3rp69o5y7QAj5Tyg1Yg-NiyhG86IT_mtFXBrER7YfUfpkamxjUlHzDCXTI13jZTo1RMjfYohJEvmIHPGa7Cy4HEj51j5O4IAWjPKS38hEKF28JnG0LhLyCesbwRMlNjlY3fLwwBS9sxLV6bxfAzhX534GnU_wAAAP__HZ-GCQ) on emergency response instead of worrying about personal safety. "_We heard that whenever our fire department and EMS were going out with our police department, a lot of them were feeling like they were in danger or approaching dangerous situations_ ," said Ashley Copeland with the nonprofit group that helped fund the vests.

Dan Patrick to create a state program that would give every Texas newborn $1,000 for stock market investment. The proposed would cost about $400 million a year and would require a constitutional amendment to make it permanent. The idea builds on a , signed into law under President Trump, that invests $1,000 for U.S.-born babies between 2025 and 2028, with funds accessible when the child turns 18. Patrick says the program would and build long-term wealth for young Texans. Power Grid Could Face Winter Strain A new that the surge in new data centers statewide raises concerns about blackouts for Texas's power grid this winter. The North American Electric Reliability Corporation cautions that nearly 100 registered data centers are now in operation, up from just 13 prior to 2021. The facilities of electricity around the clock, potentially shifting peak demand times. ERCOT officials project just a 1% outage risk for January. Many Texans, though, remain skeptical, recalling that the power grid's failure during in February 2021 left 4.5 million people without power for several days. Austin First Responders Get Bulletproof Vests Austin firefighters and paramedics now carry bulletproof vests after nearly 300 this week. The lightweight steel plate carriers protect against pistol and most rifle rounds, marking the first time every Austin firefighter has ballistic protection. Austin Fire Department Captain Russ Howard said the vests on emergency response instead of worrying about personal safety. "We heard that whenever our fire department and EMS were going out with our police department, a lot of them were feeling like they were in danger or approaching dangerous situations ," said Ashley Copeland with the nonprofit group that helped fund the vests. | Flying together with our sponsor It’s a chilling thought: how easily can a stranger find where you live? Northwest ISD spent four times its $3.5 million state allocation on buses last year. The project targets locations that have been without access to municipal water services for decades. () ➤ Van Zandt County: Commissioner Mitchell Curtis was arrested Wednesday on charges of impersonating a police officer and official oppression. Indictments allege Curtis posed as law enforcement to stop someone from firing guns on his property. subject: Mainers’ Streaming Service Subscriptions Will Be Subject to 5.5% Sales Tax Starting in 2026
body:

------------------------------------------------------------
By Libby Palanza on Dec 04, 2025 05:43 pm
Beginning on January 1, Mainers who subscribe to streaming services like Netflix, Hulu, and Spotify may see a new charge on their monthly bill. Approved as part of Maine’s supplemental budget for the current fiscal year, changes to the state’s tax code will impose a 5.5 percent fee on all streaming service subscriptions starting in [...]

| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

clean_body: Something extraordinary happened just 24 hours after Trump returned to office... | | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | Something extraordinary happened just 24 hours after Trump returned to office... name: Craig Riedel for State Senate
subject: Riedel Campaign Endorsement Update
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Craig Riedel
body:
| | | | |
---
Riedel Campaign Endorsement Update | December 5, 2025
---
Riedel Endorsed by Ohio Value Voters
---
|
---
|
Today, Ohio Value Voters issued their endorsement of Craig Riedel's candidacy in Ohio's First Senate District. Ohio Value Voters is an organization that works to help educate and inform voters across the state of Ohio. They endorse candidates that they believe will best fight for the Conservative Christian values and beliefs that Ohioans hold dear. Ohio Value Voters' other endorsements include gubernatorial candidate Vivek Ramaswamy, Senator Jon Husted, candidate for State Treasurer Kristina Roegner, and Congressman Jim Jordan.
John Stover, President of Ohio Value Voters, issued the following statement: "Ohioans in the 1st Ohio Senate District have the opportunity to elect Craig Riedel to the Ohio Senate. Craig will continue the fight for our family values, the unborn, school choice, against radical indoctrination in the classroom, and for our First Amendment rights. The endorsement of Craig Riedel was an easy decision for Ohio Value Voters. Craig will bring a wealth of conservative experience to the Ohio Senate."
Craig is honored and humbled to be endorsed by Ohio Value Voters and will proudly fight to defend Christian Conservative values as your next State Senator.

---
|
---

As the title says, today you can ask me anything in the comments, and I WILL reply.
I'll keep the questions open all day Friday, and then I'll answer on Saturday (since I'm at work all day Friday!).

name: Weston Imer
subject: Time is running out to meet our 2025 readiness goal.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Team Imer PAC and is not authorized by any candidate or candidates committee
body:
The deadline is looming, and we can’t come up short....
| | | | | [](https://udmlh9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001dWFSAZWFuT4RKV9MKRcUYnyoAi6c_nRaYlTSXotipb3IjQiqGGw5t14eP9mWk_tF0vfJmIj30-Yub9HCCkF6HicWSknFQjQF2DbG2HaVeoyCrFPEtnxUZ5kpPGZ8d3Yq61rOA625uPQEyOezrxX3wAP1Y8geNh_K2gWVjXg6nf0pc67Y7X5cOKpFfq4SqEhQBId31G2FebQ=&c=zAITVE0wXQPtFZFp3tGEamZE3o056FSBTuDKIBf640lWJgcfzL4DVg==&ch=48rqw13MCxwbNo0dRBOTappoJ-dLIsPYsHCw_wlnY6dbmYfKoRRXwA==)

---
| Friend, Christmas is almost here -- and so is one of the most important deadlines of the entire year for Team Imer PAC.
While most Americans are wrapping gifts and preparing for time with family, the political establishment is pouring last-minute dollars into their candidates, trying to drown out grassroots America First voices before we even enter 2025.
We cannot -- and will not -- be outmatched heading into the new year.
That's why this final stretch is so critical. Our end-of-year fundraising deadline is approaching fast, and we're still short of the resources we need to recruit, train, and elect the next generation of America First leaders.
Your support right now will:
* Strengthen our 2025 candidate recruitment efforts
* Boost voter registration operations in key battlegrounds
* Support organizing and outreach to young conservative activists
* Counter establishment Super PAC money trying to silence grassroots voices
* Prepare us for the critical early months of 2025

This isn't just another fundraising appeal. This is the final chance this year to set our movement up for success.
🎄 Will you make a special year-end Christmas contribution of $15, $35, $50, or whatever you can give to help close this gap?

name: Don Jr.
subject: A special thank you for you,Patriot
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRSC
body:
My father and I couldn’t do this without patriots like you. Open this note of gratitude. |
Fellow American, it's Don Jr. I wanted to take a moment to **express my heartfelt gratitude** for **your unwavering support**. My father and I know that this fight **isn't possible without patriots like you** who are willing to stand up for what's right.

We're honored to have you on our team, **Fellow American.**

**[As a small token of our appreciation, I'd like to share a special message with you. name: Congressman Al Green, TX-09
subject: It's up to us
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP61/1/111773/images/AL%20Green/SCOTUS%20FAILED%20US.png]
Friends,
The Supreme Court’s decision to allow Texas's racially gerrymandered maps to stand is a blow to democracy.
But we need to be clear-eyed about the motivation behind this map.
Greg Abbott and Donald Trump didn't carve up our district by accident. This wasn't just a generic political maneuver.
It was a targeted strike to remove me from Congress.
They targeted me because I have the seniority to block their agenda in committee.
They targeted me because I am the one brave enough to file articles of impeachment against a lawless President.
They targeted me because I have a 20-year record of standing up to them, and they know they can't beat me at the ballot box—so they tried to beat me with a map.
The GOP isn't trying to change the district lines; they are trying to change the outcome . They want to hand-pick a representative who will be less effective and less courageous.
I will not let them pick your representative for you.
They want me gone because I am effective. The best way to fight back is to keep me right here, fighting for you.
Help me defeat their targeted attack.
Chip in $25 to keep a proven fighter on the field. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/251101em]
Unafraid,
Congressman Al Green
FIGHT BACK [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/251101em]
Keep Al Green in Congress Campaign
Checks can be Mailed to:
Keep Al Green in Congress Campaign
PO Box 56761
Houston, TX 77256
United States

### Help us combat the gerrymander!
With the Supreme Court ruling that Texas’ lawmakers can use Trump and Abbott’s [racially gerrymandered congressional maps](https://g3j1.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=6932e8f99a826835a61f3a36&u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.texastribune.org%2F2025%2F12%2F04%2Ftexas-redistricting-map-us-supreme-court-2026-midterms%2F&w=618da2bf3c685a0c65a42914&c=b_69321a5200dce03e03e5355e&l=en-US&s=vHWgI3VQXinsXwIl8Edl58cIENw%3D), we need help getting on the ballot. We need to [raise an additional $1,915 in donations before Saturday to pay the filing fee](https://g3j1.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=6932e8f99a826835a61f3a36&u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.texastribune.org%2F2025%2F12%2F04%2Ftexas-redistricting-map-us-supreme-court-2026-midterms%2F&w=618da2bf3c685a0c65a42914&c=b_69321a5200dce03e03e5355e&l=en-US&s=vHWgI3VQXinsXwIl8Edl58cIENw%3D) to run for Texas’ 12th Congressional District. We are so close! Help us get there so we can continue our fight for universal healthcare and childcare > [**> Donate what you can now!**](https://g3j1.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=6932e8f99a826835a61f3a36&u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.helifortexas.com%2Fdonate%3Fss_source%3Dsscampaigns%26ss_campaign_id%3D69321a5200dce03e03e5355e%26ss_email_id%3D6932e8f99a826835a61f3a36%26ss_campaign_name%3DThe%2Brigged%2Bmaps%2Bwon%25E2%2580%2599t%2Bstop%2Bus%2Bin%2BTexas%26ss_campaign_sent_date%3D2025-12-05T14%253A23%253A30Z&w=618da2bf3c685a0c65a42914&c=b_69321a5200dce03e03e5355e&l=en-US&s=q8PC6K_qQpb2xAtJWZgNT6jd-0w%3D)

body:

| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Aup7pHlJN3ZghckVmGSftgMclBCFHv7zCfhsgwzlaEtpNzHDnTFje99vag3IpCFwNdip1hBYpiYGGmyPYHa93HAi5z92rJBFkVvtkxSz0pOUpQJK1cJdraB2126d7fOEHMKXnoleAW3SwJxaMWlpixrqG4-Esn6NJwHGLv7JdKDCGtGDM4RM0xY25JX6haBZIp32vPL0e6eFmmg_VBK4_UIW5eoxUs_8&c=L-U-lPFzpKzg1ZjlOI5qzj21uov8djQgE2MowDF2oC1GamBVoEKRyg==&ch=W3gMcNByFBkqiuyPVgQ10HdzTFM3ShKt7-tY9Jh4nWlW3bCnj0e4Hg==)

|

Peter,
We have long called for all of the Epstein Files to be released to the public. That should include any and all photo evidence, written communications, and especially the client list that Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell kept to track what big name celebrities (and politicians) "partied" on the island.
Strangely, since Donald Trump begrudgingly signed the Epstein discharge petition last month, Lauren Boebert doesn't seem terribly interested in transparency, according to her own social media.
Boebert got oddly defensive after House Oversight Democrats released new photos showing private areas of Epstein's island mansion, melting down about the release and falsely claiming they were old photos (they aren't):

---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Aup7pHlJN3ZghckVmGSftgMclBCFHv7zCfhsgwzlaEtpNzHDnTFje99vag3IpCFwNdip1hBYpiYGGmyPYHa93HAi5z92rJBFkVvtkxSz0pOUpQJK1cJdraB2126d7fOEHMKXnoleAW3SwJxaMWlpixrqG4-Esn6NJwHGLv7JdKDCGtGDM4RM0xY25JX6haBZIp32vPL0e6fYOch9VVF4xp6-4Lzy__MF&c=L-U-lPFzpKzg1ZjlOI5qzj21uov8djQgE2MowDF2oC1GamBVoEKRyg==&ch=W3gMcNByFBkqiuyPVgQ10HdzTFM3ShKt7-tY9Jh4nWlW3bCnj0e4Hg==)
---

Lauren Boebert can't be trusted to do the right thing. She is a very sick person.
Carpetbagging Boebert has switched back to her default MAGA Mode to protect Trump--because without Trump's support, she stands NO chance against her hometown, Democratic opponent, Trisha Calvarese, who has consistently outraised Boebert each fundraising quarter.
A category 5 storm is brewing for Republicans like Lauren Boebert who keep following Trump off a cliff like loyal mindless lemmings.
Boebert only survived re-election by 546 votes. In 2024, her Democratic opponent Trisha Calvarese had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district, overperforming Kamala Harris by 6 pts while Boebert embarrassingly underperformed Trump in every single county.

name: Natalie Jones
subject: 💥Busting those Pesky PDAB Myths💥
body:
Friends- We know you’ve heard a lot from us about a Prescription Drug Affordability Board
| | |
---
| |
---
💥Busting those Pesky PDAB Myths💥
---
| Friends-
We know you've heard a lot from us about a Prescription Drug Affordability Board and what it could do for Virginians. But you may have also heard from the opposition. Today, we're busting 3 myths about PDABs that you may have heard.
Myth #1: A PDAB Will Make Pharmaceutical Companies Spend Less on Research and Development
Pharmaceutical companies spend much more on advertising than research and development, and prescription drug costs are rising [above the cost of inflation](https://dpxqxvhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0019bUh-7sdFWPsJYZFwbnxn2HfX40EdR8OV_AZ3DpxTbGs5ggHSUMG-vr42j-A_D_oCqeGGa9ifdjKR9QGxj3b7WF0sEezQi452TjLz2_jHsmYzRMI_hO0p4tkEUVPykEinNK5ikrQqLynli_2-n7f-p0CIEfV95XFbAnICaSETPt6vhDgHs81gPOtpcyl2CCfHfvYXX0JwwXCzkhFHV2gG6MCnAnneTSRFjrjpQKefI_nLLABVQXykqJjXkdIi8W45LiJx-juXmOhihdTqyBIeE2eVVBBS4cbB3ZCXIxAZ2Sid_2o2cEjSfBgSv_KzKJxDdvMqj7feQ1Ym2Pn9ee0P9EC5dRDqiIy4HaL6dDkOyLeLbCidXg1WBfh8ZxfiQgsg6wPgswsXuA=&c=SC1K3r-DsDO2LvqJOqWTzf-iOHewCVjlWsEYXFt71Tc_vc_Pk5fjKQ==&ch=AHY2W6skf0j9K9Hcru5bRZ02D1iLqnwVQR3w0h8yIR8Cblqb0wCYIQ==). For example, [a study that looked at 10 different cancer drugs](https://dpxqxvhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0019bUh-7sdFWPsJYZFwbnxn2HfX40EdR8OV_AZ3DpxTbGs5ggHSUMG-vvuBNAEMRHMz9HIZMHDI8Y3OFVhgYYFImb_M80haGNycMMTUhoJeO_h-1fyIE8vrpwqZ_UI10y72jRvC2sYdLRY6kcBhmsU2_QDtpEHWK_DPWphkunqV_MWxlhXMMX8fg==&c=SC1K3r-DsDO2LvqJOqWTzf-iOHewCVjlWsEYXFt71Tc_vc_Pk5fjKQ==&ch=AHY2W6skf0j9K9Hcru5bRZ02D1iLqnwVQR3w0h8yIR8Cblqb0wCYIQ==) showed that the research and development to develop those drugs was $7.2 billion, and those 10 drugs brought in $67 billion in revenue! With profit margins like those, it's safe to say that pharmaceutical companies and their budgets will be just fine if a PDAB sets an upper payment limit on some of the life-saving drugs that are most burdensome to Virginians' wallets.
Myth #2: A PDAB Will Make Pharmaceutical Companies Move Their Business Elsewhere
Despite what they say, pharmaceutical companies are not going to leave states with PDABs. In fact, AstraZeneca is planning a $2 billion project to expand its manufacturing and research facilities in Maryland, a state that already has a PDAB. The pharmaceutical industry is one of the most profitable industries in the world, and investors will continue to find manufacturing and researching prescription drugs more worthwhile and profitable even if their products become more affordable for Virginians.
---
| | [Read More Here](https://dpxqxvhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0019bUh-7sdFWPsJYZFwbnxn2HfX40EdR8OV_AZ3DpxTbGs5ggHSUMG-vvuBNAEMRHM5BNjyJvoOCWCbPhv1nrhJcDbe1_NDUit2arhDa_1_UEUZjFvkMClKZ2N7HbpJLnZE-nHD3QCvmS5mzbER--qTshBS0lxe-otYeoTZVS6hwykBd-r2vW9FBKb8F4-_w5en20YI8fRbw2ESimoMwwJ8moHnkxXCgqa9FpAI1mM51e9QJie0f_ye_WhEnSnmcEIaomZ_c69cx-6Zp_LNzlDGOQZQxoVDknkQkGHMWeS4Ihu80YnKrIKtcpXOVccrfWZM5C72ncwpdPMFL0QCG2rAEVwMvQKx-5CtxsJ_dTK3p23HN4rWVqMHYAjWZQEba2P&c=SC1K3r-DsDO2LvqJOqWTzf-iOHewCVjlWsEYXFt71Tc_vc_Pk5fjKQ==&ch=AHY2W6skf0j9K9Hcru5bRZ02D1iLqnwVQR3w0h8yIR8Cblqb0wCYIQ==)
---
| Myth #3: A PDAB Will Cut Down on Research & Development for Rare Disease Treatments
Virginians with rare diseases already can't afford their prescription drugs. This is an empty threat from pharmaceutical companies designed to scare patients. Pharmaceutical companies [spend thousands of dollars on lobbying](https://dpxqxvhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0019bUh-7sdFWPsJYZFwbnxn2HfX40EdR8OV_AZ3DpxTbGs5ggHSUMG-vvuBNAEMRHMRXuu9p-DWaPiElY-IksPEaxyI5otQXO-mCh70KkCqBU98vakjEyurGdodBq7SkPyxc2f_M4vUJptQ6oOAZz6YAWC-PPGSXh1&c=SC1K3r-DsDO2LvqJOqWTzf-iOHewCVjlWsEYXFt71Tc_vc_Pk5fjKQ==&ch=AHY2W6skf0j9K9Hcru5bRZ02D1iLqnwVQR3w0h8yIR8Cblqb0wCYIQ==), advertising, executive compensation, and stock buybacks, much more than they spend on bringing drugs to the market.
While [1 in 4 Virginians](https://dpxqxvhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0019bUh-7sdFWPsJYZFwbnxn2HfX40EdR8OV_AZ3DpxTbGs5ggHSUMG-vvuBNAEMRHM81osEJP91HYKQOFmf9mDHwd0idEriMArEdo60l2jh9gedU79vtPllyejrsjTXOT4ESKHaNy_8UVIVkfMcjryG7_PkhKWdETWr4bNMw4lRbJd-8nIclCbA1hp4LLDVar1nanDU_R0pdYA-Ebrn4ZlV8SKOCX7qbq1szKi4U75kqg=&c=SC1K3r-DsDO2LvqJOqWTzf-iOHewCVjlWsEYXFt71Tc_vc_Pk5fjKQ==&ch=AHY2W6skf0j9K9Hcru5bRZ02D1iLqnwVQR3w0h8yIR8Cblqb0wCYIQ==) chooses not to take their prescription drugs because they cannot afford them, pharmaceutical companies are making record profits. It's time to fight back and establish a Prescription Drug Affordability Board in Virginia.
Natalie Jones Communications Director
---

---

Hi Peter,

**We have bad news and good news.**

❌ The bad news is that our powerhouse candidate, Aftyn Behn, lost her special election on Tuesday.

This is especially painful because TN ranks among the bottom in the nation for maternal health outcomes, and women in TN are living under a near-total abortion ban with no exceptions for rape or incest.

This district will soon be represented by Matt Van Epps, a Trump-endorsed Republican who said he would “100% have the President’s back” if elected to Congress. Just what we need. 🙄

✅ The good news is that this Republican victory in a historically **deep red** district was won by a **notably small margin.** 😏

Voters in the deepest red pockets of America are making their voices heard. They are excited about a pro-choice champion, a pro-WOMAN warrior, and a fearless critic of the old boys’ club in Southern states.

**Here’s the deal: At Her Bold Move, we are proud to take a chance on incredible candidates, even when they are running in red states against well-funded, Trump-endorsed, anti-choice, and often independently wealthy men. We stand by the classic underdogs – and it’s paying off!

In 2025, our win rate was 98%. The year before that? 44% of our WINS came out of RED STATES.**

We design our programs to support women candidates running in the toughest races in the country. And even though we’re often the only national organization investing in those races, we couldn’t be prouder. We believe that ALL women, regardless of WHERE they live, deserve real and true representation.

[**While it didn’t go the way we’d hoped, Her Bold Move helped move the needle in this historically red Tennessee district that has never before been represented by a woman – and we are going to continue to fight to elect women in red and blue states alike in 2026. body:
If Boebert truly supported the Epstein Files release, why would she get so upset when new information is made public?
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0013LkGT7gTn7DnkVGmAVHXju4njoC5NgwhGy52dBDYFvXR3_0zUVss9Ks6Ua2TKIKM5W-HNgVyGdHT7bZWSpKUl17Nin3uMOjuPr_68hDWIfQvONTqkR5VBzpE8LRmByXG_CPTdnEUO_SzXokRcdPI2JgbO0L97GQrlFduNka2C3gUzxSuLuCYcu8UoSRqNVyjqJcV1SDo_KedimbqE33XGsK7MGy6fpYZ&c=ys-s0114Z90yZwpMf_INWUgU3hIsZdMI6quwPceA7f3DLlNLyhnlOw==&ch=Wgq5LlGPwcuAhRFSBIZS_lMIwOsh5TD7g0QzVSB5CvSsmC1oFpfWvg==)

|

Peter,
We have long called for all of the Epstein Files to be released to the public. That should include any and all photo evidence, written communications, and especially the client list that Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell kept to track what big name celebrities (and politicians) "partied" on the island.
Strangely, since Donald Trump begrudgingly signed the Epstein discharge petition last month, Lauren Boebert doesn't seem terribly interested in transparency, according to her own social media.
Boebert got oddly defensive after House Oversight Democrats released new photos showing private areas of Epstein's island mansion, melting down about the release and falsely claiming they were old photos (they aren't):

---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0013LkGT7gTn7DnkVGmAVHXju4njoC5NgwhGy52dBDYFvXR3_0zUVss9Ks6Ua2TKIKM5W-HNgVyGdHT7bZWSpKUl17Nin3uMOjuPr_68hDWIfQvONTqkR5VBzpE8LRmByXG_CPTdnEUO_SzXokRcdPI2JgbO0L97GQrlFduNka2C3gUzxSuLuCYcu8UoSRqNVyjqJcV1SDo_Kerd-Nmr2UBz8TZlnmhioP7&c=ys-s0114Z90yZwpMf_INWUgU3hIsZdMI6quwPceA7f3DLlNLyhnlOw==&ch=Wgq5LlGPwcuAhRFSBIZS_lMIwOsh5TD7g0QzVSB5CvSsmC1oFpfWvg==)

Lauren Boebert can't be trusted to do the right thing. She is a very sick person.
Carpetbagging Boebert has switched back to her default MAGA Mode to protect Trump--because without Trump's support, she stands NO chance against her hometown, Democratic opponent, Trisha Calvarese, who has consistently outraised Boebert each fundraising quarter.
A category 5 storm is brewing for Republicans like Lauren Boebert who keep following Trump off a cliff like loyal mindless lemmings.
Boebert only survived re-election by 546 votes. In 2024, her Democratic opponent Trisha Calvarese had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district, overperforming Kamala Harris by 6 pts while Boebert embarrassingly underperformed Trump in every single county.
[The only way we will ever get accountability and transparency over the Trump Administration is by defeating Lauren Boebert and flipping the House. Will you chip in a contribution to Trisha today to help her finally get rid of Lauren Boebert?](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0013LkGT7gTn7DnkVGmAVHXju4njoC5NgwhGy52dBDYFvXR3_0zUVss9Ks6Ua2TKIKM5W-HNgVyGdHT7bZWSpKUl17Nin3uMOjuPr_68hDWIfQvONTqkR5VBzpE8LRmByXG_CPTdnEUO_SzXokRcdPI2JgbO0L97GQrlFduNka2C3gUzxSuLuCYcu8UoSRqNVyjqJcV1SDo_KfrTxMo4GHEP8wWWg2BGT9q&c=ys-s0114Z90yZwpMf_INWUgU3hIsZdMI6quwPceA7f3DLlNLyhnlOw==&ch=Wgq5LlGPwcuAhRFSBIZS_lMIwOsh5TD7g0QzVSB5CvSsmC1oFpfWvg==)

clean_body: If Boebert truly supported the Epstein Files release, why would she get so upset when new information is made public? | | | | | == | Peter, We have long called for all of the Epstein Files to be released to the public. That should include any and all photo evidence, written communications, and especially the client list that Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell kept to track what big name celebrities (and politicians) "partied" on the island. Strangely, since Donald Trump begrudgingly signed the Epstein discharge petition last month, Lauren Boebert doesn't seem terribly interested in transparency, according to her own social media. Boebert got oddly defensive after House Oversight Democrats released new photos showing private areas of Epstein's island mansion, melting down about the release and falsely claiming they were old photos (they aren't): | == Lauren Boebert can't be trusted to do the right thing. She is a very sick person. Carpetbagging Boebert has switched back to her default MAGA Mode to protect Trump--because without Trump's support, she stands NO chance against her hometown, Democratic opponent, Trisha Calvarese, who has consistently outraised Boebert each fundraising quarter. A category 5 storm is brewing for Republicans like Lauren Boebert who keep following Trump off a cliff like loyal mindless lemmings. Boebert only survived re-election by 546 votes. In 2024, her Democratic opponent Trisha Calvarese had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district, overperforming Kamala Harris by 6 pts while Boebert embarrassingly underperformed Trump in every single county. == If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | == | == | == | | | == | == | == | == | Thank you, Team Trisha Calvarese | | | | == | Mail checks to: Trisha 4 CO 9609 S University Blvd., PO Box 631322 Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 | Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado Trisha Calvarese for Congress | 9609 S University Blvd. PO Box 631322 | Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 US | |

name: Patrick J. Murphy
subject: From the Front with Patrick J. Murphy: Leadership, Innovation, and a Week of Service
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP25/1/106220/images/PJM_Logo.png]
From the Front with Patrick J. Murphy: Leadership, Innovation, and a Week of Service
Hi Friends,
It’s been an impactful week of work focused on national security, economic stability, and empowering Americans to thrive in a rapidly changing world. We are grateful to continue building momentum heading into the Army-Navy Conference next week.
https://www.instagram.com/reel/DRU-Mn3iVbE/?igsh=MW42NDF5ODV0dzQ3eA== [https://www.instagram.com/reel/DRU-Mn3iVbE/?igsh=MW42NDF5ODV0dzQ3eA==]
Looking Ahead to Baltimore
In one week, we’ll be hosting our 4th annual Army-Navy Conference at Johns Hopkins Carey Business School. This event unites veterans & business leaders who bring their lessons of service into boardrooms, statehouses, startups, and the agencies that keep our nation strong. It will be a gathering of veteran excellence and a great opportunity to connect and expand your network.
This year’s speakers represent the full spectrum of operational excellence and national service:
💠Governor Wes Moore
💠Under Armor CEO Kevin Plank
💠Representative Chris Deluzio (PA-17)
💠Medal of Honor recipient Florent “Flo” Groberg
💠Blake Hall, CEO of ID.me [http://id.me/]
💠Craig Cummings and Kelly Perdew, Moonshots Capital
💠Frank Finelli, Partner, The Carlyle Group
💠David Kurtz, CEO of Professional Services, Hilco Global
📅 December 12, 2025
🕒 10:00 AM – 3:00 PM EST
📍 Johns Hopkins Carey School of Business — Baltimore, MD
Register Here [https://www.eventbrite.com/e/2025-army-navy-policy-conference-tickets-1636746245079?aff=oddtdtcreator]
We can’t wait to see you there!
In the Media: How Economic Headwinds are Hurting Working Americans
On Wednesday, I joined Fox News’ America Reports to discuss rising grocery and utility costs, ongoing layoffs, and how economic uncertainty is affecting families across the country. We emphasized that Americans deserve leaders who can come together, strengthen our economy, and provide relief at home.
Today I’m back in NY to speak at the Fordham Business School and at 3pm I return to Fox News for a panel discussion on the global challenges shaping America’s security landscape. We’ll talk about what this moment demands responsible leaders and our need to navigate economic and geopolitical pressures while protecting our national interests and supporting our allies.
Across both conversations, our message remains focused on unity, strength, accountability and keeping America competitive and secure.
On Warrior Money: Thriving in the Age of AI
On this week’s episode of Warrior Money, we hosted a CEO known as the ‘Debt Ninja’ Our guest, Army MP sergeant and GuideOn founder Ashley Gorbulja, earned four degrees debt-free by stacking scholarships, using National Guard benefits wisely, and treating education as a strategic move rather than a box to check. She broke down how to match your values to your career goals, plan benefits across different states and programs, and avoid leaving scholarship money unclaimed.
Ashley also explained how AI, the trades, and veteran entrepreneurship are changing what smart education and career decisions look like after service.
See our full conversation on Warrior Money [https://youtu.be/NBm_O0z5BSg?si=wzO-9q5Q4P_zcbo1]
On Substack: How to Thrive in the AI Job Market
70% of Americans are worried AI will take their job. Our Substack looks at how AI is reshaping the American workforce and what it takes to stay ahead. AI will automate tasks, but it can’t replace true leadership. The jobs that endure will be the ones built on judgment, trust, communication, and the ability to lead teams through uncertainty.
The AI era will reward skilled operators—people who can think clearly, act under pressure, and move others when it matters most.
The same warrior ethos that drives excellence on the battlefield applies in business, tech, and entrepreneurship, making veterans, athletes, & first responders the perfect operators to thrive in the AI economy starved of real leadership.
Read the Substack column here:
How to Thrive in the AI Job Market [https://patrickmurphypa.substack.com/p/how-to-thrive-in-the-ai-job-market]
On the Road: The OPUS Connect Panel in Miami
On Wednesday, I joined business leaders in Miami for OPUS Connect’s Miami Private Debt Summit. I took part in a fireside chat titled “Borders, Battles, and Balance Sheets: Geopolitics in Modern Restructuring.” Our conversation focused on how political tensions have become a major market driver.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP25/1/106220/images/12.5%20.png]
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP25/1/106220/images/12.5%202.png]
Looking Back: A Thanksgiving of Service and Gratitude
Last week, we gathered with family and friends and celebrated the great American tradition of giving thanks. Our team continues to be inspired by the veterans, families, and community partners who serve others each and every day.
Thank you for being part of this mission.
Hon. Patrick J. Murphy
32nd Army Under Secretary
P.S. Please consider following us on social media @patrickmurphypa & subscribing to our weekly Substack column. [https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa?utm_source=substack-feed-item]

If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/JcTKjLm2l0SduvIvv8SeUg2?unsubscribedata=8J%2BomXiPlFIBN3jvNhs%2B3xjcOdAFJqgYl8XTK7xloyT2pDuxfviJRmKBDQLWJcEImzjTws6dGsRqbjwa3sEtYU6Q1pijlGWNp3cmd3RcmlQqarUtl9uCZo16VU7cz0tyCRwwKqiUxLF70cRQErDKRT3STgKUHp7ejmLGFz9nevWvIwsiQd8fwwqZcqiz8tfwxpipbZ9kwmoMGUqiqPQ6xWwiC13FxahYoAJD7rdObr5dYpoiOWLtCXfivvnsS3%2BtMsobBfDxeNo2EViMzHnozUKdUbYNo6GJK1Ljw0eJoygKtGTH8O1wAC4gDFYtRJwCKpKzR7dvh%2BgSA%2FFnn1kOEOMoUS53gnjI%2FIoYgm7mzGXPpXqT3LdNuhavUs1Md3MskH%2Bu7P2gZwh4y0M%2BIF7KSKLsbMmyU9xPoDjaR6xzhRLO05rzq27ZFjz%2BMYfyOHEZ .

**Peter, here’s what you missed:**

clean_body: I'm too busy to search far & wide for you AGAIN! | | | | I have ONE spot left on the official 2025 MAGA Honor Roll ... and I’m holding it for YOU. Your name has come across my desk over and over this year — more than once, more than twice — which is exactly why I told my team: “Save the final spot for Peter do NOT give it to anyone else.” I’m personally compiling the permanent 2025 MAGA Honor Roll , the exclusive list of my strongest, most loyal patriots from this historic year. This list is going straight to my desk in the Oval Office. I will be reviewing every single name myself tonight. Please don’t make me stay up all night wondering why you weren’t there when I looked for you. Thank you, President Donald J. Trump P.S. When I don’t see your name tonight, I’ll know you let the last spot go to someone else. Don’t do that to me. Confirm now and make my night. | | | | | | | | | | | | Contributions to Trump National Committee JFC Inc. are not deductible for federal income tax purposes. All contributions are subject to the limits and prohibitions of the Federal Election Campaign Act. | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---
