The Department of Energy runs it.
And now, even Amazon wants to integrate it into their AI cloud — [alongside Elon’s favorite AI network.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=4dd61ae3541b45eeb62fcc209209fd79&_e=AihJtJPgUw74RbKXMhEIqXwxSzUQQhXdxcyYHAhabg_H6FsCrPE8D90vidSgn-VbmsDlqetPJEUOZDYjD54JdEx7pQ479vfK7vS2AKnM0r8M_oQCT93HmVq46PMFNcemhmxNrTTa-CNixlPBB84XEMDDC6cphjfk8URMgwcuoyuihwMr2q1xJy8T9w0ty9mFtv2Jckg3FzX5DZb3H2k0UVF5kc77ahE683RItQkWhDjfLzaQ-ZwWpk_FSi8oBiL08jwyAjw1YT9K8KRc1mo1Cx8MQAyz4hyAXE9z1_R0LxakhmzBnI4HJJlsbnMIrGGGXtQ4u7mu-T6pAdJVXr9fC1qq5fJoaL7Y5FKcmE1bHkJW15S36gGaahK17bTKl3GkkT3J9b7M7OjbzP-VbnCTHdcWk4bi5XQ2W_O49pLHvz1Go0psZw0DbS_S5A23929S8PPhtSyuyFmwBoXShBzPfumas0KrwEAqLPJ6Va7ZUQLrL8tjk3-0PHatPd4uplM61KqYL2nzzM1vZ8wXEeGQwSM_JHUKmO2qzy3bh84qZm_At3gyOk8Ah0nq0r7RvOYcOiJMgYrd1cGt-U-g0e9ZZQ%3D%3D)

This same company is still **15 times smaller than Nvidia.**
Nvidia already soared **7,730%**.
This one could be next.

[See why I call it “Elon’s #1 AI stock” — and why it could be the biggest winner of 2025.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=4dd61ae3541b45eeb62fcc209209fd79&_e=AihJtJPgUw74RbKXMhEIqXwxSzUQQhXdxcyYHAhabg_H6FsCrPE8D90vidSgn-VbmsDlqetPJEUOZDYjD54JdEx7pQ479vfK7vS2AKnM0r8M_oQCT93HmVq46PMFNcemhmxNrTTa-CNixlPBB84XEMDDC6cphjfk8URMgwcuoyuihwMr2q1xJy8T9w0ty9mFDMV80Q39Ur8czEItP4GkkkgPBT17wf6Hz1FSeaG3eFVFULcDYZR1RKwT_7gFK6wHOeBqQB9AzWcZHE1DitrpFr2-NL2J6EkTS4bNBAvRBQHRlobyFoedo4n_Ked0jTF7Y8Suj6VHWhVt1ZqjbOjNTE1prE7jBhnrT7ZCrLcQ92zwij8JXNRQyt1YYny1CNKSWdNCkmbFo2kjlpZlGNMuK5iiLXW7-8Mex9hxnr_-utqQiD_AOmYlsGNZ5v2yEWb8U6n1RJzc8mNfYherCc4nwQsBQRNuNJnMwhsMg72FVXUxuEycNlWBBU2-8tvH8k1VaQg-nf5NGIuHuDoveIG1OoqHTH_sJsRKAJRzygRXUZ1cic1ukXx4KQp3iMUobG_aJ8q9IGmQBDPZ5H--s9Y1KQ%3D%3D)
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

clean_body: Powered by AI chips from a company you’ve probably never heard of.| | | | December 05 | | | | | Caught on Camera: Strange discovery on the Mars | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120671831/587000745/186742633?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_ButtigiegRelaunch_251205_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNTg0Y2U5NS1lZGQxLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=LJuJjhMczlBmouT0EEnkg9eEUyefg4CKgyX97EgR4EM=) Derek,

In a moment, I’m going to ask you to chip in to support Senator Mark Warner’s official reelection launch. But first, I want to explain why this moment calls for all of us to lean in.

Too often, the news feels designed to leave people discouraged – especially when Donald Trump and Congressional Republicans seem unconstrained in serving themselves and the well-connected instead of the people they were elected to represent.

But this is exactly why we must insist on using the power that democracy puts in our hands as citizens. We’re not bystanders, and we are far from powerless.

Indeed, we have had some fresh reminders of that recently. Across the country in recent weeks, we’ve seen voters choose results over chaos, and leadership over cynicism. Candidates have prevailed with a focus on solving real problems and bringing down costs for families.

**Hope isn’t something we wait around for – it’s something we create together. And one of the ways we do that is by activating behind leaders who approach public service with seriousness and a sense of responsibility like Senator Mark Warner.**

[_**If you’re able, will you please chip in to help Mark’s campaign kick off on the strongest footing clean_body: | | | = Derek, In a moment, I’m going to ask you to chip in to support Senator Mark Warner’s official reelection launch. But first, I want to explain why this moment calls for all of us to lean in. Too often, the news feels designed to leave people discouraged – especially when Donald Trump and Congressional Republicans seem unconstrained in serving themselves and the well-connected instead of the people they were elected to represent. But this is exactly why we must insist on using the power that democracy puts in our hands as citizens. We’re not bystanders, and we are far from powerless. Indeed, we have had some fresh reminders of that recently. Across the country in recent weeks, we’ve seen voters choose results over chaos, and leadership over cynicism. Candidates have prevailed with a focus on solving real problems and bringing down costs for families. Hope isn’t something we wait around for – it’s something we create together. And one of the ways we do that is by activating behind leaders who approach public service with seriousness and a sense of responsibility like Senator Mark Warner. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately: = = = = = = What happens next in our country will depend on whether we stand up for democratic values, protect health care, deliver policies that bring down costs for families instead of giving massive tax breaks to the wealthy, and replace the chaos of today’s Washington with something better. Thank you for being part of this effort, and for believing in what we can build together. Pete = * | | Friends of Mark Warner 1490-5A Quarterpath Road #213 Williamsburg, VA 23185 United States | Click here to . | Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner

subject: If you want to make a tangible difference in this race
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
body:
#

In 2021, Jon Ossoff and Rev. Raphael Warnock won a historic runoff election and secured the Senate majority for the Democrats.
That victory didn’t _just happen._
_Jon won because of the hard work and support of grassroots supporters like you — folks who showed up to take a phonebanking shift, post on social media, knock doors, and yes, chip in to emails just like this one to power Jon’s campaign._
As we get closer to 2026, there will be opportunities to show up in all types of ways. [**But right now, if you want to make a tangible difference in this race, the best thing you can do is make a donation to help Jon build the strongest campaign possible — which is why we’re asking:**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.lXrKfY&refcode=em251205-1-subs-a&t=1&akid=1213%2E5691347%2E3M1Ohy)

body:
| | | Friend — In January, I’ll be sworn in as a member of the Virginia House of Delegates. _And I’m proud to say, we are not easing into this._ **Democrats are reintroducing the b****ills that passed in 2025 but were vetoed by Governor Youngkin — bills that would:**
* Lower energy costs by requiring utilities to be more efficient
* Expand weatherization and energy savings for families
* Let localities protect affordable housing
* Raise the minimum wage to $15 by 2028
* Guarantee paid sick leave for Virginia workers

These are common-sense, kitchen-table policies. Now, we have a second chance to get this done, and this time, we intend to finish the job. **But here’s the catch:** if we want to pass these bills and protect them from the next wave of attacks, we need a strong, organized operation behind them — outreach, advocacy, and a team that can keep constituents informed and engaged. **That’s where you come in.** **[If you’re ready to see higher wages, lower costs, and basic dignity for workers become Virginia law, I’m asking you to chip in whatever makes sense for you today — $10, $20, $50, or more if you’re able.](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvhsqD6hnNNbCP7JMDskKO3DcwO3FPpifEsk6R5UXJAqeoZfkhx0rJYxvzeQyPAOXxPzaHpN7uxnA66Ao0W-7EqZyFw6iLW1nc6uNsMh9U_6P3FRkFikFXLI0eap87016mevaSsugUYPBO5DGR-jEt9MUFK-vTWpICaFS7KuRvP1BL6ByWWQXY9Y9P961aM8GuvNAS7itxziXUah0HPoqarBptcjKibKp2Q9eIfoQnGxouXOHAymSAMP-n2yoyOe0DTB4t_NbMoVfcABQtM1rWpleD5PG4xJFdAVowWu7W0G4OqDLkyOgTbTKPkYC-G_ETBwcenGy8q9nmV0fJI3662aUHwM_IlYKkb8W7fe3giaA/4m6/Ve0CXH2rTe6dgPN1rBarQQ/h0/h001.XVMYMsQyZqJRs_6QzZKShm2_adaCqwpw5-_2fMxMMxI)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately._ [Donate $10

# **Kash Patel accuses Biden admin of negligence in DC pipe bomb case**
FBI Director Kash Patel charged that President Joe Biden's FBI was **[guilty of"sheer incompetence](https://substack.com/redirect/bff9c67a-964f-445e-bad4-46f7df9db05a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** or complete intentional negligence" in allowing the DC pipe bomb case to languish for nearly five years before the Thursday arrest of Brian Cole Jr., 30, of Woodbridge, Virginia, who is accused of planting pipe bombs outside the Democratic and Republican National Committees on January 5, 2021, hours before the Capitol riot.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/011810ba-afe5-46ef-80ef-ce18d0bbb5b9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Trump-era FBI reexamined evidence to crack case:** Patel told Fox News that "the prior administration sat on the evidence for four years" with no new evidence produced, but the current FBI led by Deputy Director Dan Bongino "went out to the country, brought in our experts" and reexamined "three million lines of evidence," including cell phone tower data dumps and geolocational data that wasn't properly analyzed, asking "Why weren't all the phone numbers scrubbed? Why weren't they connected?"
* **Suspect faces 15-30 years if convicted:** Cole, who investigators say started planning the attack in 2019 using bank and cell phone data to link him to production and placement of the bombs, will make an initial court appearance Friday afternoon and faces between 15 and 30 years in prison on the two counts he currently faces, with officials saying more charges are possible.

* * *
# **Congress moves to limit Trump 's military strikes in Caribbean**
Lawmakers from both parties are introducing legislation to restrict funding and require **[congressional approval for Trump administration military operations](https://substack.com/redirect/ea21977a-d2f9-4e6b-92a8-dac76e54c490?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** in the region following intense scrutiny of strikes targeting alleged drug smugglers, including concerns about a controversial Sept. 2 "double-tap" strike that killed survivors from an initial attack, with experts predicting the administration will "shift tactics" to avoid harsh political consequences.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ea21977a-d2f9-4e6b-92a8-dac76e54c490?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Democrats introduce bills to curb Venezuela military action:** Sens. Jeff Merkley and Tim Kaine introduced the Prohibiting Unauthorized Military Action in Venezuela Act of 2025 to bar federal funding for strikes without congressional approval, while Kaine, Chuck Schumer, Adam Schiff, and Rand Paul introduced a war powers resolution to prevent Trump from engaging in hostilities within or against Venezuela, with lawmakers urging release of video footage from the Sept. 2 strike.
* **Controversy over Hegseth 's alleged "kill them all" order:** The Washington Post reported that Secretary of War Pete Hegseth allegedly ordered everyone on board an alleged drug boat to be killed in a Sept. 2 operation, prompting a second strike to eliminate survivors, though the White House disputed this claim and said Adm. Frank "Mitch" Bradley directed the second strike, with Sen. Tom Cotton saying Bradley was "very clear that he was given no such order."
* **Top Senate Democrat "deeply disturbed" after classified briefing:** Sen. Jack Reed, top Democrat on Senate Armed Services Committee, said after Bradley's closed-door Capitol Hill briefing Thursday that "this briefing confirmed my worst fears" and urged Trump to release "complete, unedited footage" of the Sept. 2 strike, saying the Defense Department "has no choice but to release" it as the president agreed, while the Trump administration has conducted at least 21 strikes near Venezuela as part of its anti-drug crusade.

* * *
# **DHS captures dozen criminal illegal immigrants in Minneapolis operation**
The Department of Homeland Security announced **[the arrest of at least 12 criminal illegal immigrants](https://substack.com/redirect/c6777778-c0c2-442c-aa5a-5cd499b9748b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** during Operation Metro Surge in Minneapolis, targeting individuals convicted of serious offenses including child sexual assault, domestic violence, and gang activity.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c6777778-c0c2-442c-aa5a-5cd499b9748b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Violent offender profile:** Immigration and Customs Enforcement detained five Somali nationals, six Mexican citizens, and one El Salvadoran, with arrestees including Abdulkadir Sharif Abdi--a Somali gang member with convictions for fraud and vehicle theft--plus others convicted of sexually assaulting minors aged 13-15, aggravated assault with weapons, and alien smuggling.
* **Local leadership criticized:** DHS Assistant Secretary Tricia McLaughlin blasted sanctuary policies and politicians including former Vice Presidential candidate Tim Walz and Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey for enabling "pedophiles, domestic terrorists, and gang members to roam the streets," declaring ICE agents are risking their lives while elected officials "sit by and do nothing."
* **Separate federal charges filed:** The Justice Department charged Abdimahat Bille Mohamed, a 28-year-old Somali national, with kidnapping and raping a woman he met on Snapchat after he received no prison time under a May plea deal for two prior sexual assault cases, with Attorney General Pam Bondi warning "there are more charges to come" as investigators match his DNA to unsolved rape cases.

* * *
# Stephen Smith goes off on traitorous Dem lawmakers for video encouraging troops to disobey Trump
ESPN commentator Stephen A. Smith stood firm during a **[heated exchange](https://substack.com/redirect/70c2bdf1-73b5-4c88-aaf3-91e5ff92bf90?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** on "The View" Thursday, criticizing Senator Mark Kelly for appearing in a video encouraging military personnel to refuse unlawful orders from President Trump.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/69a8af5f-62b3-46f5-99d0-5fe0d96c8f75?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Refusing to back down:** When co-host Sunny Hostin labeled Smith "loud and wrong" and offered him a chance to reconsider his position on Kelly's participation in the Democratic lawmakers' video, Smith immediately responded "I'm not changing a thing. I didn't stutter when I said it."
* **Kelly 's background complicates message:** Smith argued his objection wasn't about the content but rather Kelly's military credentials as a former Navy combat pilot, warning that veterans can face serious consequences for suggesting troops disobey orders--even illegal ones--citing family members with military experience who explained service members "can be implicated for implying something" that could land them in trouble.
* **Disagreement over military law:** Hostin and Joy Behar repeatedly emphasized Kelly specifically referenced "illegal" or "unlawful" orders aligned with military code of conduct, suggesting Smith misunderstood the distinction, though Smith insisted he comprehended the difference while maintaining his stance, noting "two governors" texted supporting Hostin's view while "two senators" backed his position.

* * *

# [Trump's Corrupt Pardons Are a Middle Finger to Every American](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4258115&post_id=180805952&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwODA1OTUyLCJpYXQiOjE3NjQ5NTEwMzgsImV4cCI6MTc2NzU0MzAzOCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQyNTgxMTUiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.1m4Y66d7UuMaoBivkwM-rnyu7s1KuiLu91TpmXuk1F4)
### Why Cuellar's Pardon Hits Different
| | [Mike Nellis](https://substack.com/@mikenellis)
---
| Dec 5
---
| [](https://substack.com/@mikenellis)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4258115&post_id=180805952&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwODA1OTUyLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2NDk1MTAzOCwiZXhwIjoxNzY3NTQzMDM4LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDI1ODExNSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0._E34pq6Vkwyh1W52ExIOI3plEIyjwE-Tx8ebyzSCMxU&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

---

_**[Endless Urgency is free to read, but we're hustling to launch our new content studio in January and pay our staff a living wage. Paid subscribers make it all possible--just $8/month if you're enjoying the work](https://substack.com/redirect/2e17e07c-16c1-41f3-a678-c92c696dd7aa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg): **_
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2e17e07c-16c1-41f3-a678-c92c696dd7aa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
I don't think there's anything angering me more right now than the corruption of the Trump administration -- and the sheer brazenness of these daily deluge of pardons. Trump has already issued over 1,600 pardons. Joe Biden? Just 80. And yeah, there were a few Biden pardons I didn't love -- but with Trump, it's literally every day. One or two people. It's jaw-dropping.
I want to zero in on one pardon in particular -- though honestly, I could go off on dozens -- and that's the pardon this week of Democratic Congressman Henry Cuellar of Texas.
Full disclosure: I've done some work for Cuellar before. I don't anymore. And this one really, deeply pisses me off -- because I have no idea what Trump got out of it.
I've seen some Dem influencers try to frame it like Trump's playing 4D chess -- pardoning a Democrat to divide us, make us fight each other. I don't buy that for a second. Trump's not some Machiavellian genius -- that's giving him way too much credit. It also completely glosses over the fact that Cuellar was facing trial for taking $600,000 in bribes from an oil and gas company. That's not some minor slip-up. That's serious.
Some folks have said the case against Cuellar was flimsy. Maybe. I don't know. But that's what a trial is for. Let the process play out. Let a jury decide. And I think it's important for Democrats -- _especially_ when it's one of our own -- to say that out loud.
* * *
_**[Endless Urgency is free to read, but we're hustling to launch our new content studio in January and pay our staff a living wage. Paid subscribers make it all possible--just $8/month if you're enjoying the work](https://substack.com/redirect/2e17e07c-16c1-41f3-a678-c92c696dd7aa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg): **_
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2e17e07c-16c1-41f3-a678-c92c696dd7aa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
Because I don't know why Trump did this. Maybe money moved behind the scenes. Maybe there was a political angle. Maybe he just did it to screw with us. But whatever the reason, he did it -- and now you've got people out here saying, "Well, look, Trump pardons Democrats too! That's bipartisan!"
I don't give a damn what party you're in -- if you're accused of a crime, you should go through the process. Same with the Epstein files. I don't care if Democrats are in there -- release it all. Let the public see what the rich and powerful are doing behind closed doors. Because corruption is everywhere. It's unchecked, and it's metastasizing.
Issuing pardons without any meaningful review or explanation is extremely dangerous because it signals that normal legal safeguards no longer apply. This kind of unchecked power reinforces the reality that we are already living under increasingly authoritarian conditions -- and the more leaders get away with it, the deeper that governance style entrenches itself. There's zero accountability for the handful of people who abuse their power and fuck over regular Americans for profit.
Democrats have to be willing to call that out. If we want to be taken seriously on corruption -- which, by the way, _should_ be one of the defining issues of 2026 and 2028 -- we've got to own it. We've got to take on insider trading in Congress. We've got to deal with the rot inside our own house. No more ignoring it because it's inconvenient or uncomfortable.
One of the reasons Trump thrives is because people believe the whole damn system is corrupt. That everyone's just in it for themselves. And you know what? That belief _helps_ him. People say, "At least Trump's honest about it." And it drives me insane. Every time I hear a voter say that in a focus group, it's like someone jamming a fork into a garbage disposal in my brain.
But they're not wrong to feel disillusioned. And a lot of Democratic strategists know that corruption is a message that breaks through. I believe that too. It's a way to talk about who's winning and who's losing in Trump's America. It ties directly to issues people care about: affordability, healthcare, security, fairness. That's where we can connect.
Same goes for Epstein. The Trump administration has become a full-blown protection racket for billionaires and their well connected buddies. And we've got to name that. But we've also got to name it when it's happening in our own damn party. No more looking the other way.
Maybe Cuellar's innocent. I honestly don't know. But he never got his day in court. And that matters.
Here's my final thought: what kind of message does this send to the people at the FBI and DOJ who are actually trying to do their jobs? Trying to take down bribery schemes, political corruption, drug traffickers, sexual predators? How demoralizing must it be when Trump turns around and pardons the very people they spent years building a case against?
Take that case in Texas this week -- Trump's own DOJ brought charges against a guy involved in rigging a stadium deal. Trump pardoned him too. Why? Most likely because the guy got close to Trump's inner circle and he made a big enough donation. Maybe he got the right cocktail server to whisper his name at Mar-a-Lago. Who knows.
But that's what this has become. And I've reached the point where I think the pardon power, as it exists, needs to go. It was meant to be a failsafe -- a check on the system when it gets something wrong. Instead, it's now the most blatant form of institutional corruption we have.
We've got to keep calling it out. If we're serious about winning elections -- about building a country where accountability and responsibility actually mean something -- then this can't slide. Because _this_ is why a third of Americans don't vote. They see a rigged game. A system where the rich and well connected protect each other, and everyone else gets screwed.
We can't let it happen again. Not with the pardons. Not with Epstein. Not with any of it. We've got to keep the pressure on -- even when it's hard, even when it's messy, even when it's one of our own.

It was meant to be a failsafe -- a check on the system when it gets something wrong. Instead, it's now the most blatant form of institutional corruption we have. We've got to keep calling it out. If we're serious about winning elections -- about building a country where accountability and responsibility actually mean something -- then this can't slide. Because this is why a third of Americans don't vote. They see a rigged game. A system where the rich and well connected protect each other, and everyone else gets screwed. We can't let it happen again. Not with the pardons. Not with Epstein. Not with any of it. We've got to keep the pressure on -- even when it's hard, even when it's messy, even when it's one of our own. Mike Nellis is a Democratic strategist and entrepreneur who has raised over $1 billion for Democratic campaigns and causes. He 's the founder of Authentic.org, an award-winning fundraising and advertising agency, and a former Senior Advisor to Vice President Kamala Harris. This Substack is where he writes with endless urgency about the issues of the day--and how we can save the Democratic Party and our democracy. You 're currently a free subscriber to Endless Urgency with Mike Nellis. For the full experience or to support my work so I can keep it free for as many people as possible, upgrade your subscription. | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Mike Nellis 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

# [Cancel Trump's Ridiculous White House Ballroom](https://default.salsalabs.org/T8b7f5c0b-1327-47c8-a56c-8a53fe224771/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [](https://default.salsalabs.org/Teaee5f16-f6e9-4459-9688-8b37e3d08e0b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | _(After you take this action, come see others on on[our new Substack page](https://default.salsalabs.org/T1e0972f6-c478-4b11-a9bc-a093e36ca326/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)! Thanks to the [20,000+](https://default.salsalabs.org/T0e074215-9b9c-4868-945d-64a753cb17bb/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) there!)_ Inflation is rising, job creation has plummeted, and the deficit is at an all-time high. America is on the wrong track, but the solution is clear: **Ballroom! Amirite? ** Trump's 90,000 foot, gold-gilded monstrosity (seriously, it looks like the goose that lays golden eggs had explosive diarrhea) has been a disaster since it started, and it's time to end the stupidity. And now Trump has fired the architect, and gotten a new one, who will probably be succeeded by a dozen others, none of whome will actually get paid. We don't need a White House ballroom twice the size of the White House itself. It's DC, and there are about eleventy million event spaces of every size across the city that could be used. And if you really want to expand the space at the White House itself, fine, but add what you need, not enough to have Earth over for dinner. [**Take action, and end this now: "Cancel Trump's Ridiculous White House Ballroom"**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T9fd5904c-893a-4ddf-9bad-3ac2d06a0123/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

name: Jane Fonda
subject: 🎂 You’re invited to my 88th birthday roast
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee
body:
My friends promised embarrassing stories. I said yes anyway.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120676597/587014976/1328794144?refcode=ea.81308d74-f3d1-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.81308d74-f3d1-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjgxMzA4ZDc0LWYzZDEtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=lrXt03to74ioBiPh_75-LMt6crluyh-f00tnIxt-tZM=&emci=c1078bec-5cd1-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=81308d74-f3d1-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=4823105)
---
I'm turning 88 this month, and my team has decided that instead of letting me celebrate quietly (which, let's be honest, was never going to happen), they're throwing me a proper roast. **[On December 17th, Lily Tomlin and some of my dearest friends are gathering virtually to roast and toast me — which means embarrassing stories, heartfelt moments, and reflections on a lifetime of raising hell for what's right. And I want YOU to be there.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120676598/587014977/1820297183?refcode=ea.81308d74-f3d1-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.81308d74-f3d1-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjgxMzA4ZDc0LWYzZDEtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=lrXt03to74ioBiPh_75-LMt6crluyh-f00tnIxt-tZM=)** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120676599/587014978/1820297183?refcode=ea.81308d74-f3d1-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.81308d74-f3d1-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjgxMzA4ZDc0LWYzZDEtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=lrXt03to74ioBiPh_75-LMt6crluyh-f00tnIxt-tZM=) | [**🎟️ Reserve your ticket now →**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120676600/587014979/1820297183?refcode=ea.81308d74-f3d1-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.81308d74-f3d1-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjgxMzA4ZDc0LWYzZDEtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=lrXt03to74ioBiPh_75-LMt6crluyh-f00tnIxt-tZM=)
---
At 88, you start to realize that what matters isn't how many candles are on the cake — it's what you do with the time you have left.
And what I want to dedicate my final act to is living life with intention. To electing climate champions who will fight like hell for our planet. To proving that ordinary people, standing together, can outmatch the fossil fuel industry’s billions. I want to look back on my 88th year knowing that we fought for one another, for the planet we love, and for every generation that will inherit what we do next.
**That's why every ticket to this event goes directly toward JanePAC's work to flip the House of Representatives on November 3, 2026, because my birthday wish is simple: send climate champions to Congress and send Republicans into a spiral.**
Oh, and did I mention? All ticket holders will receive the recipe for one of my favorite holiday cocktails (or mocktails, if that's more your style). You'll want something festive to toast with!
**[So don’t miss out on this incredible opportunity! RSVP today to join us virtually on December 17th at 5:00PM PT/ 8:00PM ET.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120676601/587014980/1820297183?refcode=ea.81308d74-f3d1-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.81308d74-f3d1-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjgxMzA4ZDc0LWYzZDEtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=lrXt03to74ioBiPh_75-LMt6crluyh-f00tnIxt-tZM=)**

clean_body: My friends promised embarrassing stories. I said yes anyway. | I'm turning 88 this month, and my team has decided that instead of letting me celebrate quietly (which, let's be honest, was never going to happen), they're throwing me a proper roast. = = | = At 88, you start to realize that what matters isn't how many candles are on the cake — it's what you do with the time you have left. And what I want to dedicate my final act to is living life with intention. To electing climate champions who will fight like hell for our planet. To proving that ordinary people, standing together, can outmatch the fossil fuel industry’s billions. I want to look back on my 88th year knowing that we fought for one another, for the planet we love, and for every generation that will inherit what we do next. That's why every ticket to this event goes directly toward JanePAC's work to flip the House of Representatives on November 3, 2026, because my birthday wish is simple: send climate champions to Congress and send Republicans into a spiral. Oh, and did I mention? All ticket holders will receive the recipe for one of my favorite holiday cocktails (or mocktails, if that's more your style). You'll want something festive to toast with! = = I hope to see you there! xx Jane = | = | = = Follow along: | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|--- Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee. This is the whole reason behind President Donald Trump's massive anti-voting executive order. | | We all know that mail-in voting laws are about ballot accessibility, **making it easier for voters to participate in our elections**. Elderly voters, the disabled, our military service members -- they all vote by mail.Mail-in voting has been happening back to the Civil War. It's always been safe and secure, and mail-in voting fraud is supremely rare. States have spent millions of taxpayer dollars investigating voter fraud since 2020's "big lie" and found virtually nothing. **Mail-in voting has been used widely in some states for decades and in most of the country since the pandemic.** There's no good faith reason to get rid of it. **[Opponents of voting rights have proven they'll go to any length necessary to stop people from voting and make sure that they stay in power. clean_body: @JeffMerkley: Why does Trump want to ban Vote by Mail? Because it blocks Election Day interference: closing polling places, long lines, voter suppression. Vote by Mail is the best tool we have to put power where it belongs—in the hands of the people. I'll fight like hell to protect it.| | | | | This is the whole ball game, Team: = This is the whole reason behind President Donald Trump's massive anti-voting executive order. After every election in which he's been a candidate or in power, Trump has raged over the use of mail-in ballots in general and ballot deadlines in particular . He's tried to claim fraud for every single ballot counted after Election Day. It's worth noting that Trump himself has used mail-in ballots to vote , most recently in an election in his now-home state of Florida.This isn't about safety or security. It's a blatantly partisan exercise to suppress the vote and make it harder for you to have your voice heard in our elections. It's about their anti-democratic authoritarian desires and need for total control. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = | = | = | | = | = | = | | We all know that mail-in voting laws are about ballot accessibility, making it easier for voters to participate in our elections. Elderly voters, the disabled, our military service members -- they all vote by mail.Mail-in voting has been happening back to the Civil War. It's always been safe and secure, and mail-in voting fraud is supremely rare. States have spent millions of taxpayer dollars investigating voter fraud since 2020's "big lie" and found virtually nothing. Mail-in voting has been used widely in some states for decades and in most of the country since the pandemic. There's no good faith reason to get rid of it. = | | | | | | | ---|---|---|--- Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country. clean_body: | | | Team, As a former teacher and, more recently, a high school principal in Harlem, I know how focused so many educators are on working tirelessly to close the opportunity and achievement gaps that exist across our country. Yet, we are watching politicians systematically undermine educators and the education system across the country, including right here in Florida. Education is the great equalizer. It transformed my students’ story, and it has the power to transform millions more. During college, I taught English to children in New Delhi's slums, worked in Senator Bill Nelson's Washington office, and advocated through the I Have A Dream Foundation. Later, I went from my classroom to being a principal to the White House - and then, to working with state leaders at Apple. Through it all, I've learned that real change happens when policymakers work in partnership with the communities they serve. I’m fighting for: ALL children to have access to universal pre-kindergarten programs. ALL educators to have the tools to help students grow every day. ALL Americans to gain the skills to succeed in a 21st-century economy. I am determined to make our institutions vehicles for educational excellence and economic freedom. I will be a proud voice for policies that put all Americans on a path to prosperity. | | | ---|---|--- | | To a better future, Pia Dandiya Pia Dandiya is a first-generation American, educator, and public servant who has dedicated her life to expanding opportunity for others. She’s running for Congress to build an economy that empowers every American to thrive—not just survive—and to defend the programs that millions rely on, like Social Security, Medicaid, and support for our veterans. Pia is ready to bring bold, people-first leadership to Washington—and she’s counting on your support. Paid for by Pia Dandiya for Congress

name: Roy Cooper
subject: Peter, this is how we can win in North Carolina.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
Let’s do this again.
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98378?refcode=em20251205-9794&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9794%2E614464%2E6vtDol)
North Carolina is one of the last true swing states on the map, Peter. I should know.
I won both my elections for Governor in years that Donald Trump carried the state. It wasn’t easy, but with a groundswell of grassroots enthusiasm, we prevailed.
In the last few weeks, we’ve begun building out a team that can win here again – this time for a seat in the United States Senate. We have a plan that we know can provide Democrats with success here in NC. And it’s only been possible through your support.
**[I have to ask for that support again today, Peter, because we’re preparing for this to become the most expensive U.S. Senate race in history. With your help, we’ll engage with voters from every corner of this state and prove that our vision for the future is right for North Carolina. Please split a donation between our campaign and Heartland Patriots.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98378?refcode=em20251205-9794&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=9794%2E614464%2E6vtDol)**
Statewide elections here are competitive and expensive. Republicans are already planning to spend hundreds of millions against us because they know this seat is the key to the Senate majority.
But as we begin sharing our message with North Carolinians — a message rooted in respect for the values we share and the fundamental rights we’ve spent so long fighting for — I am confident that we’ll be able to bring voters onto our team.
We can succeed here in North Carolina if we work together.
**[If I can count on you to stand with me in the fight, please split a contribution between our campaign and Heartland Patriots today.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98378?refcode=em20251205-9794&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=9794%2E614464%2E6vtDol)**
**[DONATE](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98378?refcode=em20251205-9794&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=9794%2E614464%2E6vtDol)**
Thanks so much,
Roy Cooper

| [](https://www.facebook.com/lucaskuncemo/) | [](https://twitter.com/lucaskuncemo) | [](https://www.instagram.com/lucaskuncemo/)

name: Roy Cooper HQ
subject: a dollar (that’s it)
body:
It could change the course of this race.
|
# [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1898?t=1&akid=98%2E124072%2Ejx7rzX)
Peter, before you delete this email, let us make our case: **[We only need 60 seconds of your time to request a dollar (that’s it) from your pocket.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2135?t=7&akid=98%2E124072%2Ejx7rzX)** Roy Cooper is in the midst of the most competitive U.S. Senate race of 2026 — and our MAGA opponent is gaining ground. Meanwhile, far-right actors are already pouring millions of dollars into defeating Roy. _Experts say this could be the first Senate race in history to cost over a BILLION dollars._ This is the moment we HAVE to create a groundswell of grassroots support to turn the tide back in our favor, Peter, and your support is critical to our success. Showing your support by donating now could help change the course of this toss-up race. **[If you’ve been waiting for the right moment to give or questioning whether a contribution fits in your budget, we want to make it easier for you. We are asking you to give $1 — that’s just four quarters — to say you’re with us.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2135?t=8&akid=98%2E124072%2Ejx7rzX)** | **[DONATE $1](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2135?t=9&akid=98%2E124072%2Ejx7rzX)**
---
We couldn’t do this without you,
Team Cooper
* * *
Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.

**[Donate today to help win this seat and flip the Senate blue.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?t=2&akid=98%2E124072%2Ejx7rzX)**
[DONATE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?t=3&akid=98%2E124072%2Ejx7rzX)
---

[SHOP OUR MERCH STORE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2133?t=5&akid=98%2E124072%2Ejx7rzX)
[info@roycooper.com](mailto:info@roycooper.com) | [Privacy Policy](https://act.roycooper.com/go/825?t=6&akid=98%2E124072%2Ejx7rzX)
Cooper Victory Fund
PO Box 1190
Raleigh, NC 27602
Paid for by Cooper Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Cooper for North Carolina, DSCC, and North Carolina Democratic Party — Federal.
---
Contributions or gifts to Cooper Victory Fund are not tax deductible.
The first $7,000 of each contribution from a person and the first $10,000 from a multicandidate committee (“PAC”) will be allocated to Cooper for North Carolina. The first $3,500 of a person’s contribution and the first $5,000 of a PAC’s contribution will be designated for the primary election; the next $3,500/$5,000 will be designated for the general election. The next $10,000 from a person and $5,000 from a PAC will be allocated to North Carolina Democratic Party – Federal. The next $44,300 of a contribution from a person and the next $15,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC General Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Headquarters Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Legal Fund. A contributor may designate his or her contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if following it would result in an excessive contribution.
Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address. If you have donated with a different email address, with a check, or with a spouse — thank you so much! We have that on file and cannot thank you enough for supporting this campaign.
By clicking the links above, you certify that you are responsible for paying all charges incurred in using the debit or credit card to be charged, and that your personal funds will be the true source of the contribution.

If you wish to receive fewer messages from us, [submit this form to let us know.](https://act.roycooper.com/signup/i-want-less-emails-cooper-north-carolina/?t=10&akid=98%2E124072%2Ejx7rzX)

name: Bushra Amiwala
subject: You and I were sold snake oil
body:
| | | | | [](https://e96gjzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0018m2cG5QrzQUht4CFHwOEQ-fhGjEPpMW41yWrIwJQxkg1t-PDV25YWV_VKLX-3U3tEdhpQh-sLr7P56HAQGFSHIm0a4aMBcSRMa5cxneQB6hiibYvlo9Kgju243pkzUeIdww1GAQqG6WWgA8luxMxQu4aK3DRrGXuOVRKmPwse20c0eZE8MFYv7MTbNrQVboHpyfUssPTxqQC3Lde5WH_YypEhXZD2HTf&c=0bbKgvhKIDrkF56JnOrbA6cdUnY1eN42ctatvYR2Pz2MeePYoh6S8g==&ch=dqq9RXyyJfxvT2hC2NUyxvP8dlFqurPvcrTxlhuBBChDbbq6Xf8s6w==)
---
|
Peter,
In 2024, Donald Trump ran on a promise of economic populism, vowing to lower costs and help the working class.
Less than a year later, the verdict is in: Americans were sold snake oil.
---
| [](https://e96gjzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0018m2cG5QrzQUht4CFHwOEQ-fhGjEPpMW41yWrIwJQxkg1t-PDV25YWV_VKLX-3U3tEdhpQh-sLr7P56HAQGFSHIm0a4aMBcSRMa5cxneQB6hiibYvlo9Kgju243pkzUeIdww1GAQqG6WWgA8luxMxQu4aK3DRrGXuOVRKmPwse20c0eZE8MFYv7MTbNrQVboHpyfUssPTxqRv8bo_9YKxntY2VIEnSpks&c=0bbKgvhKIDrkF56JnOrbA6cdUnY1eN42ctatvYR2Pz2MeePYoh6S8g==&ch=dqq9RXyyJfxvT2hC2NUyxvP8dlFqurPvcrTxlhuBBChDbbq6Xf8s6w==)
---
His policies have pushed the economy to the brink. Energy bills, healthcare premiums, and grocery prices are skyrocketing.
The average US home buyer is now 59 years old. An entire generation is being locked out of the American Dream, saddled with student loan debt and forced to rent forever while private equity firms buy up our neighborhoods.
Seniors can't afford their insulin. Americans are losing their jobs en masse. But billionaires? They're doing better than ever.
This is the consequence of decades of the same old "trickle-down" economics that has rigged the economy to line the pocket of billionaires while families in my district work themselves to the bone just to survive.
It is time for Democrats to actually fix the rigged economy.
That's why I am running on a vision where my neighbors don't just work to survive--but thrive. That means fighting for single-payer universal healthcare, breaking up monopolies that drive costs up, building affordable housing, canceling student debt while making public colleges tuition-free, and cutting our bloated military budget to finally invest in education and our communities.
This is how we fix a rigged system. If you are ready to send a representative to Congress who will bring real solutions to the real problems working people face, I need you with me.

clean_body: | | | | | == | Peter, In 2024, Donald Trump ran on a promise of economic populism, vowing to lower costs and help the working class. Less than a year later, the verdict is in: Americans were sold snake oil. | == His policies have pushed the economy to the brink. Energy bills, healthcare premiums, and grocery prices are skyrocketing. The average US home buyer is now 59 years old. An entire generation is being locked out of the American Dream, saddled with student loan debt and forced to rent forever while private equity firms buy up our neighborhoods. Seniors can't afford their insulin. Americans are losing their jobs en masse. But billionaires? They're doing better than ever. This is the consequence of decades of the same old "trickle-down" economics that has rigged the economy to line the pocket of billionaires while families in my district work themselves to the bone just to survive. It is time for Democrats to actually fix the rigged economy. That's why I am running on a vision where my neighbors don't just work to survive--but thrive. That means fighting for single-payer universal healthcare, breaking up monopolies that drive costs up, building affordable housing, canceling student debt while making public colleges tuition-free, and cutting our bloated military budget to finally invest in education and our communities. This is how we fix a rigged system. If you are ready to send a representative to Congress who will bring real solutions to the real problems working people face, I need you with me. == | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | == | | == | | == | | == | | == | | == | == | Let's take our future back. In solidarity, | == Bushra Amiwala Democrat for IL-9 | | == | | | | == | Mail checks to: Bushra for Congress PO Box 911 Skokie, IL 60076 | | == | | | | Bushra for Congress | PO Box 911 | Skokie, IL 60076 US | |

https://rokhanna.com [https://rokhanna.com]
Peter,
Millions of families know Ms. Rachel as a beloved educator – a modern-day Mr. Rogers whose work helps children learn kindness, empathy, and confidence. She has always used her platform to speak up for children around the world.
Now she is under attack for speaking out on behalf of starving children in Gaza. Will you join me and sign my petition to stand with Ms. Rachel? [https://act.rokhanna.com/a/ms-rachel-petition?contactdata=xCxRbTroXOQzt2jmtUEUiNGfIiM018mE+URgd15EkGiS7aO2mtMwuUrfVWntiGyjvOVqDAS2vhHVh6KnkD0OY72h/6NM4z1q/8wh6oGAG5ej7h3IUFB4dNaB6guucTeHg+CYDqU2VofNZYLtuoSWdeOQjf+RqR83oc0Nq0cPY3fBJvmXd8poUIK1/FpHuhLT]
https://act.rokhanna.com/a/ms-rachel-petition?contactdata=xCxRbTroXOQzt2jmtUEUiNGfIiM018mE+URgd15EkGiS7aO2mtMwuUrfVWntiGyjvOVqDAS2vhHVh6KnkD0OY72h/6NM4z1q/8wh6oGAG5ej7h3IUFB4dNaB6guucTeHg+CYDqU2VofNZYLtuoSWdeOQjf+RqR83oc0Nq0cPY3fBJvmXd8poUIK1/FpHuhLT [https://act.rokhanna.com/a/ms-rachel-petition?contactdata=xCxRbTroXOQzt2jmtUEUiNGfIiM018mE+URgd15EkGiS7aO2mtMwuUrfVWntiGyjvOVqDAS2vhHVh6KnkD0OY72h/6NM4z1q/8wh6oGAG5ej7h3IUFB4dNaB6guucTeHg+CYDqU2VofNZYLtuoSWdeOQjf+RqR83oc0Nq0cPY3fBJvmXd8poUIK1/FpHuhLT]
Let me be clear: Supporting the dignity and safety of all children is not antisemitism. It is a basic moral commitment we all share: that every child, regardless of where they live, deserves food, safety, and a future.
It takes courage to speak up. I want to send Ms. Rachel a note to let her know that we have her back. Will you join me? [https://act.rokhanna.com/a/ms-rachel-petition?contactdata=xCxRbTroXOQzt2jmtUEUiNGfIiM018mE+URgd15EkGiS7aO2mtMwuUrfVWntiGyjvOVqDAS2vhHVh6KnkD0OY72h/6NM4z1q/8wh6oGAG5ej7h3IUFB4dNaB6guucTeHg+CYDqU2VofNZYLtuoSWdeOQjf+RqR83oc0Nq0cPY3fBJvmXd8poUIK1/FpHuhLT]
ADD YOUR NAME [https://act.rokhanna.com/a/ms-rachel-petition?contactdata=xCxRbTroXOQzt2jmtUEUiNGfIiM018mE+URgd15EkGiS7aO2mtMwuUrfVWntiGyjvOVqDAS2vhHVh6KnkD0OY72h/6NM4z1q/8wh6oGAG5ej7h3IUFB4dNaB6guucTeHg+CYDqU2VofNZYLtuoSWdeOQjf+RqR83oc0Nq0cPY3fBJvmXd8poUIK1/FpHuhLT]
Thank you for your support, your activism, and all that you do.
Onward,
Ro
Wait! One more thing–
Ro doesn't accept a cent from lobbyists or PACs, meaning our work is completely funded by grassroots supporters like you. By donating just $3 today, you’re helping us create a country that works for everyone, not just those at the top. That means a renewed investment in American manufacturing, Medicare for All, student debt relief, and a green future.
If you want to support our people-powered campaign as we continue to fight for progress in Washington, please consider making a donation of $3 or more today. Thank you!

name: The Grant Institute
subject: Grant Report: December 5, 2025
body:
Election News Update from the Grant Institute

| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/a50f9a9c5fab/grant-report-september-12-12770572?e=c16f400a56)
---
|
---
| | | | | | **The Perils of Online Voting****By Justin Riemer, President, USGI, and Marshal Trigg, Junior Counsel, USGI**
Christmas came early in Anchorage, Alaska, [according to the New York Times](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=87a0440fdf&e=c16f400a56).
The Mobile Voting Project (“an experiment that feels both inevitable and impossibly futuristic,” cooed the Times) now allows voters to receive and cast ballots entirely online after providing identification to election officials. The article, while noting security concerns, ultimately presents the initiative sympathetically. Anchorage’s election administrator told the paper they’re “trying to make it even easier . . . to vote . . . so you don’t really have an excuse not to vote anymore.”
That framing is a common one; but is voting a service that must be made maximally convenient? And should convenience be subordinate to other considerations like election security?
What the Times largely omits is more instructive. A group of the nation’s leading voting security experts [issued a pointed response](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=848c306aea&e=c16f400a56) criticizing the article for ignoring decades of research reaching the same conclusion: “online voting is not secure.”
Their statement also highlighted a few inconvenient facts. Bradley Tusk, the venture capitalist funding the Mobile Voting Project, previously commissioned a project to develop standards for secure online voting. When his own [team of experts warned](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=2026e362db&e=c16f400a56) that “current technology cannot provide the level of security needed” for internet voting, Tusk ignored them and pressed ahead anyway. And despite Tusk’s claims that mobile voting increases participation, his 2020 trial in Washington’s King County Conservation District elections saw turnout [remain flat](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=f734e2ad53&e=c16f400a56) at 0.49% of registered voters. The promised democratic revival didn’t materialize, yet the pitch remains unchanged.
Indeed, many security experts chafed at the King County venture. As one [put it](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=3c4e754be8&e=c16f400a56): “I don’t know that I have run across cybersecurity experts whose mortgages are not paid by a mobile-voting company who think it’s a good idea.” The same year, MIT researchers [determined](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=d55085675d&e=c16f400a56) that the mobile voting app “Voatz”—also funded by Tusk—contained vulnerabilities that “allow different kinds of adversaries to alter, stop, or expose a user’s vote.”
Other experiments in online voting hint at its potentially catastrophic consequences. A [study](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=9856ddbb34&e=c16f400a56) of Ontario’s 2022 online municipal elections revealed flaws and other vulnerabilities “that risked secretly hijacking up to a million online ballots.” And online voting certainly doesn’t eliminate the possibility of human error. Ironically, the [International Association for Cryptologic Research (IACR)](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=41ce65a015&e=c16f400a56) recently had to [rerun](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=7f1b9948e9&e=c16f400a56) its online leadership elections “after an official lost the encrypted key needed to unlock” the results.
Those who oppose internet voting aren’t Luddites or partisans. They [include](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=d3b9b87952&e=c16f400a56) some of the most respected computer scientists in the world who base their opposition on [technical realities](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=f052db16e0&e=c16f400a56): the attack surface is too large, the defenses are inadequate, and the consequences of failure are severe.
Internet voting is often sold as the future, i.e., a natural progression from online commerce to civic participation. However, elections are inherently different. For-profit industries have determined that the increased profits from commerce, such as online banking, outweigh the inevitable cons due to fraud. But such a cost-benefit analysis is obviously ill suited to voting. Elections are not designed to absorb a certain amount of fraud and still deliver intended results. Votes must be both secret and secure. Anonymity makes it impossible to verify individual ballots were counted as intended, and you rarely can reverse election results once officials are sworn into office. A successful attack on an election might never be detected and, even if it were, cannot be remedied.
The stakes are far higher than individual convenience. And the real test of election administration isn’t whether it embraces the latest technology, but whether it maintains public confidence while ensuring all and only eligible voters can participate. In an era of intense polarization and disagreement on voting policies, opposition to online voting remains one of the few areas of expert consensus across the political spectrum. We should preserve it.
