Let's make history in 2026 – together.

name: American Patriot Daily
subject: Extraordinary Courage. Extraordinary Comeback.
body:
unsubscribe
https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xul=1nmc5a54x67zfmd5fi2bt1ih1a32znfdvzx2gdabijyibmzw850ujscqnyj1u3lxnqx3e66zb26hm7xag4ctouo1k22c6tp0g78um9nymi034hkrc0pp&__stmp=t6t2ky&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh

Please read the message below from our sponsor.

Steve Thompson, Editor

“I did everything I could for him ...”

https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xtl=t0sh775makq4p5ja2v6yzitcfpi5h6aki5v6k4u77tas4o6k36aie1l72irxpi2w1i4m9n081dkq4hwqpwbnf9luwbvykdndfjuisy7j9mo6apk1kzbhrjksne9goentv80504xv0cscasi47g3n1pg9vj51vj68ajsfg&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t6t2ky&__onlt=t

https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xtl=t0sh775makq4p5ja2v6yzitcfpi5h6aki5v6k4u77tas4o6k36aie1l72irxpi2w1i4m9n081dkq4hwqpwbnf9luwbvykdndfjuisy7j9mo6apk1kzbhrjksne9goentv80504xv0cscasi47g3n1pg9vj51vj68ajsfg&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t6t2ky&__onlt=t

Not long ago, Dr. Shafir Botner, director of MAGEN DAVID ADOM’s paramedic school and a Medevac helicopter paramedic, was treating injured civilians at the scene of a rocket strike in Be’er Sheva, Israel, when he looked up and recognized a familiar face. There, working alongside the MAGEN DAVID ADOM team to assist the injured civilians was Chief Inspector Or Yosef of the Israel Police.

The last time Dr. Botner had seen him, Inspector Yosef was fighting for his life after being critically wounded by an RPG while helping civilians during the Nova Music Festival massacre on October 7, 2023.

A MAGEN DAVID ADOM paramedic was treating the inspector when he realized Yosef would not survive long enough to make it to the hospital in his ambulance. So, he immediately called for a helicopter.

When Dr. Botner and his crew landed, they quickly loaded Inspector Yosef into the helicopter.
They knew every second mattered
https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xtl=t0sh775makq4p5ja2v6yzitcfpi5h6aki5v6k4u77tas4o6k36aie1l72irxpi2w1i4m9n081dkq4hwqpwbnf9luwbvykdndfjuisy7j9mo6apk1kzbhrjksne9goentv80504xv0cscasi47g3n1pg9vj51vj68ajsfg&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t6t2ky&__onlt=t
.

“He was in very serious condition and had lost a massive amount of blood.

I did everything I could for him.”

But now, this patient who so nearly died on October 7 was not only still alive, he was also outside helping others. Seeing Inspector Yosef up on his feet and back on the job filled Dr. Botner with emotion.

“Or Yosef is truly an inspiration. To return to the field after such a devastating injury and help others injured in a missile attack — that’s extraordinary.”

I wish you could go with me sometime when I visit the MAGEN DAVID ADOM National Dispatch Center just outside Tel Aviv or one of the 206 MAGEN DAVID ADOM emergency medical stations located across Israel. There you will encounter some of the most extraordinary men and women you may ever meet.

There is something about the determination and compassion I see in their faces that always moves me. They are there to save lives.

HELP SAVE LIVES IN ISRAEL ►
https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xtl=t0sh775makq4p5ja2v6yzitcfpi5h6aki5v6k4u77tas4o6k36aie1l72irxpi2w1i4m9n081dkq4hwqpwbnf9luwbvykdndfjuisy7j9mo6apk1kzbhrjksne9goentv80504xv0cscasi47g3n1pg9vj51vj68ajsfg&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t6t2ky&__onlt=t

It takes grit and guts to do what they do every day. When a call comes in, they respond without hesitation — missiles may be falling, active shooters may be on the scene, buildings may be burning, but they are committed to going above and beyond to save as many lives as possible, even if it means putting their own lives on the line.

Again and again, these paramedics, EMTs, and first responders rise above fear, push beyond limits, and stand in the gap. Their bravery, their sacrifice, their relentless dedication — it’s nothing less than extraordinary.

May I ask you today to
give to support these exceptional women and men
https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xtl=t0sh775makq4p5ja2v6yzitcfpi5h6aki5v6k4u77tas4o6k36aie1l72irxpi2w1i4m9n081dkq4hwqpwbnf9luwbvykdndfjuisy7j9mo6apk1kzbhrjksne9goentv80504xv0cscasi47g3n1pg9vj51vj68ajsfg&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t6t2ky&__onlt=t
?

MAGEN DAVID ADOM relies almost entirely upon private donations and not government funding — which means the people of Israel are counting on the faithful financial support of generous friends like you so that help is able to come in moments of dire need.

Your gift
https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xtl=t0sh775makq4p5ja2v6yzitcfpi5h6aki5v6k4u77tas4o6k36aie1l72irxpi2w1i4m9n081dkq4hwqpwbnf9luwbvykdndfjuisy7j9mo6apk1kzbhrjksne9goentv80504xv0cscasi47g3n1pg9vj51vj68ajsfg&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t6t2ky&__onlt=t
will provide the medical supplies and equipment, blood reserves, and emergency vehicles that these extraordinary modern-day heroes must have to save every man, woman, and child possible.

God bless you,

Catherine Reed

Chief Executive Officer

GIVE TO SAVE LIVES ►
https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xtl=t0sh775makq4p5ja2v6yzitcfpi5h6aki5v6k4u77tas4o6k36aie1l72irxpi2w1i4m9n081dkq4hwqpwbnf9luwbvykdndfjuisy7j9mo6apk1kzbhrjksne9goentv80504xv0cscasi47g3n1pg9vj51vj68ajsfg&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t6t2ky&__onlt=t

MAGEN DAVID ADOM is Israel’s emergency services system, providing critical medical care and ambulance services for victims of terror, natural disasters, automobile accidents, and all other emergencies in Israel. It is also responsible for Israel’s national blood supply. MAGEN DAVID ADOM saves lives in Israel every day. Become a
Lifeline Partner
https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xtl=1bnpvbp0dtrlv9siskvp3gyaj6n1vq5h7bmsfsggxeddjzztbdao36qrv498wlobnctjejtz6u21ji1qquvzeh7zbodo3b81j4o4uamv3m148sqjdpqohzy6wobimp9yg9t8mgcw9k8wn6l7pntswdize2fmqa31s&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t6t2ky&__onlt=t
now.

name: Mandela Barnes
subject: I couldn’t believe my ears
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mandela for Wisconsin
body:
“[Barnes] belongs to the party... of slavery”
| Friend, I couldn’t believe my ears when I heard this. First, my Republican opponent called me a dangerous extremist because I want to make it easier for families to make ends meet. Now, he’s going on radio talk shows and saying this: | “I would remind Mr. Barnes: he belongs to the party that was the party of slavery.”
**— GOP Frontrunner Tom Tiffany**
---
I don’t have to tell you how outrageous this stuff is. But I will ask you this: **[can you power my campaign with a donation of any amount that’s meaningful to you right now so we can reject the kind of politics that aims to divide and distract us?](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=mb-em-fr-dec25.SSbw4W&amount=15&refcode=SBD_MB_EM_FR_120525_Tiffany_Tier1_D1_S1&t=2&akid=5683%2E196590%2EzAn-fx)**

Let me take a moment to remind Mr. Tiffany of a few things: his party has **failed** to ensure people can afford their healthcare, **failed** to protect our farmers, and **failed** to lower the cost of groceries.
Voters deserve substance from their elected officials.
This kind of rhetoric does nothing to expand Badgercare, keep our communities safe, put food in the mouths of hungry people, or fund our schools.
_My Republican opponent is stuck in the past — I’m focused on building a better future for everyone in Wisconsin._**[Please chip in to fight back against this unacceptable rhetoric and support a campaign focused on delivering for Wisconsin.](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=mb-em-fr-dec25.SSbw4W&amount=15&refcode=SBD_MB_EM_FR_120525_Tiffany_Tier1_D1_S1&t=9&akid=5683%2E196590%2EzAn-fx)**
Thanks for standing with me,
Mandela Barnes

clean_body: While you’re out holiday shopping, Democrats are out shopping too — for new ways to tax you, control you, and MASS up New Hampshire. | | | | | | | | | | | Friend,While you’re out holiday shopping, Democrats are out shopping too — for new ways to tax you, control you, and MASS up New Hampshire. So this year, we’re making it easy:Give the gift of stopping Democrats. Seriously. It’s the only gift you can give that won’t break, won’t clutter the house, and will help keep New Hampshire free. Because while families across the state are hanging lights and baking cookies, the left is baking up schemes to turn our state into Massachusetts. | | | Governor Ayotte and our Republican majorities are delivering historic wins — banning sanctuary cities, bail reform, education freedom, and no new or increased taxes.So if you want to give New Hampshire something meaningful this year... If you want to protect everything we’ve built... If you want to make Democrats’ holidays slightly less cheerful... | | | Thanks for standing with us — and for giving the best gift of all: stopping Democrats before they can ruin the state we love.Merry Christmas and Happy Holidays, NH GOP Finance Team | | | | | | | | | Paid for by the New Hampshire Republican State Committee, Chairman Jim MacEachern. name: Team Maxine
subject: Wish Maxine a Happy Birthday!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maxine for Congress
body:
https://maxinefororegon.com/ [https://maxinefororegon.com/]
Peter, will you join us in wishing Maxine a very happy birthday? [https://secure.ngpvan.com/PojlEaKmmEmOli7EpCAM6g2]
SIGN OUR CARD [https://secure.ngpvan.com/PojlEaKmmEmOli7EpCAM6g2]
https://secure.ngpvan.com/PojlEaKmmEmOli7EpCAM6g2 [https://secure.ngpvan.com/PojlEaKmmEmOli7EpCAM6g2]
Every day, Maxine’s compassion and clarity of purpose inspire us.
She fights for our civil rights, our healthcare, and our communities—no matter what obstacles people try to throw in her way. That’s just who she is— she’s the real deal.
That’s why we are so excited to celebrate her today here at Dexter HQ! [https://secure.ngpvan.com/PojlEaKmmEmOli7EpCAM6g2]
Many of you saw the headlines, but we were there when she boarded a plane to El Salvador to help Kilmar Abrego Garcia. We walked down the hall with Maxine when she carried a Pride flag onto the House floor in protest of discriminatory policies. We sat in the backseat when she drove to deportation centers across Oregon, determined to find and reunite a family.
These moments don’t always make the news. But they make Maxine who she is. And that’s why we’re celebrating her today.
So, Peter, will you join us in wishing Maxine a very happy birthday by signing her card? [https://secure.ngpvan.com/PojlEaKmmEmOli7EpCAM6g2]
SHARE YOUR BIRTHDAY WISHES [https://secure.ngpvan.com/PojlEaKmmEmOli7EpCAM6g2]
Love,
Team Maxine
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_med_email-25q4?refcode=x_251205_HBDMaxine_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_med_email-25q4?refcode=x_251205_HBDMaxine_act] Maxine Dexter is a critical care physician with decades of experience using facts and following science to help her patients.
Now she's using those skills in Congress to ensure healthy communities by standing up to Big Pharma, fighting climate change, and expanding reproductive rights, as well as rebuilding a fair economy for all by protecting Social Security and Medicare.
To learn more about Maxine, visit MaxineForOregon.com [https://maxinefororegon.com/] .

name: Michael Bennet
subject: We're paying more and getting less. I'm sick of it.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for Governor
body:
So despite what billionaires like Trump wants us to think, the affordability crisis is no "hoax." That means together, we can fix it.
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120684463/587037646/-2054079050?refcode=jtk1009-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiYzc1NTI4Yy1mY2QxLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=duPPnVpZg15biKYqFjBYryRvVZJnFObV2TwvMFxIlUU=)
---
Friend – is it just me, or does it seem like nowadays, we're all paying more for _much, much_ less?
Home ownership is out of reach. Child care is on par with college tuition. Shopping for health insurance is like playing a guessing game you can't win, _if_ your job offers it at all. We’re paying about 30% more for groceries now than we did in 2020... and I could keep going.
And, Trump’s tariffs are driving prices higher – _just in time for the holidays._ Yet as corporations boast record profits, take-home pay isn't keeping up.
So, despite what our president wants us to think, you know as well as I do that the affordability crisis is _not_ "fake news." That means we _can_ fix it.
**We just have to stop waiting on Washington Republicans to deliver on their empty promises and create solutions in Colorado, for Coloradans.**
**_[And if I can count on the grassroots support of folks like you](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120684466/587037647/851209409?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk1009-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiYzc1NTI4Yy1mY2QxLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=duPPnVpZg15biKYqFjBYryRvVZJnFObV2TwvMFxIlUU=)_ to win the governor's race, my plan to lower the cost of living starts by:**
* Building affordable homes closer to jobs and transit
* Taking on home and auto insurance options to make them budget-friendly
* Cutting costs for health care, mental health care and prescription drugs
* Keeping rural clinics and hospitals open, staffed, and operating
* Making child care within reach for working families
* Expanding cheaper and reliable transportation statewide
* Stabilizing energy rates (and keeping service reliable)
* Fixing the roads
* Forcing energy-sucking data centers to pay their fair share

**As governor, my top priority will be leveling the playing field so that our economy **_actually works_**for Colorado's working families again.**
But heading into 2026, President Trump and his allies will spend _anything_ to crush our people-powered momentum and elect someone who'll let Washington keep nickel-and-diming Colorado families. **That means I won't be able to rise above the guaranteed flood of GOP-allied corporate attacks to come without your small-dollar support today.**
**_[So if you're in a place where you can spare a few bucks today, will you pitch in $5 or any amount that works best for you right away?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120684471/587037649/851209409?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&amount=5&refcode=jtk1009-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiYzc1NTI4Yy1mY2QxLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=duPPnVpZg15biKYqFjBYryRvVZJnFObV2TwvMFxIlUU=)_**

| [Donate $25](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120684483/587037653/851209409?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=jtk1009-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiYzc1NTI4Yy1mY2QxLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=duPPnVpZg15biKYqFjBYryRvVZJnFObV2TwvMFxIlUU=)

name: Emily Randall
subject: I'm endorsing 3️⃣ Democrats in toss-up races
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Emily Randall for Congress
body:
These leaders will actually uphold the f**king Constitution!

| Big news, Peter: I’m endorsing three️ more Democrats running to flip competitive districts from red to blue! I’m proud to support**Denise Powell** in Nebraska’s 2nd District, **Manny Rutinel** in Colorado’s 8th District, and **Marni Von Wilpert** in California’s 48th District. These leaders care deeply about building an America that’s affordable and healthy – and when elected, they’ll _actually uphold the f**king Constitution._ [_**If that’s all you need to hear, split a contribution of $25 or any amount between our campaigns so we can replace billionaire-backed Republicans with champions for working people →**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120686092/587042595/803658276?amount=25&refcode=RS_EM_FR_2025.12.05_Endorsements_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YWUwNmNhYS1mZGQxLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=AKfjNpa6LZTr5UVxDgIZ2ZWPffYxCFnHPi9TDmH8VZ8=) Let’s meet the candidates: [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120686093/587042596/803658276?amount=25&refcode=RS_EM_FR_2025.12.05_Endorsements_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YWUwNmNhYS1mZGQxLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=AKfjNpa6LZTr5UVxDgIZ2ZWPffYxCFnHPi9TDmH8VZ8=) **➡️ **Denise Powell is a mom, small business owner, public school champion, and healthcare advocate. Last year, she worked with teachers and parents to repeal a bill that would have given public tax dollars to private schools. 💡 Nebraska’s 2nd is an open seat, and one of a handful of Republican Districts that Kamala Harris won last year. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120686094/587042597/803658276?amount=25&refcode=RS_EM_FR_2025.12.05_Endorsements_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YWUwNmNhYS1mZGQxLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=AKfjNpa6LZTr5UVxDgIZ2ZWPffYxCFnHPi9TDmH8VZ8=) **➡️ **Manny Rutinel is a Colorado State Representative who understands what working people are going through. Growing up, he worked every day after school and even sold his blood at a plasma center to help pay family bills. 💡 Colorado’s 8th District is one of the most competitive in the country, electing MAGA Republican Gabe Evans in 2024 by just 0.8 percent. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120686096/587042598/803658276?amount=25&refcode=RS_EM_FR_2025.12.05_Endorsements_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YWUwNmNhYS1mZGQxLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=AKfjNpa6LZTr5UVxDgIZ2ZWPffYxCFnHPi9TDmH8VZ8=) ➡️ Marni Von Wilpert is a lifelong public servant – from the Peace Corps, to writing pro-union legislation as a Congressional staffer, to taking on polluters as a civil prosecutor, to now serving on the San Diego City Council. 💡 She’s running against Republican Darrell Issa, the third-richest member of Congress. California’s 48th District is competitive following the redrawing of CA maps by Prop 50. **I need Denise, Manny, and Marni in Congress with me next year to clean up the MAGA mess and actually work for working people. clean_body: These leaders will actually uphold the fking Constitution! | Big news, Peter: I’m endorsing three️ more Democrats running to flip competitive districts from red to blue! I’m proud to supportDenise Powell in Nebraska’s 2nd District, Manny Rutinel in Colorado’s 8th District, and Marni Von Wilpert in California’s 48th District. These leaders care deeply about building an America that’s affordable and healthy – and when elected, they’ll actually uphold the fking Constitution. = Let’s meet the candidates: = ➡️ Denise Powell is a mom, small business owner, public school champion, and healthcare advocate. Last year, she worked with teachers and parents to repeal a bill that would have given public tax dollars to private schools. 💡 Nebraska’s 2nd is an open seat, and one of a handful of Republican Districts that Kamala Harris won last year. = ➡️ Manny Rutinel is a Colorado State Representative who understands what working people are going through. Growing up, he worked every day after school and even sold his blood at a plasma center to help pay family bills. 💡 Colorado’s 8th District is one of the most competitive in the country, electing MAGA Republican Gabe Evans in 2024 by just 0.8 percent. = ➡️ Marni Von Wilpert is a lifelong public servant – from the Peace Corps, to writing pro-union legislation as a Congressional staffer, to taking on polluters as a civil prosecutor, to now serving on the San Diego City Council. 💡 She’s running against Republican Darrell Issa, the third-richest member of Congress. California’s 48th District is competitive following the redrawing of CA maps by Prop 50. I need Denise, Manny, and Marni in Congress with me next year to clean up the MAGA mess and actually work for working people. Our future is counting on us. = = And if you can’t give right now, please Thank you for everything you do 💙 – Emily | | Emily Randall is proud to represent Washington’s 6th District in Congress. She grew up in a working-class Union household in Port Orchard and was the first in her family to attend college. Peter, Across the country — and right here in Vermont — we've seen a rise in disgusting, divisive attacks against our immigrant neighbors. Whether it's Afghans rebuilding their lives or Somali families contributing to our communities, some people would rather stoke hate than offer a hand. **Becca Balint is NOT one of those people.** She’s standing strong for all Vermonters — and all people — no matter where they were born, what language they speak, how they worship, or even who they voted for. **But she can’t do it alone.** **[If you're with Becca in rejecting fear and embracing compassion, chip in today to keep her voice in Congress LOUD and CLEAR.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120686178/587043005/-2008368835?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjYyZTRkYy1mZGQxLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4PB-rXiqzzws8U-7PuzM-c-o93d_n2yAKuLlzhOXVJg=)** **[CHIP IN HERE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120686181/587043008/-2008368835?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjYyZTRkYy1mZGQxLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4PB-rXiqzzws8U-7PuzM-c-o93d_n2yAKuLlzhOXVJg=)** Becca has even traveled to participate in immigration field hearings — showing up in person to speak up for the dignity and safety of those too often left behind. **She’s doing the work — and that work takes resources.** **Becca often says this moment demands both warriors and empaths, people who fight fiercely, and feel deeply. She’s proud to be both.** In the face of hate, she has doubled down on what makes Vermont special — **the belief that we always take care of each other.** When others look away or stay silent, Becca speaks out. But standing up to hate requires more than courage — it requires a community willing to show up and give what they can to make it all happen. clean_body: | = Peter, Across the country — and right here in Vermont — we've seen a rise in disgusting, divisive attacks against our immigrant neighbors. Whether it's Afghans rebuilding their lives or Somali families contributing to our communities, some people would rather stoke hate than offer a hand. Becca Balint is NOT one of those people. She’s standing strong for all Vermonters — and all people — no matter where they were born, what language they speak, how they worship, or even who they voted for. But she can’t do it alone. = = Becca has even traveled to participate in immigration field hearings — showing up in person to speak up for the dignity and safety of those too often left behind. She’s doing the work — and that work takes resources. Becca often says this moment demands both warriors and empaths, people who fight fiercely, and feel deeply. She’s proud to be both. In the face of hate, she has doubled down on what makes Vermont special — the belief that we always take care of each other. When others look away or stay silent, Becca speaks out. But standing up to hate requires more than courage — it requires a community willing to show up and give what they can to make it all happen. = Let’s show EVERYONE what it looks like to be a good neighbor. — Team Becca If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = mailto:beccabalintvt@beccabalint.com | Paid for by Becca Balint for Vermont P.O. Box 291 Burlington, VT 05402

| Donald Trump's brash decision to rig the maps in 2026 could backfire on him spectacularly.
Here's where things stand right now:
In November, California, under Gov. Gavin Newsom's leadership successfully passed a redistricting law which passed with 65% of the vote.
Then, Virginia Democrats announced a bold plan to redraw their maps and eliminate as many as 4-5 GOP seats.
But it's not nearly enough, with all of the red states Trump is gerrymandering, California and Virginia are but a drop in the bucket.
It is on us to pick up the torch from where they left off. With the GOP preparing a gerrymander in Florida that could take as many as a dozen seats, blue states like Colorado have a duty to redistrict and level the playing field. They started it and we must finish it if we hope to survive until 2028 with our Democracy intact.
This week Fair Fight Colorado launched the ballot initiative that will give Coloradans a chance to vote on redrawing Colorado's maps -- but our effort will be Dead On Arrival unless we can harness the grassroots energy and enthusiasm this situation calls for.
[Trump is betting that we won't have the guts do this. We have our backs to the wall and we desperately need your support to pull this off. We need as many people giving $25, $10, or whatever amount is meaningful to them, to make redistricting in Colorado a reality:](https://zzejj9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ZayZguF002d89yrqcvE23t1Lxpqk9vWynlouQoUGlYpvvoKo0t12rq8qBSb50MlgSbXDwxB0P1EHJcoNP_x6Mv3KgjLz8xIZnr3wxcLY4B_uzjytSfSrN5qwMUiwi9VpyiyQAq2HOcfScx3YqfUS8SoQMUzhz-rwNhRx8448XLyEeaKGXotv205Y30OA3XXifbPUX5OPDse_HVYXDxwjB4ZlRVnPjFbk&c=jHqCam52iGHra-Db1eT24etZXDc3kapZyxtr8x4lfjKPycT44wmwLQ==&ch=tjLBiq1fjYyiGEXnjLtHZPARnMwL5h3vJEAvT9VUGBhuXlpwWlLCyA==)

They started it and we must finish it if we hope to survive until 2028 with our Democracy intact. | | | | If you believe you've received this email in error, you may unsubscribe below. | | | | | == | Donald Trump's brash decision to rig the maps in 2026 could backfire on him spectacularly. Here's where things stand right now: In November, California, under Gov. Gavin Newsom's leadership successfully passed a redistricting law which passed with 65% of the vote. Then, Virginia Democrats announced a bold plan to redraw their maps and eliminate as many as 4-5 GOP seats. But it's not nearly enough, with all of the red states Trump is gerrymandering, California and Virginia are but a drop in the bucket. It is on us to pick up the torch from where they left off. With the GOP preparing a gerrymander in Florida that could take as many as a dozen seats, blue states like Colorado have a duty to redistrict and level the playing field. They started it and we must finish it if we hope to survive until 2028 with our Democracy intact. This week Fair Fight Colorado launched the ballot initiative that will give Coloradans a chance to vote on redrawing Colorado's maps -- but our effort will be Dead On Arrival unless we can harness the grassroots energy and enthusiasm this situation calls for. == | | | | | == | == | == | == == We simply cannot afford to get squeamish when it comes to pushing back against Trump's effort to rig the maps and steal the 2026 midterms. The stakes are too high. Thank you for rolling up your sleeves and getting involved in the fight. -- Team Fair Fight Colorado | | == | Fair Fight Colorado | 1601 Arapahoe Street FL 10 | Denver, CO 80202 US | | | | | | |

Peter,

Congress is like a battlefield—thrilling, mission-driven, and full of conflict. At least no one's shooting at us here. Well, not with guns, anyway.

The out-of-touch fake news "reporters" at Politico decided they would target the most dangerous men in DC: **Navy SEALs.**

We all ran for office because we were still hungry to protect and fight for our country. The war may be over for us, but we can't abandon the part of our lives that made us who we are. We were part of an elite unit on the front lines, and Congress is somewhere else we can be out front doing something special for our country.

_**Yet the liberal news corporations, who _desperately_ want to keep the status quo, _attacked us_ for wanting to take on the Swamp establishment and business-as-usual going on in our government.**_

All of us –**Navy SEALs Tim Sheehy, Eli Crane, and Ryan Zinke** – are teaming up to stop career politicians and lazy bureaucrats, but we're up against more than just the DC establishment. We're up against the liberal media, RINOs, and revenge-seeking Democrats.

clean_body: They’re targeting the most dangerous men in DC: Navy SEALs. | Peter, Congress is like a battlefield—thrilling, mission-driven, and full of conflict. At least no one's shooting at us here. Well, not with guns, anyway. The out-of-touch fake news "reporters" at Politico decided they would target the most dangerous men in DC: Navy SEALs. We all ran for office because we were still hungry to protect and fight for our country. The war may be over for us, but we can't abandon the part of our lives that made us who we are. We were part of an elite unit on the front lines, and Congress is somewhere else we can be out front doing something special for our country. Yet the liberal news corporations, who desperately want to keep the status quo, attacked us for wanting to take on the Swamp establishment and business-as-usual going on in our government. All of us –Navy SEALs Tim Sheehy, Eli Crane, and Ryan Zinke – are teaming up to stop career politicians and lazy bureaucrats, but we're up against more than just the DC establishment. We're up against the liberal media, RINOs, and revenge-seeking Democrats. We need you, Peter to keep us on the frontlines, bucking the corrupt status quo! -> If the media wants to attack us for having a "warrior mentality" - the mentality to do whatever it takes to achieve a mission... -> If the media wants to attack us for supporting President Trump's MAGA movement - the movement that says we can make America great... -> If the media wants to attack us for taking on the government corruption, fraud, and waste - which has been exposed by Trump's DOGE department... ...Then they don't understand WHY Donald J. Trump and ALL of us were elected! As SEALs, we all shoot straight with each other. We're going to shoot straight with you, too: This hit piece was horsesht and part of a larger conspiracy to silence us. If they can silence us, they can sure as hell silence you. Thank you so much for your time, Tim Sheehy Eli Crane Ryan Zinke Three Navy SEALs Serving in Congress | " "I SERVED AS A NAVY SEAL, BUILT ONE OF THE LARGEST AERIAL FIREFIGHTING COMPANIES IN AMERICA CREATING OVER 200 MONTANA JOBS, AND STARTED A VETERAN-LED CATTLE RANCHING AND FEEDING OPERATION. WHETHER IT WAS IN WAR OR BUSINESS, I SEE PROBLEMS AND SOLVE THEM. AMERICA NEEDS CONSERVATIVE LEADERS WHO LOVE OUR COUNTRY. I'M RUNNING FOR THE U.S. SENATE BECAUSE OUR CAMPAIGN IS ABOUT SERVICE, DUTY, AND COUNTRY -- NOT POLITICS AS USUAL." * | PAID FOR BY TIM SHEEHY FOR MONTANA Use of Tim Sheehy, Eli Crane, and Ryan Zinke's military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.

| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | You’re not alone — but you don’t have to stay stuck. body:
| This year was chaos. Next year is going to be crucial.

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

_It was meant to stay classified - now it’s spreading fast._

**China’s new airborne drone carrier is reportedly entering its final stages, and Taiwan may be the first target.**

Dylan Jovine, CEO & Founder
Behind the Markets
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

clean_body: New footage and military intel confirm what U.S. officials hoped to avoid:| | | | December 05 | | | | | China’s New Sky Weapon Just Shocked the World | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. name: VoteVets
subject: No More Forever Wars
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS
body:
If we don’t stop this now, an entire new generation of Americans in uniform is going to go through the same thing we did.
#
Peter—
For all the breathless talk of Donald Trump as the “President of Peace,” he sure seems to like war.
He’s doing everything he possibly can to start a war with Venezuela.
He’s openly considering sending American troops back to Afghanistan.
He even wants a war in America itself, with endless political deployments of the military against our own people.
Nobody voted for more endless, boundless conflicts like Iraq and Afghanistan — Trump voters included — but that is exactly what Trump’s trying to plunge us into.
If we don’t stop this now, an entire new generation of Americans in uniform is going to go through the same thing we did.
**This entire organization exists to prevent that from happening, and this is what we are demanding of our leaders:**
* End the march toward a costly and unauthorized War of the Americas, and military adventurism worldwide.
* Stop endless political deployments of the military into American cities – now.
* No return of troops to Afghanistan.

If you agree, please: [**Add your name and join 13 other VoteVets members in the House who have already signed on.**](https://act.votevets.org/signup/251205_vv_em_forever_war/?source=em251205-1-160&akid=34806%2E3013755%2EUIYxoA)
| [NO MORE FOREVER WARS](https://act.votevets.org/signup/251205_vv_em_forever_war/?source=em251205-1-160&akid=34806%2E3013755%2EUIYxoA)
---
We hope you’re with us.
The team at VoteVets

name: Troy Downing
subject: This isn’t a drill. They’re coming after MAGA.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Troy Downing for Congress
body:
Peter, do you consider yourself a true Patriot? If you do, I’m BEGGING YOU to read this email...

[](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)Hey you!
Yes, you, Peter.
No, not your laptop. The person looking at it!
I was really hoping to reach you today.
This is Congressman Troy Downing, and I want to thank you for opening my message. Can I borrow just one more minute of your time?
**President Donald J. Trump and House Republicans have accomplished a historic eleven-month run unlike anything in modern American history.**
_🇺🇸 We secured the border 🇺🇸_ _
__🇺🇸 We brought back American jobs 🇺🇸_ _
__🇺🇸 We cut taxes for working Americans 🇺🇸_ _
__🇺🇸 We reduced government waste 🇺🇸_ _
__🇺🇸 We eliminated the woke agenda 🇺🇸_ The tide has turned in our favor.
**_BUT!!! BUT!!! BUT!!!_**
**WE CAN’T REST ON OUR LAURELS NOW!!!**
The Democrats are more desperate than ever.
They’ve doubled down on extremism with their unhinged socialist economic policies, woke DEI agenda, and aggressive climate change plan.
They’ve become a dangerous cabal that **CANNOT BE TRUSTED** with America’s future.
If the pro-Trump GOP can hold the majority through 2028 and solidify America’s Golden Age.
But we need the funds to make it happen NOW!
The further we fall behind the Democrats in fundraising, the closer we get to losing our chance.
**_Peter, do you consider yourself a true Patriot? It’s time to prove it._**
I’m not asking you to risk life and limb for your country (though I think you would). I’m just requesting $17 to lock in a Republican House Majority for two more years. [Is it worth it to you?](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)[CHIP IN $17 RIGHT NOW
](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)–Troy| |

subject: What my union president father taught me

The labor movement built the middle class in this country, and I'm running for State Treasurer to make sure Colorado's government works as hard for working families as they work for themselves. Thank you to the AFL-CIO and every union member fighting for dignity, fair wages, and better working conditions. Your fight is my fight—always has been, always will be. In solidarity, Brianna

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: The Mountain Minute: 12/5/2025
body:
Colorado schools win landmark settlement protecting female athletes
| | | | [](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
---
**PRESENTED BY[ROCKYMOUNTAINVOICE.COM](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
| | Hi friends,
Our coverage of Colorado’s landmark victory for female athletes [caught fire on X](https://link.email.joinrmv.) yesterday—Riley Gaines shared the story with her followers, sending it soaring past 80,000 views. It’s a [huge moment for Colorado girls’ sports](https://link.email.joinrmv.) and a powerful affirmation that fairness and biological reality are winning, even in our very liberal state of Colorado.
Listen to today's Mountain Minute podcast [here](https://link.email.joinrmv.), or watch on [YouTube](https://link.email.joinrmv.) or [Rumble](https://link.email.joinrmv.). Here is a summary of today's top stories:
🏔️ **Here in Colorado...** Colorado politics heated up as Douglas County’s new progressive school board took early, private oaths—removing conservatives ahead of schedule and sparking accusations of a [secret power grab](https://link.email.joinrmv.). Eight districts won a[ landmark settlement protecting female athletes](https://link.email.joinrmv.) by allowing teams and facilities to be designated by biological sex. The PUC advanced rules accelerating a [natural-gas phase-out](https://link.email.joinrmv.), raising fears of higher utility bills. [President Trump blasted Gov. Polis](https://link.email.joinrmv.) for keeping Tina Peters imprisoned, reigniting election-integrity debates. A GOP canvass board member in Arapahoe County [refused to certify results](https://link.email.joinrmv.), though certification proceeded. And a federal judge shielded state health officials from lawsuits over [Colorado’s gas-stove warning-label mandate](https://link.email.joinrmv.).
**🇺🇸 On the National Stage...** Trump [rolled back Biden-era fuel-efficiency mandates](https://link.email.joinrmv.), saying they inflated vehicle costs; automakers hailed the change as restoring affordability and manufacturing strength. Meanwhile, [private-sector payrolls fell by 32,000 jobs](https://link.email.joinrmv.)—driven by small businesses losing 120,000 positions—signaling economic pressure despite overall low layoffs.
**🗣️ Commentary Spotlight...** Pastor Drake urges readers to [ground themselves in real hope](https://link.email.joinrmv.) rather than headline-driven “fake news” as Advent begins. A companion commentary warns that [Dominion’s rebrand as Liberty Vote still leaves major election-integrity concerns](https://link.email.joinrmv.)—pointing to inherited software risks, cloud vulnerabilities, and concentrated control—and calls for independent audits and greater voter oversight heading into 2026.
This is the Mountain Minute for Friday, December 5, 2025.
– Team RMV
---
|
---
**TODAY'S TOP STORIES**
**[Colorado schools win landmark settlement protecting female athletes](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
In a move that reshapes how school athletics operate across the state, School District 49 and seven partner districts have secured a landmark settlement with the Colorado High School Activities Association. The agreement formally protects biological sex policies for sports, locker rooms and overnight travel, and it removes CHSAA from the center of Colorado’s most charged debate over fairness, privacy and Title IX.
The December 4 settlement also allows the districts to continue full participation in sanctioned activities without penalty. District 49 described the outcome as a decisive step in restoring equal opportunity for girls and strengthening the role of local school boards in setting policy.
“CHSAA deserves credit for doing the right thing,” Superintendent Peter Hilts said in a statement Thursday. “By reaching this agreement, they’ve recognized the need to protect fairness in competition and privacy in school facilities for our female athletes. We appreciate their willingness to engage in thoughtful dialogue and reach a resolution that puts students first.”
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
|
---
**[Liberty Vote’s Big Buy: What It Means for Colorado Voters and Why Questions Remain](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY COLORADO INSTITUTE FOR FAIR ELECTIONS, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
* **Dominion rebrands as “Liberty Vote,” but major red flags remain:** Despite marketing the buyout as an all-American fresh start, the new company inherits Dominion’s systems across 60 Colorado counties, raising concerns about whether this is meaningful reform or just a new label on the same opaque machinery.
* **Ownership shift concentrates unprecedented power in one man:** New owner Scott Leiendecker controls voting infrastructure in 27 states despite no engineering background, while his company’s tech history includes ties to foreign-linked software, cloud-based voter data, and systems previously associated with irregularities.
* **Election-integrity experts urge real transparency—not promises:** The article warns that trust won’t be restored without full independent forensic audits of Liberty Vote’s software and processes, pushing Colorado voters to demand verifiable security rather than rely on corporate assurances.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
|

The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!
[DONATE TO ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
|
---
**[The quiet takeover: What early oaths and a Friday ultimatum meant for Douglas County Schools](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY SHAINA COLE, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
* **Progressives seized the board through secret early oaths:** Three incoming Douglas County school board members were sworn in privately—some even on Thanksgiving—days before the public ceremony, effectively ousting conservatives early and triggering accusations of a coordinated, non-transparent power grab.
* **Ultimatum used to force control of the agenda:** Incoming director Susan Meek issued a Friday threat: add her policy reversal to the agenda or the new majority would swear themselves in immediately, a move outgoing leaders called coercive and unethical.
* **Parents worry new majority will roll back protections:** The timing ensured the controversial Healthy Kids Colorado Survey—packed with sensitive questions—would fall under the new board, raising fears they will shift it from parent opt-in to opt-out and weaken parental oversight.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
|
---
**ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️ **
[**Good News or Fake News: Hope — Waiting for the Real News**](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
**BY DRAKE HUNTER | COMMENTARY | DECEMBER 4** _When you look for hope, peace, joy, and love in your relationships and reality during this Advent Season, what source is shaping who you are—the Good News or the fake news? Across Colorado, the nation, and around the world, people start their day scrolling through headlines, alerts, predictions, and debates. The news cycle never stops. It rarely offers hope._ __
[**“The DOJ can take a hike”: Jena Griswold rejects federal demand for voter data**](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
**BY JEN SCHUMANN | STATE | DECEMBER 3** _Colorado Secretary of State Jena Griswold escalated her standoff with the Trump administration this week, rejecting a request for the state’s full, unredacted voter file. “We will not comply with the Trump Department of Justice’s request for Coloradans’ sensitive voting information. The DOJ can take a hike; it does not have a legal right to the information. Colorado will not help Donald Trump undermine our elections and hurt the American people.”_
__
[**Clerks vs. the Constitution: Why the CCCA’s Letter to Polis Gets It Wrong**](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
[****](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**BY A.L. GOODWIN | COMMENTARY | DECEMBER 2** _The Colorado County Clerks Association (CCCA) sent a letter urging Governor Polis to block the potential transfer of Tina Peters to federal custody. That request rests on unconstitutional assumptions and a series of demonstrably false claims—many of which CCCA Director Matt Crane repeated in his November 24, 2025 interview on 710 KNUS, spread across two morning segments — Let My Tina Go! and Should Tina Peters Be Pardoned?_

It’s a and a powerful affirmation that fairness and biological reality are winning, even in our very liberal state of Colorado. Listen to today's Mountain Minute podcast , or watch on or . Here is a summary of today's top stories: 🏔️ Here in Colorado... Colorado politics heated up as Douglas County’s new progressive school board took early, private oaths—removing conservatives ahead of schedule and sparking accusations of a . Eight districts won a by allowing teams and facilities to be designated by biological sex. The PUC advanced rules accelerating a , raising fears of higher utility bills. for keeping Tina Peters imprisoned, reigniting election-integrity debates. A GOP canvass board member in Arapahoe County , though certification proceeded. And a federal judge shielded state health officials from lawsuits over . 🇺🇸 On the National Stage... Trump , saying they inflated vehicle costs; automakers hailed the change as restoring affordability and manufacturing strength. Meanwhile, —driven by small businesses losing 120,000 positions—signaling economic pressure despite overall low layoffs. 🗣️ Commentary Spotlight... Pastor Drake urges readers to rather than headline-driven “fake news” as Advent begins. A companion commentary warns that —pointing to inherited software risks, cloud vulnerabilities, and concentrated control—and calls for independent audits and greater voter oversight heading into 2026. This is the Mountain Minute for Friday, December 5, 2025. – Team RMV | TODAY'S TOP STORIES BY JEN SCHUMANN, In a move that reshapes how school athletics operate across the state, School District 49 and seven partner districts have secured a landmark settlement with the Colorado High School Activities Association. The agreement formally protects biological sex policies for sports, locker rooms and overnight travel, and it removes CHSAA from the center of Colorado’s most charged debate over fairness, privacy and Title IX. The December 4 settlement also allows the districts to continue full participation in sanctioned activities without penalty. District 49 described the outcome as a decisive step in restoring equal opportunity for girls and strengthening the role of local school boards in setting policy. “CHSAA deserves credit for doing the right thing,” Superintendent Peter Hilts said in a statement Thursday. “By reaching this agreement, they’ve recognized the need to protect fairness in competition and privacy in school facilities for our female athletes. We appreciate their willingness to engage in thoughtful dialogue and reach a resolution that puts students first.” | BY COLORADO INSTITUTE FOR FAIR ELECTIONS, Dominion rebrands as “Liberty Vote,” but major red flags remain: Despite marketing the buyout as an all-American fresh start, the new company inherits Dominion’s systems across 60 Colorado counties, raising concerns about whether this is meaningful reform or just a new label on the same opaque machinery. Ownership shift concentrates unprecedented power in one man: New owner Scott Leiendecker controls voting infrastructure in 27 states despite no engineering background, while his company’s tech history includes ties to foreign-linked software, cloud-based voter data, and systems previously associated with irregularities. Election-integrity experts urge real transparency—not promises: The article warns that trust won’t be restored without full independent forensic audits of Liberty Vote’s software and processes, pushing Colorado voters to demand verifiable security rather than rely on corporate assurances. | The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support! | BY SHAINA COLE, Progressives seized the board through secret early oaths: Three incoming Douglas County school board members were sworn in privately—some even on Thanksgiving—days before the public ceremony, effectively ousting conservatives early and triggering accusations of a coordinated, non-transparent power grab. Ultimatum used to force control of the agenda: Incoming director Susan Meek issued a Friday threat: add her policy reversal to the agenda or the new majority would swear themselves in immediately, a move outgoing leaders called coercive and unethical. Parents worry new majority will roll back protections: The timing ensured the controversial Healthy Kids Colorado Survey—packed with sensitive questions—would fall under the new board, raising fears they will shift it from parent opt-in to opt-out and weaken parental oversight. | ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️ BY DRAKE HUNTER | COMMENTARY | DECEMBER 4 When you look for hope, peace, joy, and love in your relationships and reality during this Advent Season, what source is shaping who you are—the Good News or the fake news? Across Colorado, the nation, and around the world, people start their day scrolling through headlines, alerts, predictions, and debates. The news cycle never stops. It rarely offers hope. BY JEN SCHUMANN | STATE | DECEMBER 3 Colorado Secretary of State Jena Griswold escalated her standoff with the Trump administration this week, rejecting a request for the state’s full, unredacted voter file. “We will not comply with the Trump Department of Justice’s request for Coloradans’ sensitive voting information. The DOJ can take a hike; it does not have a legal right to the information. Colorado will not help Donald Trump undermine our elections and hurt the American people.” A.L. GOODWIN | COMMENTARY | DECEMBER 2 The Colorado County Clerks Association (CCCA) sent a letter urging Governor Polis to block the potential transfer of Tina Peters to federal custody. ## The media's latest lie backfires...
The Washington Post set off a firestorm with a [flat-out lie](https://substack.com/redirect/11c51d02-76e9-4087-a059-412387dcb35e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
They claimed that Pete Hegseth was in the room and personally ordered a strike that killed innocent boaters.
It's the latest in a long line of examples of the media just completely fabricating stories
It never made sense to anyone who's ever been part of an actual operation, and now the truth is out.
These mainstream media outlets seem dead set on destroying any credibility they have left.
It's an odd move, but it seems to be their new MO.
Let's break it down...

Fellow Patriot, the solution is simple.

1. Continue Mass Deportations.
2. Pause Immigration.
3. Take Down, Defund, and Investigate Corrupt NGOs that keep the Immigration Industry going.
4. Restore American Sovereignty and the Rule of Law in Dem Enclaves.

Biden and Mayorkas broke our immigration system deliberately. That's why I am calling for a historical precedent to freeze immigration, end abuses, close loopholes and put Americans first.

**No more loopholes, no more magnets.**

For too long, the Swamp has enabled a coordinated push to bring in massive foreign populations and exploit taxpayer resources.

**[Fellow Patriot, if you agree, I need you on my team.](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=a253d34bda7d4c81845d918d092babd2&_e=__2ibYxoC_y0MkJ9kUsFLVDhNdDJ5qHVQ9_32UyBc9MRWIEWbNfCqGjYxuxBMc1JZsHCMilWq0cIjNfKqLcyZpeoeym5g1YidTm0maeZU3hypP1loG1DEUfInSjF5q20JDI9o8wtZOT4RDySsHEiyy1-x-NGV1oI8VK63qpgk5P1e96bFs2xeFD_w8MLacx_iLaLRzbWu4rVZPmieBFc2gg9bZnB-VrgrLz4cWaYpMPagCnjFNqfW8SdISN9h7iTr1lgm-Xw11kMSf-7UGUfukBvgJVCP2qm3wraUN7e8qKw6n4rkxpncgKNtlbhakwYF3kaAo4HN2aJTZeazo3r4UCpXMB3iooCCCJAAwaXwNlHfGAfcHzZJymDcr0b-YX-6tGiw_VKx-apoptisnH3uv8LyiXgKZs56zZ4R9ZX1Mb3m1d8JKeRqsD9QStjFXADf02rxGt89NW2dojNLHxolTbLbcAj2grWH6dZhmpiIZxNQWwpON2RtVD0TYT6bJt_MiZ1AvXxk9EO6IFyXvMaHamclE2Gex7ni5mdRT_mDThIqoNgrlMm89alCwUdywTP7uqDrIHBFXr3gsUouT0W7yUSiZleMNZ7YcrAZBLMtmcYuzX4mKZvS8DoYMpqjY9gmQf8E-KgGw-UC8jy-Eii2b6v8AMwl8vWfFz7cQE6oaM%3D)**

It's time to hit PAUSE and prioritize the American people and Western values.

**Get our House in order and put American citizens _first_.**

Let's **_PAUSE_** immigration to end this crisis.

clean_body: Check this out: | W-O-W Peter, check out this chart from the first hour after launch: = All those dots represent real, grassroots donors from all over who made a contribution to re-elect Governor Tina Kotek. And this is just a snapshot of yesterday’s energy. We couldn’t be more grateful — because this is the momentum we need to keep Tina fighting for Oregon’s working families. This team’s response was nothing short of amazing. Tina is counting on folks like you chipping in $5 here or $10 there to fund her campaign for a better Oregon. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Thanks for all your support, Peter! [**Please, chip in whatever you can right now to help us pull further in front »**](https://links.trueamericanconservatives.com/s/c/9NCAgy81KhKxk-NaiQjUUBx3u1-LxW99beD4lI_kWa8EFP8u4zbomkIldfBdTmGx910zztIfLTXpeYfl_kgdeC6j94lk4pmo0TciWPRqBINpLfWCymGDk5XXXAG3zJOsWBCrnKdfyDa_tJrvzkgFswLwE7ETjmu2NV4NKaRxU0Ndx0eNoeBsqm8fOq7CbHoSIoN8Q3f6xar-uVVmZmeSKb_ynbPmoCozJrJYTlYwRBzj_eE2UsfU4wNz9wADbqwrEcwZ_TrtK8Kt3IkpyqACXp17DXSEmyZU0OSkl-8HRUoDCAbUG5CzcT9fXNx9olYVAiPDI3sMA2PtjMDK3BLduekEzrzO_MeoiSoF9r6eZwZryiG6GlG1zimEGJCLEsUNGSvDSayl_7M_S8GfN7s42AT5cdvofXXVFahjmWmLNx2sd2SWEvFhTYHuZxAIUKUFUyT5lXCEI8OG4zYfur9b73BxZMJ8ZNxwW1qbybQXfGKZpdS2VNRIQapS6w2-9jVROhpf-SCwcFnbUFmPJgsETe3oyhCJqTNwsdzaERvtWO5vty2muUrBjNAEGKTj-vUQl2KirHQeEhmq/T0K61HEiWNeGxkfhO-VLq7v6JUkELS0N/15)

name: Melat Kiros
subject: I want to tell you about my story
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

When people in power build their political careers by taking checks from
anywhere and anyone, you never really know whose interests they’re
serving.

Our current representative, Diana DeGette, has taken contributions from
every corner of Washington’s influence machine, from Big Pharma and Big
Tech to fossil fuel lobbies and corporate defense contractors.

That’s not how you build trust, that’s how you lose it.

My story is different.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251205-mk.iUtotO&refcode=em20251205-37253&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1002&akid=37253%2E1203867%2E2Npel_ ]An old picture of Melat and her family standing in front of a fence.

My family came here from Ethiopia through the Diversity Visa Lottery,
determined to build a better future. In those early years, my parents
worked every job they could find, saved every dollar, and showed me that
nothing matters more than honesty and showing up when it counts.

That’s why I refuse corporate PAC money, want to make sure every dollar
this campaign raises is spent responsibly, and why I’m committed to never
selling out the working families in our district to the same lobbyists my
opponent has spent decades cozying up to.

I’m running this campaign with small donors and the people who live right
here in CO-01 — not lobbyists, not corporations, not AIPAC.

Denver deserves leadership shaped by real struggle, not by decades of
Washington dealmaking. If you believe in a politics rooted in integrity,
not access... in community, not connections... in standing up even when it
costs you something... [ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251205-mk.iUtotO&refcode=em20251205-37253&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1003&akid=37253%2E1203867%2E2Npel_ ]Will you split $5 or whatever you
can to help us keep building a campaign that puts people first?

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251205-mk.iUtotO&refcode=em20251205-37253&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1022&akid=37253%2E1203867%2E2Npel_ ]Contribute $5

Washington has enough politicians who will take money from anyone to stay
in power. Denver deserves someone willing to tell the truth, and someone
you can trust to fight for you.

Thanks for letting me share my story,

Melat

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20251205-37253-footer&t=1023&refcode2=37253_1203867_2Npel_&akid=37253%2E1203867%2E2Npel_

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1024&akid=37253%2E1203867%2E2Npel_

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
JusticeDemocrats.com
1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster, PA 17603
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com
