name: John Padora
subject: While Republicans Consolidate Power, We Build the Firewall They Never Saw Coming
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Padora for Colorado
body:
Republicans just scored a major victory in Texas by pushing through a redistricting map designed to cement their House majority before 2026

| [|
---](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/YxxC5Gk9Lycybs9nzVNeLg~~/AAUS1hA~/D_zgA61RqJHGvPtKeqiaAdTkYJHYD_qloOnEeJWiXMngjDwCeqwfK-sAjXR5g-XXuHD8rFPPewj4xT4jkj97fXMjzmcFYyPq7MhCrHrMMDoIsquPTrGsexZFRwJn4JV6)
| | **Friend,** **Republicans just scored a major victory in Texas by pushing through a redistricting map designed to cement their House majority before 2026.** It is part of a broader national strategy to lock in power and sidestep the will of the voters. But something important is happening across America. **States are fighting back.**
California is challenging GOP power grabs in the courts.
Virginia is moving on reforms.
And here in Colorado, our Democratic candidate for governor has said he supports **emergency redistricting** so our state can join that fight and protect fair representation. This moment is bigger than one map in Texas. It is part of a national struggle over democracy itself. And it is why the firewall we are building in Colorado’s Fourth District matters so much. CO 04 is not considered a swing district, and the political establishment assumes it is safely Republican. They think Lauren Boebert can parachute in, stir up chaos, divide communities, and coast to reelection. **But they are wrong.** While Republicans manipulate districts to secure their power, **we are building a grassroots coalition they never expected**. Ranchers, teachers, veterans, young families, working people, and independents who are tired of being ignored are showing up in ways that surprise even seasoned operatives. **If Democrats want to stop extremism and hold the House, we cannot only play defense in the usual places. We need new battlegrounds. We need breakthroughs in districts like CO 04.** And here is the truth behind why this matters. **Millions of us across this country are standing up to authoritarianism and fighting back against Project 2026.** I have been in the streets all year with people from every corner of Colorado resisting racially driven partisan gerrymanders, standing up to ICE overreach, calling out the weaponization of our justice system, and refusing to stay silent while the Supreme Court bends to political pressure. **Our democracy and our institutions are under siege.**
This is not politics as usual.
This is a fight for the future of the American experiment. And we need leaders who will not just step into the trenches but know how to fight because they have already been doing so for years. That is what this movement represents. Our future is too precious to hand over to extremists. We are patriots who love this country.
We are patriots who despise fascism, aspiring dictatorship, and the people like Lauren Boebert who enable it. But to stay competitive and keep building this movement, I need to be honest. **We are falling behind financially.**
We still have a long way to go to remain viable before the end of the year, and the resources required to challenge Boebert in a district like this are significant. January 1st marks the start of election season, and every campaign will surge forward with massive spending and national backing. That is not the reality of our race. I am not taking corporate PAC money. I am not taking AIPAC money. Corporate mega donors are not bankrolling this campaign. There are no dark money super PACs doing the heavy lifting. **This campaign is powered entirely by people.**
That is what makes it honest.
That is what makes it strong.
And that is what will make it historic if we win. Together we can catch the political establishment off guard and prove that power does not belong to those who redraw maps in the dark. It belongs to the people willing to stand up and build something stronger. **[Chip in today so we can stay competitive, grow this grassroots movement, and keep building the firewall Colorado’s 4th District needs to stop extremism in its tracks.](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/u1rk6yzT1gOZ0kTS5XKaSA~~/AAUS1hA~/urAOM-mOk6_7USt3yV8WM8KKFfTb8u6N2D297RUGuBpnPf9oIdREAhpL2BTYVK7ohluhTfSa4HRfq0Gpz6k1ZysQfj0q4ezlFqJRXk_pOSkzm0gcyXgJRKxSTz7Ut0NNy-0IlWNET8djOdtZtWWvc0OnG1e-fhIqmt5ZeFEzpQY~)** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | [Chip in $10 now >>](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/g2eJbDfII57WklNmprqCzQ~~/AAUS1hA~/v6jh_JzT1vvGan6Vur_gUo1xXjkRDrPwULBDkwcpZEnocjTjhYVxnFDTDe5dx0KySFYwCQK6iIZ9jAj3IJIUd4ojbqFqUF7rM8BP_JUH5ZrzjOOInMaqhlmMw1zL6FtVbqziVWVS8JFyhxoXb7McUJceWopF0oG5QVwvkFlCwd9pDIE7DkSYrQRxZSwDIbt1) | [Chip in $25 now >>](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/Xcwf218lvYEbDwYnRUn3Yw~~/AAUS1hA~/v6jh_JzT1vvGan6Vur_gUo1xXjkRDrPwULBDkwcpZEnocjTjhYVxnFDTDe5dx0KySFYwCQK6iIZ9jAj3IJIUd4ojbqFqUF7rM8BP_JUH5ZpYcJLbuKaW4OOaZ3nl7JOvvliqg6I44u2F28gjiTJZqCZ0K0JEM-j724ZpEnLmGyZSsosImWB7-ITtHf4b_r75)

name: Kristen McDonald Rivet
subject: Childcare costs are too high – here’s what we’re doing.
body:
|
#
Friend, Every day, I hear from parents that childcare is becoming nearly impossible to afford. Costs keep climbing – and too many families are being forced to make impossible decisions about work, finances, and their children’s wellbeing. That’s why I introduced the **Early Education Savings Program Act**. Our bipartisan bill expands the original 529 savings program – a tax-free investment account originally created to help families save for higher education – so parents can also use it for childcare expenses. | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120701102/587086510/-1307106724?refcode=em-12.05.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjAyNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOGE4Njc2MC0xZGQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Zu1FSfrrIQ4DsqWphAyx88ABY9n8qaRL_PshM_mc8iM=)
**Lawmakers introduce bill aimed to make childcare more affordable**
_“Expanding 529s to cover childcare is an easy and important step that will help families save for and afford childcare for their young kids.” said McDonald Rivet._
---
Working parents know the mental gymnastics of deciding whether or not they can leave work to pick up their kids – or if they can afford care if they can’t get away from the office.
Joseph and I raised six kids. We know firsthand just how expensive childcare can be. It pushes your budget to the breaking point, and with living costs higher than ever, **parents should not have to break the bank to afford childcare.**
I’ve spent my career working to expand access to childcare and early childhood education programs across mid-Michigan, and I plan to keep working towards a more equitable future for everyone.
I promise to keep fighting for affordable, high-quality childcare so that every family – no matter their income or circumstances – has the resources and support they need to succeed.
**[If we want to keep delivering solutions like this for mid-Michigan families, we must hold this frontline seat. Your contribution of $3 today will help us protect MI-08 and keep fighting for the policies working families deserve.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120701104/587086522/-185804085?refcode=em-12.05.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjAyNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOGE4Njc2MC0xZGQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Zu1FSfrrIQ4DsqWphAyx88ABY9n8qaRL_PshM_mc8iM=)**

clean_body: | Friend, Every day, I hear from parents that childcare is becoming nearly impossible to afford. Costs keep climbing – and too many families are being forced to make impossible decisions about work, finances, and their children’s wellbeing. That’s why I introduced the Early Education Savings Program Act. Our bipartisan bill expands the original 529 savings program – a tax-free investment account originally created to help families save for higher education – so parents can also use it for childcare expenses. | = Lawmakers introduce bill aimed to make childcare more affordable “Expanding 529s to cover childcare is an easy and important step that will help families save for and afford childcare for their young kids.” said McDonald Rivet. Working parents know the mental gymnastics of deciding whether or not they can leave work to pick up their kids – or if they can afford care if they can’t get away from the office. Joseph and I raised six kids. We know firsthand just how expensive childcare can be. It pushes your budget to the breaking point, and with living costs higher than ever, parents should not have to break the bank to afford childcare. I’ve spent my career working to expand access to childcare and early childhood education programs across mid-Michigan, and I plan to keep working towards a more equitable future for everyone. I promise to keep fighting for affordable, high-quality childcare so that every family – no matter their income or circumstances – has the resources and support they need to succeed. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = More soon, Kristen | We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to . Thank you for supporting Kristen McDonald Rivet for Congress! Kristen for Michigan P.O. Box 854 Bay City, MI 48707 United States

## Dance Night Brings Dads, Daughters Together
At Louisiana’s largest maximum-security prison, nearly 30 inmates got a rare chance to reconnect with their daughters during the facility’s [first-ever father-daughter dance](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykULuOgzAQ_BroiLyLDaFwcU26XHslWswa9mRsZJxE-fsT-YXr5iGNZubnPt5JgsTlmza2fvFj8WmM_DoCl8J53Li8Vgks0aWNJpre6dQpjqjQNICNMuNMEt6jqWfrmZHbmi30nR66QWFfr5apnc2sgZx3E6B3nWl7UAhX1aOmrhZ7pgEqgwAK9UXRgANNA2lQBieutOJZSsqX3ySxrOzDOz05X1za6mDXUvajar8qvFV4o_0ccFonyUVc4ApvIT3kEIrU7FmOFBtPZeXczPRY1vIB0XGj2k5PBtsrDEqzBn0Fci0SqMEDEdXZzvtLQpCj6yutlo0kfHqUfx_4tPgXAAD__16HhSg). Held at the Louisiana State Penitentiary in Angola, the event gave long-incarcerated men, many serving decades-long sentences, a moment to simply be dads again. The dance, organized by God Behind Bars, featured tuxes, roses, and tearful embraces as fathers slow danced with daughters many hadn’t seen in years. [Watch a video here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFuwyAUheGnwVssuAY7DAyVqmzp2hFd4BITYRM5JNRvX7mv0PU_0jec76u9YsppvX3hQibeoq2x2JXaM1OttNmFaptTprT6sqBDt5ej42qBgzoJOHFlA6a8W9UFE4mAho6MmEapR81h6mbjZBR4DiTOoAYYgxuCdl55yckRx7FL5tAEcAVCcJA9Rw0anUYpuAJHTHIKqZatv5e01pli3subtt6XpctmrvXxZMMHgwuDS2ut38urvhwd-1Gw-pkNlzcbPl153H9INj-VbjPh0VLO6TlOTPLbgin_kfXfX7wN_AYAAP__tnd1Nw).
## Coma Patients Reunite, Get Engaged
Seven years after [suffering separate brain injuries](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkbuupDAMQL8GOo8STwKTgmIburvtlsiAA1nlMQrWjebvV9xf2MrSsXSkY__5Wr4oxJCP35R48odfxJclc7sii3BdEks7Q-SQt5JopfVTbk55QYUWNIKyy04hfhbb75NnRn72POlxMG5wCsf-nF6W7cspt5I3g9435548vF7e2A2fNHAfptumUVnUWqF5KHLoaHVktLK4cmcU70FKffwtIcvJPn7KN9fHVlIfp1PkfXXPXx3OHc6ttUeT82fZ4UxVwha5w_nuugdJKBlaqXHvcBbmfEE7CyQWCBnOcr2DUATywhU4cT1CPsDXkuA-wwVUGQ4WuXGiWgPfJqtGIKXdsK8aNG1PMKNdwY3ogTw7TdobM-i-Tvu7hRjDNYydUUeiEH9S5L9_8D3hvwAAAP__1AmckA), Zach Zarembinski and Isabelle Richard got engaged in the same St. Paul hospital where they first crossed paths as coma patients. Zach was injured playing high school football; Isabelle was in a car crash days later. They met when Zach, newly awake, offered hope to Isabelle’s family as she lay unconscious nearby. [Their moms kept in touch](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGSnDAQRL8GMlFigOUICJxsdk4dUgMawfgkDSXGq9q_d2F_wqXd1V3d79fn8okcOO0_MdLsd7-olyVRuQKpUl4iaTk4EKdNIq64vuXWMS1gYTAtGDssDjm8l6F2sycC6mqa2_HRT4_Jwlgfs-vWntzoV0Dw7TbZjlYcPx52HLrNuo-a57utBTtA21roG4sTTLhO2Ld2gJWq3pJjldz8Fk56kA9veVFuNol1mA_V86q6HxU8K3iWUppdxN03EmmR_NVI3it4ahFzkpyBjM8STeSU6BJFUw4xkdRwMnqQOeQ6WTEY9ErZFPzitJs_5__YjeIymMnspHo7EXNmchU86zy7s3AIfD3Gqrd7RA7_Zuq3-b5m-BsAAP__q4uVog), and years later, a reunion sparked a romance. This month, Zach proposed in the Regions Hospital lobby, where it all began. Both had parts of their skulls removed—on opposite sides. _“So it’s kind of like we complement each other,”_ Isabelle said. [Watch a video report](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGO6yAQheGnwV2sYQD7uqCIdJVU2WKbLdEYDzErbCKHBPntV95X2PY_0lecr5u7UUxxvX_Qwjbcgyshu5XrM3EpvLmFS51j4rj6vNBI456PTqtDQHOSeALjJoppd6aZbGBGVg1b2Xd66AbAvpktq3-99DooxBGlQeh60j2wN2RYKtVEe2gSwaCUgLoFGnCgcSAtweDIQgNPseSt_c5xLTOHtOc3b63PS5PsXMrjKdRZ4EXgpdba7vlVXiMf-1Go-Fmoy1uo_5_nqz5B152vvtns9KgxpfjseqHhvlBMv2T58xdviz8BAAD__xlEcpk).
## Couple Weds Where Love Began—In the Pool
Edward LaRue, 82, and Marlene Parsons, 78, [tied the knot](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGunTAQRL8Gur2yFxsuhYs0t3tpU6LFLLCRjZHtPMTfR-QX0s6MRkfn19f0RRLk2H5SZLdu61TXNB18lcC1cp4i12uXwHL4FGmm-U5PTseECi1oBGWnhSTck20XtzIjdy07PfRm7EeFQ7s77b0na7B7-_k9W99rMjzqYdW2Mz0OrbjnTaOyqLVC81I04kjzSEYrizM3RvEiNeXX7yRH3XkNd_rm_PIptsHttZ6l6X40-Gnwc3I6Az9Vg5_Ch6QMPv05A8PGtUCknIUXkAPu6AnOlAJcO2eGuvMNq-RSIXJ9FhdVzkCc0yy-gA9UCmj9tmrQXZvdcl4SgpR-aIzaIkn4x1T_W-a3w78BAAD__xZxjCM) last month in the same YMCA pool in Maryville, Ill., where they first met during a water aerobics class. Wearing water-friendly wedding gear—trunks and a top hat for him, a white suit and veil for her—they exchanged vows with friends, family, and a lifeguard looking on. The Y’s aquatics director, newly licensed to officiate, [led the ceremony](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDGOpTAQRE9jMiO7MbAOHGxC9jfdEDW4AY9sjEzPR9x-xL_CJBWUSq9K9f81vjDEsK__MJFb1mXkJY87XWckZipjIr62ECnsc0444XTnx8d9BAWt1CBVO3oM8R7byruFCKipyOm-M7azCvpqc38AfG-0NcrP0MwWm75dOuPt3CpreqiCe2gaVAtaKzC1QgsWJ4tGqxYmEkaRD5xL_ZXDzhst8c5vKvWcUxXdxnycovkrYBAwXNdVL6GcjJEKmycjYHgaBAxaP2IEDHP-PiLJlfiUCUsJ5OWdZpRHzlFeGxWSvNEtPyyZiAUMVXH-uEKM4ex6YdSaMMTPCv71fW8HPwEAAP__eP-ElQ). Both had lost spouses six years earlier and weren’t sure they’d marry again. But after Edward proposed last year, Marlene eventually said yes. _“We splashed each other in the pool,”_ she said of their early flirtations. _“It was great,”_ she said of their big day. [Watch a video report](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykj71u6yAYQJ8Gtljw8XcZGO7QDJXSoanUEUH4sKmwSR0Sy29fpa_Q9UjnSOfz5E-h1LKMb2FGl8fse25-we1WsXdc_Yx9m0rFslzaHGKIe3vysHhgoA4cDkz5FErdvaLJZURAQdFxo6XVloGhk0ORFRdohNZRaW5zViwJI4U16V_UQIt71jgwBZwzkAMLFmyINkjOFEQkkmEqva3DVytLnzDXvT1wHS5tptVNvV9vRPwncCRw3Nu934eIBI7n9_vrefzeP14YXV26bqXWctOGSDbOodRfv_95_OHgJwAA__9Xim00).

clean_body: Seven years after , Zach Zarembinski and Isabelle Richard got engaged in the same St. Paul hospital where they first crossed paths as coma patients. | | | | | | Friday, December 5, 2025 ---|--- Good Afternoon! In this week's Feel Good Flyover , we're highlighting a Delta captain who kept a childhood promise by as a thank you for believing in him long before the dream seemed possible. It got us thinking: Has anyone ever done something so extraordinary for you that it changed your life? Don't miss Amy Hess on the , where she talks with Chrissy Joy, the trainer behind some of Hollywood's most remarkable canine performers. This interview is exclusively available on the podcast, so . **You may have seen pop up lately. There's a reason: 1.5M+ Christians use it to stay grounded when the news feels overwhelming. Clear updates. Unbiased news. Biblical reminders that point you back to Christ. . | Dance Night Brings Dads, Daughters Together At Louisiana’s largest maximum-security prison, nearly 30 inmates got a rare chance to reconnect with their daughters during the facility’s . Held at the Louisiana State Penitentiary in Angola, the event gave long-incarcerated men, many serving decades-long sentences, a moment to simply be dads again. The dance, organized by God Behind Bars, featured tuxes, roses, and tearful embraces as fathers slow danced with daughters many hadn’t seen in years. . Coma Patients Reunite, Get Engaged Seven years after , Zach Zarembinski and Isabelle Richard got engaged in the same St. Paul hospital where they first crossed paths as coma patients. Zach was injured playing high school football; Isabelle was in a car crash days later. They met when Zach, newly awake, offered hope to Isabelle’s family as she lay unconscious nearby. , and years later, a reunion sparked a romance. This month, Zach proposed in the Regions Hospital lobby, where it all began. Both had parts of their skulls removed—on opposite sides. “So it’s kind of like we complement each other,” Isabelle said. . Couple Weds Where Love Began—In the Pool Edward LaRue, 82, and Marlene Parsons, 78, last month in the same YMCA pool in Maryville, Ill., where they first met during a water aerobics class. Wearing water-friendly wedding gear—trunks and a top hat for him, a white suit and veil for her—they exchanged vows with friends, family, and a lifeguard looking on. The Y’s aquatics director, newly licensed to officiate, . Both had lost spouses six years earlier and weren’t sure they’d marry again. But after Edward proposed last year, Marlene eventually said yes. “We splashed each other in the pool,” she said of their early flirtations. “It was great,” she said of their big day. . | The following story is featured exclusively on The Feel Good Flyover Podcast. Clicking the link will take you directly to this story: ➤ Amy Hess spotlights the trainer behind Hollywood's most talented dogs, and how she's using their talent to spread joy. () | Flying together with our sponsor We all know the news can pull your heart in the wrong direction, fast. does the opposite—with clear headlines, no spin, and Christ-centered reminders that keep you steady when everything else feels loud. In just five minutes, you can stay informed without losing hope. 1.5M+ Christians already trust it. What are you waiting for? . | ➤ A Delta Air Lines captain fulfilled a childhood promise by surprising his grandfather as the pilot of his flight. The moment proved that the flight simulator his grandfather couldn't really afford to buy had finally paid off. () ➤ Readers of Woman's Weekly magazine knitted more than 46,000 woolen mice for rescue cats in shelters, setting a Guinness World Record. The handmade toys, distributed to over 300 U.K. rescue centers, provide comfort and stimulation for thousands of kitties awaiting families. () ➤ Moments after her wedding, an off-duty nurse stopped to help a man critically injured in a car crash in New Orleans. Still in her wedding dress, she assessed his injuries and stabilized him until paramedics arrived. () ➤ A veteran's backyard had been overgrown with weeds for four years until a property cleaning company cleared it for free and then surprised him with $14,000 and a fully insured car. () ➤ When a Swedish kayaker found orphaned baby otter Leya, he nursed her back to health and released her into the wild. She now jumps into his yellow kayak whenever she sees him on the lake for visits. () ➤ Rejecting traditional mountain shelters that clash with nature, designers created a must-see glass bivouac that camouflages into Alpine rocks. () Flying together with our sponsor ➤ Upgrade your kitchen with this ultra-durable , designed to resist scratches, odors, and bacteria. Its sleek, non-porous surface protects your knives while offering unbeatable stability for everyday prep. Easy to clean and built to last, it’s the premium board serious home cooks swear by. () | Fitz, a snow-loving cat from Ontario, enjoys sleigh rides and hikes in winter gear. video via The Dodo/Fitzcapades - YouTube | What five-letter word becomes shorter when you add two letters to it? | Flying together with our sponsor You already have enough things stressing you out this holiday season. The news shouldn’t be one of them. gives you the headlines without the chaos and pairs them with Christ-first perspectives, reminding you of what is most important this holiday season. body:
✈️ Hitting the road.
#

If flipping the House and Senate in the next election and putting a check on Trump's power is important to you, then I am asking you to make a contribution to my campaign before the next FEC deadline ends?
Why me?
Here's why.
During our Senate campaign, a good number of folks came to Arizona to support my election. It made a difference -- they could help build turnout, register voters, or even hold events without me while I was in other parts of the state.
So, from today through our FEC deadline on New Years Eve, I’m going to be sending you a few more emails than normal.
Now it's my turn to do the same.
As you know, I am a Marine combat veteran elected to the Senate from Arizona in the same election Donald Trump won the state. Lots of Latino voters, conservative veteran voters, and independents checked his name and then on the next line they checked mine.
In other words, I got many of the voters we need to win in order to flip close House and Senate races in the next election.
That means I'll be on the road ... a lot. And it means I'll need the resources to make it happen.
[**So like I said, if flipping the House, the Senate, and putting a check on Trump's power during his final two years in office is important to you — please make a contribution of $5 or any amount you can afford today.**](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1000?c0=ruben-em-dec25.cqctgP&refcode=em251205-1-160-tf&t=1&akid=4045%2E734936%2Ebft-jN)

Friend ,
This isn’t about a photo or a dinner.
This is about something historic, and I want you to be a part of it! I’m serious.
[JOIN MAGA HALL OF FAME](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)

This will be the permanent home honouring the greatest patriots who stood with me when the Extreme Left, the Fake News, and the Deep State tried to destroy our movement.
**I’m personally selecting the first 500 Founding Members, and I want YOU!**
**These 500 spots will NEVER be offered again.**

The Radical Left is spending hundreds of millions to tear down everything we built. They want to erase our victories. They want to rewrite history so our movement never happened.

**_[Contribute any amount right now and lock it in>>](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)_**

Team, it’s Andrea. I’m sounding the alarm on Trump’s billionaire tax giveaway in the media, online, and at events across my district. And in the midst of the GOP’s destruction, I’m still working to deliver meaningful, positive change for Oregon’s working families. **[But National Republicans know I’ve been a thorn in their side.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120702119/587089831/318555295?amount=6&refcode=email_120525_beforeweekend&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1NTU1ZDk3OC0xZmQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GzmMBYr21jSlhlw1eKZzjKlkTHXpZrbOYQcwsjUpl9Q=) **They’ve come within single digits of flipping OR-06 in two straight elections, and we know they won’t hesitate for a moment to flood this district with dark-money attack ads to replace me with a MAGA rubber stamp. Your donation will help ensure my campaign is ready to respond to any attacks coming our way. **[That’s why I’m coming to this grassroots team today: So, Team are you with me? Please chip in $6 or whatever you can to keep OR-06 blue and help me hold the line in Congress as I fight for Oregon’s working families. →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120702120/587089832/318555295?amount=6&refcode=email_120525_beforeweekend&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1NTU1ZDk3OC0xZmQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GzmMBYr21jSlhlw1eKZzjKlkTHXpZrbOYQcwsjUpl9Q=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | | [Donate $6 →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120702121/587089833/1477919608?express_lane=true&amounts=6,15,25,50,100&amount=6&refcode=email_120525_beforeweekend&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1NTU1ZDk3OC0xZmQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GzmMBYr21jSlhlw1eKZzjKlkTHXpZrbOYQcwsjUpl9Q=)

name: Movement 4 Freedom
subject: December Monthly Meeting: You’re invited!
body:
Join us Dec. 18th

| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/b2dc6d8aa179/tockcw1cwy-215?e=81ab29c4d2)
---
[|
---](https://movement4freedom.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c20cdecc59106d39c65725a01&id=70f7edba7e&e=81ab29c4d2)
| | | | | | Peter,
---
| | We’re closing out the year with a powerful lineup, and you’re invited to join us!
On **Thursday, December 18th at 8PM EST,** the Movement for Freedom is hosting our Monthly Members & Supporters Meeting, where we bring our community together to connect, organize, and stay grounded in the work ahead.This month’s call is one you won’t want to miss.
---
| | | | | | | | | | | [RSVP TODAY](https://movement4freedom.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c20cdecc59106d39c65725a01&id=d03bd48986&e=81ab29c4d2)
---
| | **📣 Candidate Spotlight: Hartzell Gray** We’re proud to share that Hartzell Gray, our newly endorsed candidate for Missouri’s 4th District, will join us. Hartzell is a lifelong organizer who fights for his community with the same conviction he brings to his campaign — for living wages, Medicare for All, housing as a human right, LGBTQIA+ rights, and a future where working people come first. Come hear directly from him about building people-powered change and what’s next for his campaign.
**🤝 Partner Highlight: Youth Demand DC** We’re also partnering with Youth Demand DC, a dynamic youth-led organization pushing for justice, accountability, and an end to the war on Palestinians. They’ll share updates on their ongoing campaigns, and how our community can plug in.This movement is strongest when we work together. So join us! Your voice and presence matter.
👉 [RSVP now for the December 18th call at 8PM ET.](https://movement4freedom.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c20cdecc59106d39c65725a01&id=b063b5099f&e=81ab29c4d2)
Let’s close out the year rooted in community, clarity, and action.
---
| |
_**The Movement for Freedom Team**_
---
|
---
|
---

---](https://movement4freedom.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c20cdecc59106d39c65725a01&id=680aaad9ff&e=81ab29c4d2)

clean_body: Join us Dec. 18th | | | | | | | | | | | | | Peter, | | We’re closing out the year with a powerful lineup, and you’re invited to join us! On Thursday, December 18th at 8PM EST, the Movement for Freedom is hosting our Monthly Members & Supporters Meeting, where we bring our community together to connect, organize, and stay grounded in the work ahead.This month’s call is one you won’t want to miss. | | | | | | | | | | | | | 📣 Candidate Spotlight: Hartzell Gray We’re proud to share that Hartzell Gray, our newly endorsed candidate for Missouri’s 4th District, will join us. Hartzell is a lifelong organizer who fights for his community with the same conviction he brings to his campaign — for living wages, Medicare for All, housing as a human right, LGBTQIA+ rights, and a future where working people come first. Come hear directly from him about building people-powered change and what’s next for his campaign. 🤝 Partner Highlight: Youth Demand DC We’re also partnering with Youth Demand DC, a dynamic youth-led organization pushing for justice, accountability, and an end to the war on Palestinians. They’ll share updates on their ongoing campaigns, and how our community can plug in.This movement is strongest when we work together. So join us! Your voice and presence matter. 👉 Let’s close out the year rooted in community, clarity, and action. | | The Movement for Freedom Team | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright (C) 2025 Movement 4 Freedom. All rights reserved.

Welcome to the Indiana Democratic Party's weekly newsletter, The Kernel of Truth. The State Party will provide the most important facts - a kernel of truth - from the week. _[If you were forwarded this newsletter, you can sign up to receive the Kernel every week by clicking here.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120702358/587090244/1416943042?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmZGExZTMwMC0yMGQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=oydPnZbzmAADZXyGfB5spCGJyEI2RRL6wAW0xgVYEIE=&emci=7d22aaf7-1dd2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=fda1e300-20d2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=4925535)_ [_**If you'd like to donate to the INDems' Reorganization Fund, you can do so by clicking here.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120702362/587090248/1764064610?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmZGExZTMwMC0yMGQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=oydPnZbzmAADZXyGfB5spCGJyEI2RRL6wAW0xgVYEIE=) ****
_Republican Supermajority in State House of Representatives Passes Unfair Maps in Near Party Line Vote_ [__](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120702365/587090251/1061437565?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmZGExZTMwMC0yMGQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=oydPnZbzmAADZXyGfB5spCGJyEI2RRL6wAW0xgVYEIE=&emci=7d22aaf7-1dd2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=fda1e300-20d2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=4925535) On Friday, the Republican supermajority in the State House of Representatives voted by a near party line margin to advance House Bill 1032, which redraws Indiana’s congressional districts to try and achieve a “9-0 Republican” map in the 2026 elections. Democrats stood strong, with every Democratic member of the chamber voting NO. Now, the bill goes to the State Senate, with the fate of the new maps on the line. Instead of focusing on affordability, childcare, healthcare, education, or any of the issues impacting Hoosiers, Republicans have chosen to waste taxpayer dollars on a partisan sideshow. Our message is to keep calling your State Senators and tell them that you oppose redistricting by calling **(317) 232-9400**. ****_New Maps Split Communities of Interest and Hoosier Neighborhoods With One Goal in Mind: Eliminate Democratic Representation in Indiana_
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120702369/587090255/245729568?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmZGExZTMwMC0yMGQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=oydPnZbzmAADZXyGfB5spCGJyEI2RRL6wAW0xgVYEIE=&emci=7d22aaf7-1dd2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=fda1e300-20d2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=4925535) Republicans in the Statehouse have wasted over four months on the partisan sideshow of redistricting. In order to achieve their goals, they’ve had to suggest a map that splits communities of interest all across Indiana. The new maps would connect Gary to Wabash, split Indianapolis in four, and split Valparaiso and Muncie in half. A short drive on Meridian Street from downtown Indianapolis to Carmel would pass through five of Indiana’s nine congressional districts. Throughout this process, Democrats proposed multiple pieces of legislation to rein in utility costs, healthcare costs, and make life more affordable for Hoosiers. Republicans rejected them because they are scared of losing their majority in Congress, and know they must rig the maps to stay in power. On top of it, while hundreds of Hoosiers came to the Statehouse on Monday to oppose these maps, only a few dozen showed up to a rally in support on Friday. ****_Each week, the State Party will highlight key policy from Democrats at the Statehouse or across the state. Democrats deliver a better future for Hoosier families, and we urge you to share this information with your networks._ [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120702373/587090259/1465606364?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmZGExZTMwMC0yMGQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=oydPnZbzmAADZXyGfB5spCGJyEI2RRL6wAW0xgVYEIE=&emci=7d22aaf7-1dd2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=fda1e300-20d2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=4925535) We are highlighting the work this week of U.S. Rep. André Carson of Indianapolis who took over as the leading Democrat on the Transportation and Infrastructure’s Subcommittee on Aviation. Rep. Carson has brought back over $1 billion for Indiana projects from Washington D.C. throughout his time in Congress, and his goals on this subcommittee include increasing passenger safety, securing jobs, and strengthening our economy. While Republicans try to split his district into four pieces, Rep. Carson is hard at work like he always has been to reach across the aisle and bring home more dollars and projects for Hoosiers. **[Click here to see other opportunities and party events near you on Mobilize!](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120702376/587090262/1316591425?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmZGExZTMwMC0yMGQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=oydPnZbzmAADZXyGfB5spCGJyEI2RRL6wAW0xgVYEIE=&emci=7d22aaf7-1dd2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=fda1e300-20d2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=4925535)** clean_body: | Welcome to the Indiana Democratic Party's weekly newsletter, The Kernel of Truth. The State Party will provide the most important facts - a kernel of truth - from the week. = ** Republican Supermajority in State House of Representatives Passes Unfair Maps in Near Party Line Vote On Friday, the Republican supermajority in the State House of Representatives voted by a near party line margin to advance House Bill 1032, which redraws Indiana’s congressional districts to try and achieve a “9-0 Republican” map in the 2026 elections. Democrats stood strong, with every Democratic member of the chamber voting NO. Now, the bill goes to the State Senate, with the fate of the new maps on the line. Instead of focusing on affordability, childcare, healthcare, education, or any of the issues impacting Hoosiers, Republicans have chosen to waste taxpayer dollars on a partisan sideshow. Our message is to keep calling your State Senators and tell them that you oppose redistricting by calling (317) 232-9400. New Maps Split Communities of Interest and Hoosier Neighborhoods With One Goal in Mind: Eliminate Democratic Representation in Indiana Republicans in the Statehouse have wasted over four months on the partisan sideshow of redistricting. In order to achieve their goals, they’ve had to suggest a map that splits communities of interest all across Indiana. The new maps would connect Gary to Wabash, split Indianapolis in four, and split Valparaiso and Muncie in half. A short drive on Meridian Street from downtown Indianapolis to Carmel would pass through five of Indiana’s nine congressional districts. Throughout this process, Democrats proposed multiple pieces of legislation to rein in utility costs, healthcare costs, and make life more affordable for Hoosiers. Republicans rejected them because they are scared of losing their majority in Congress, and know they must rig the maps to stay in power. On top of it, while hundreds of Hoosiers came to the Statehouse on Monday to oppose these maps, only a few dozen showed up to a rally in support on Friday. Each week, the State Party will highlight key policy from Democrats at the Statehouse or across the state. Democrats deliver a better future for Hoosier families, and we urge you to share this information with your networks. We are highlighting the work this week of U.S. Rep. André Carson of Indianapolis who took over as the leading Democrat on the Transportation and Infrastructure’s Subcommittee on Aviation. Rep. Carson has brought back over $1 billion for Indiana projects from Washington D.C. throughout his time in Congress, and his goals on this subcommittee include increasing passenger safety, securing jobs, and strengthening our economy. While Republicans try to split his district into four pieces, Rep. Carson is hard at work like he always has been to reach across the aisle and bring home more dollars and projects for Hoosiers. Information on County Party events, Town Halls, protests, volunteer opportunities, and more can be found on our Save Your Spot at This Year’s Holiday Party = This year’s Holiday Party is TONIGHT. Please join us for an exciting night of fellowship with Hoosier Democrats in Downtown Indianapolis. = Upcoming Candidate Trainings Join the National Democratic Training Committee on Wednesday, December 10th from 1-2 pm ET for a free virtual live training on What is a Democratic Precinct Chair. INDems IN the News IndyStar: WTHR: Indiana Capital Chronicle: * Indiana Capital Chronicle: [content goes here] 101 W Washington St., #1110E Indianapolis, IN 46204 Paid for by the Indiana Democratic Party, www.indems.org. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.

name: Frank Mrvan
subject: Hold the line
body:
Right now, this is the most important district in the entire country in the battle for the House majority.

Take a look: [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120703229/587092043/247673694?refcode=251205_fm_mrvan_img2&ms=251205_fm_mrvan&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTM1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMmUwNDY5MS0yMWQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hJvHJDn1pOknbCiCCetp_t0AP9_F_o5L_fPmq_PKOsA=) N/A, I wish I could tell you exactly what will happen next. The truth is, right now, it feels like a coin flip. I can’t say with certainty which way things will go, but I do know this: **every bit of support we can rally in the next crucial days will make a real, measurable difference for Indiana.** We’ve already seen the power of this team. Thanks to your support, we’ve been able to put significant pressure on the GOP’s special session, slow their momentum, and make our voices heard. **But this fight is far from over.** The coming days are critical, and the stakes couldn’t be higher. disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scholten for Congress
body:
I didn’t run for Congress to raise money. I ran to deliver results for West Michigan, protect our freedoms, and make government work for the people again.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120703251/587092096/32658279?refcode=BLU_EM_HSC_2025-12-05_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTY0MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwYmUwNDY5MS0yMWQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=u1wn5K9Id57VmPRL4WRNlhuFeHyq-t7TkCJlWFil3N0=)

| Hey there, it’s Hillary.

My least favorite part of campaigning is asking for money.

I didn’t run for Congress to raise money. I ran to deliver results for West Michigan, protect our freedoms, and make government work for the people again. But the reality is, running a competitive campaign in one of the most closely-watched districts in the country means I can’t avoid fundraising.

In the short amount of time I’ve been in Congress, I’ve seen just how much damage Republican chaos and dysfunction can do. Democrats only need three seats to take back the House next year. If we want to take back the House majority, we need to build a firewall right here in Michigan.

[_**Our final FEC filing deadline of the year is approaching quickly, so I have to respectfully ask: Will you chip in $15, or whatever you can, to help us hit our fundraising goal so we can take back the House next year?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120703252/587092097/32658279?refcode=BLU_EM_HSC_2025-12-05_1&amount=15&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTY0MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwYmUwNDY5MS0yMWQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=u1wn5K9Id57VmPRL4WRNlhuFeHyq-t7TkCJlWFil3N0=)

Thank you for always sticking by my side and reading this note.

clean_body: I didn’t run for Congress to raise money. I ran to deliver results for West Michigan, protect our freedoms, and make government work for the people again. | = | Hey there, it’s Hillary. My least favorite part of campaigning is asking for money. I didn’t run for Congress to raise money. I ran to deliver results for West Michigan, protect our freedoms, and make government work for the people again. But the reality is, running a competitive campaign in one of the most closely-watched districts in the country means I can’t avoid fundraising. In the short amount of time I’ve been in Congress, I’ve seen just how much damage Republican chaos and dysfunction can do. Democrats only need three seats to take back the House next year. If we want to take back the House majority, we need to build a firewall right here in Michigan. = | | | = Every time I press send on an email like this, I wish I didn’t have to. But when I remember that our campaign is about delivering for West Michigan, it makes it worth it. Thank you for always sticking by my side and reading this note. Gratefully, Hillary * | | = Paid for by Scholten for Congress Scholten for Congress PO Box 6233 Grand Rapids, MI 49516 United States | = = If you would like to receive fewer emails, . body:
<a clicktracking=off href="http://example.com">
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/NPP_Ed_logo_RGB_green1-01.jpg]
Hi Friend,
Welcome to the latest New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) newsletter!
Earlier this week, the PA [https://www.politicspa.com/could-changes-finally-be-coming-to-pa-election-laws/141393/] House State Government Committee [https://www.politicspa.com/could-changes-finally-be-coming-to-pa-election-laws/141393/] [https://www.politicspa.com/could-changes-finally-be-coming-to-pa-election-laws/141393/] voted on two bills that have the potential to impact our voting rights: State Rep Mehaffie's Voter ID bill (HB771) and State Rep. McClinton's omnibus bill (HB1396). Both passed out of committee this week and will now be voted on by the full house.
An omnibus bill is a legislative bill that combines multiple, sometimes unrelated, provisions or bills into one single bill. It's essentially a package deal of different legislative proposals, often used to facilitate the passage of legislation, particularly appropriations bills.
HB 771 is sponsored by State Rep. Thomas Mehaffie (Dauphin) and “would require all voters arriving at their election precincts to show identification or attest under penalty of law that they are legally able to vote in Pennsylvania.”
State Rep. Malcolm Kenyatta (Philadelphia) voiced his opposition to the bill. The bill doesn’t address [https://www.politicspa.com/state-government-committee-sends-voter-id-election-omnibus-bills-to-house-floor/141399] the real issues at stake. “We need to solve problems of voter irregularities and voter fraud,” he said. “We do not have demonstrable evidence that leads us to make the point that we need to address voter ID in this way. Let’s talk about the issues that do exist. This legislation doesn’t address those.”
HB 1396 has a more recognizable name behind it – Speaker of the State House Joanna McClinton (Delaware/Philadelphia). State Rep. Ben Waxman (Philadelphia) complemented the Speaker, saying that the legislation is a “comprehensive approach to election reform that tries to dramatically improve the process and makes administration of elections much smoother.”
Among other changes, [https://penncapital-star.com/campaigns-elections/voter-id-and-vote-by-mail-reform-bills-move-to-pa-house] House Bill 1396 would also give election workers up to a week before Election Day to prepare to count mail-in ballots, a process that has been a bottleneck for election results in parts of the state, providing fodder for election deniers.
The vote-by-mail provision has been the subject of numerous lawsuits, including one now before the U.S. Supreme Court, as candidates and parties have argued over how election officials should handle irregularities, such as errors on completed ballots, that are not explicitly addressed.
The bill would make clear that county election officials are required to notify voters if their mail-in ballots have been rejected for the lack of a signature and give the voter an opportunity to “cure” the error.
Upcoming events
As Allegheny County voters prepare for the pivotal 2025 local, municipal and judicial elections, the New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) is hosting two critical public candidate forums to give community members the chance to hear directly from the candidates shaping our schools and courts. These moderated discussions will allow voters to ask questions and learn more about each candidate’s priorities and qualifications before heading to the polls.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/Pittsburgh%20School%20board%20forum%208.5x11.png]
Meet the candidates running for the Pittsburgh School Board and hear how they plan to impact our schools and students.
Moderator: Dr. Kimeka Campbell is our Senior Director of Campaigns and Engagement. She is a recognized community activist, political strategist, and civic engagement leader.
With over 15 years of experience in community organizing, political consulting, and nonprofit leadership, Dr. Campbell has been instrumental in shaping voter engagement strategies and building coalitions for progressive change. She co-founded Young Professionals of Color–Greater Harrisburg (YPOC), an organization dedicated to fostering leadership, advocacy, and professional growth for communities of color.
When: Friday, May 9, 2025 | 6:00 PM – 7:30 PM
Where: Hosanna House, 807 Wallace Ave, Wilkinsburg, PA 15221
RSVP [https://click.everyaction.com/k/106793662/542679746/-435660973]
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/Allegheny%20judicial%20flyer.png]
Get to know the Allegheny County Common Pleas judicial candidates before you cast your ballot.
Your vote shapes our justice system!
Moderator: Rachel Bracken, Alliance for Justice (AFJ), Senior Regional State Courts Manager
Rachel is a Senior Regional State Courts Manager with Alliance for Justice/AFJ Action. In this position, she manages AFJ/AFJ Action’s involvement in state supreme court justice elections and selection processes. Prior to joining the Justice Team, Rachel was on AFJ’s Outreach Team where she helped create AFJ’s State Court Justice Project and launch AFJ Action’s State Courts Matter initiative. She also coordinated AFJ’s networks of law professors, attorneys, and political donors to advocate around federal judicial nominations and federal courts’ impact on our constitutional rights and freedoms.
When: Monday, May 12, 2025 | 6:00 PM – 8:00 PM
Where: Hosanna House, 807 Wallace Ave, Wilkinsburg, PA 15221

https://www.instagram.com/p/DJbyV6DPLdJ/ [https://www.instagram.com/p/DJbyV6DPLdJ/]
The deadline to request your mail-in ballot for the primary election is May 13th at 5PM.
Request a mail-in ballot or find your local ballot drop-off location here [vote.pa]
Your county election office must receive your completed mail ballot by 8PM on May 20th.
We recommend mailing it at least 5 days prior, or delivering it by hand at an official ballot drop off location.
#GoVotePA
SHARE ON INSTAGRAM [https://www.instagram.com/p/DJbyV6DPLdJ/]
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/7237344C447445787878394747342B75584E47375A57552F745265427662334B544A4A36505A58657A5871474B7A5A6E53705571424758325657365969365747/editor_images/WCM%20logo.JPG] #CourtsMatter
This past Friday, Courts delivered wins for Pennsylvania voters [https://www.democracydocket.com/news-alerts/a-double-win-for-voters-in-pennsylvania] . One ruling ensured that undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots will be counted, while the other rejected a bid to purge more than 277,000 from the state’s voter rolls.
First, the 3rd U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals denied [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/445-2025-04-24-Order-denying-PA-Attorney-Generals-motion-to-intervene.pdf] a request by Pennsylvania Attorney General Dave Sunday (R), on behalf of the state, to halt a recent federal court ruling that Pennsylvania must count undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots.
The ruling stemmed from a lawsuit originally filed [https://www.democracydocket.com/cases/pennsylvania-undated-and-wrongly-dated-mail-in-ballots-challenge-eakin/] in 2022, challenging the state’s instructions to county boards of elections that any mail-in ballot that’s timely cast but either missing a date or containing the wrong date on the outer return envelopes should not be counted.
After the federal judge’s ruling in March of this year, the plaintiffs appealed [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/441-2025-04-02-Notice-Of-Appeal-.pdf] the decision to the 3rd Circuit. The appeal remains ongoing, but today’s order ensures that counties will count wrongly dated and undated ballots during this month’s primary.
Second, a Pennsylvania district court judge dismissed a lawsuit by the 1789 Foundation to have hundreds of thousands of voters purged from Pennsylvania’s voter rolls.The 1789 Foundation, also known as Citizen_AG, filed [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/1-2024-10-29-Complaint.pdf] a lawsuit in October against Pennsylvania Secretary of State Al Schmidt (R) claiming Schmidt failed to maintain voter rolls in line with the National Voter Registration Act.
The group wanted the court to force Pennsylvania to remove 277,000 registrants who did not respond to confirmation notices in the 2020 general election cycle removed from the voter rolls.
District Court judge Robert Mariani dismissed [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/67-2025-05-02-Memorandum-.pdf] the 1789 Foundation’s lawsuit on the grounds that the organization lacked standing to bring the case in the first place.
In law, standing [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Standing_(law)] is a condition that a party seeking a legal remedy must show they have, by demonstrating to the court, sufficient connection to and harm from the law or action challenged to support the party's participation in the case.
In other news, this year marks the 15th anniversary of a landmark U.S. Supreme Court ruling that fundamentally reshaped the world of campaign finance. For good-government supporters, it’s a somber milestone. Find out why in Spotlight PA’s full report. [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/citizens-united-supreme-court-ruling-campaign-finance-dark-money]
The 2010 decision in Citizens United v. Federal Election Commission , they say, vastly expanded the role of big money in politics and ushered in an era of unprecedented — and in some cases, untraceable — spending on elections by ultra-wealthy special interests.
In Pennsylvania, the court decision’s impact on races for state and local offices was muted — at least early on. That is because of Pennsylvania’s notoriously lax campaign finance laws. Some state lawmakers have sought to tighten rules around so-called “dark money” groups, but their legislation didn’t make it through the full legislature. Continue reading for more on Citizens United and its lasting impact [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/citizens-united-supreme-court-ruling-campaign-finance-dark-money] .
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/DelCo_2.png] We are hiring!
Let's provide civic education together!
We're looking for people passionate about voting rights in Philadelphia, Allegheny, Erie, Lawrence, Bucks, Montgomery, Chester, and Delaware County to help us shape the future of Pennsylvania.
Join our team and help us provide civic education in our communities and register folks to vote!
Positions start at $20 an hour.
Interested? Contact jobs@nppef.org [jobs@nppef.org]
EMAIL US [jobs@nppef.org]
News You Can Use
There are things every one of us can do [https://www.democracydocket.com/opinion/we-have-more-power-than-trump-wants-us-to-believe-heres-what-you-can-do/] in our daily lives to help ensure free and fair elections. Some actions are small — so small that you might dismiss them as unimportant. Don’t. Every important journey begins with a single step, and the first is no less important than the last.
1. Educate Yourself
Elections can be complicated. The rules vary from state to state — and sometimes even from county to county. These rules also change frequently due to new laws, policies or court rulings. My first recommendation: spend time each month learning what’s happening with voting laws in your state and nationally.
Spotlight PA has an 2025 Election Center [https://www.spotlightpa.org/elections] with all the information you need to be prepared to vote this year, including guides to the big races, key dates, and more.
* New: Which Pittsburgh mayoral candidate most aligns with you? Take our quiz [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pittsburgh-mayor-election-2025-candidates-quiz]
* Get answers to your questions with our AI-powered Election Assistant [https://www.spotlightpa.org/elections/assistant/]
* Everything you need to know about mail voting [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-mail-ballot-how-to-request-fill-out-return] , becoming a poll worker [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-how-to-be-poll-worker-guide]
* Everything you need to know about how to vote and where to vote [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pennsylvania-primary-election-day-2025-voter-information-registration]
* Guides to Commonwealth, Superior Courts [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-commonwealth-superior-court-candidates] , vetting judicial candidates [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-candidate-vetting-guide]
* Guides to State College school board [https://www.spotlightpa.org/statecollege/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-state-college-school-board-anderson-huff-barlow-black-desmarais-mcternan-sogor] , Reading City Council president [https://www.spotlightpa.org/berks/2025/04/reading-city-council-president-primary-election-2025]
* Guides to voting as a college student [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-college-student-voting-guide]
En Español
* Guía para votar por correo [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pensilvania-elecciones-primarias-2025-votar-correo-como-llenar] , la presidencia del Concejo Municipal de Reading [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/reading-concejo-municipal-primarias-elecciones-2025]
* Guía para quién está en la papeleta de las primarias [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pensilvania-primarias-elecciones-2025-votar-informacion] , cómo convertirse en trabajador electoral el 20 de mayo [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pensilvania-elecciones-2025-como-convertirse-trabajador-electoral]
2. Stay Engaged
To protect democracy, we all must stay engaged day in and day out. We can’t just show up right before an election and tune out afterward. If we remain committed, democracy will win.
Check out a Montgomery County Commissioner’s effort to educate voters. It's now an emmy-nominated short film. “Running elections in Pennsylvania's third-most-populous county, Neil Makhija said he’s seen the slippery slope from election misinformation to political violence.” ( Philadelphia Inquirer [https://www.inquirer.com/politics/pennsylvania/montgomery-county-commissioner-neil-makhija-emmy-20250508.html] )
3. Share What You Learn
Once you’ve figured out what’s going on, share that information with your networks and community. It might feel awkward to bring up voter suppression or changes in election laws when we’re not in the middle of a major election — but local elections happen in off years, and special elections are more common than you think. Now is the perfect time to start conversations about voting.
Here, in Pennsylvania, we vote every year, twice a year!
4. Join or Support Pro-Democracy Organizations
Is partisan politics not for you? There are numerous nonprofits and grassroots organizations working every day to protect voting rights and ensure free and fair elections. Supporting these groups with your time or resources helps these groups do their work.
Check out the work New PA Project has been up to lately below. AND , check out our upcoming events listed above!
5. Become a Trained Poll Worker
Many counties face a shortage of poll workers, especially during election season. Being a poll worker requires dedication, attention to detail and a willingness to be trained. It’s a meaningful way to serve your community and help protect the integrity of the voting process.
Here are some resources that may be helpful:
Pennsylvania Capital-Star - Advocacy groups offer training for Pa. primary poll watchers [https://penncapital-star.com/briefs/advocacy-groups-offer-training-for-pa-primary-poll-watchers/]
Spotlight PA - Election 2025: How to become a poll worker for the PA primary [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-how-to-be-poll-worker-guide/]
Franklin County Free Press - Poll Workers Needed for Pennsylvania’s 2025 Primary Election [https://fcfreepresspa.com/poll-workers-needed-for-pennsylvanias-2025-primary-election/]
6. Engage Your Elected Officials
Call and write your elected officials at the federal, state and local levels. Tell them that protecting voting rights and ensuring free and fair elections are your top priorities. Even better, show up at public meetings. Ask them directly what they’re doing to combat voter suppression. Be informed about pending legislation and urge them to support pro-democracy initiatives. This applies no matter where you live or who represents you — Democrat or Republican. Constituent pressure always matters. Don’t let anyone tell you otherwise.
7. Vote in Every Election
Yes — every election. That means local elections, primary elections and special elections. Become a super-voter. The more you vote, the more informed and engaged you become. You’ll also be better positioned to notice and report problems — like changes to polling locations or voting equipment — that could impact turnout or accessibility.
Make a plan to vote in the primary election, Tuesday, May 20th. When are you voting this year? Who are you going with? How are you voting this year -- by mail or on Election Day? As a reminder, the last day to request a mail ballot is Tuesday, May 13th!
Recent Events
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/61467144756E73314C4E7335774562454C6644444649732F492B6843473664593068482F7644555678714E564D50363235594F7336424671626839454D43762F/editor_images/491048556_18398781181107182_1677966645695572406_n.jpg] Solidarity for Immigrants
On Monday, May 5th, also Cinco de Mayo, over 300 people, representing 18 community organizations from across Pennsylvania, gathered at the State Capitol in Harrisburg to rally for immigrant rights.
We amplified voices that have been silenced, advocated against injustice, and showed that solidarity is not just a word—it is a force that creates change.
This event was a declaration that Pennsylvania stands against injustice and division.
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/61467144756E73314C4E7335774562454C6644444649732F492B6843473664593068482F7644555678714E564D50363235594F7336424671626839454D43762F/editor_images/signal-2025-05-05-120402.jpeg] [https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/61467144756E73314C4E7335774562454C6644444649732F492B6843473664593068482F7644555678714E564D50363235594F7336424671626839454D43762F/editor_images/signal-2025-05-05-122847.jpeg]

[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/356574676F576F4656484552317542704442666A614C4C4D5334613152463762514A70726B39646D4F494334727651544F3756582B436D446461684F5A536E4F/editor_images/unnamed%20%281%29.jpg] Last Monday, May 5th was the last day to register to vote for the primary election!
Our teams were out in their communities leading up to the voter registration deadline on Monday. We helped folks ensure they have the ability to exercise their right to vote in the primary on Tuesday, May 20th.
Our teams are currently getting ready for GOTV - a time of year where we are on the ground connecting with our communities and mobilizing them to vote.
It's our favorite time of year to build relationships with those we hope to uplift.

clean_body: [] Hi Friend, Welcome to the latest New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) newsletter! Earlier this week, the PA [] House State Government Committee [] [] voted on two bills that have the potential to impact our voting rights: State Rep Mehaffie's Voter ID bill (HB771) and State Rep. McClinton's omnibus bill (HB1396). Both passed out of committee this week and will now be voted on by the full house. An omnibus bill is a legislative bill that combines multiple, sometimes unrelated, provisions or bills into one single bill. It's essentially a package deal of different legislative proposals, often used to facilitate the passage of legislation, particularly appropriations bills. HB 771 is sponsored by State Rep. Thomas Mehaffie (Dauphin) and “would require all voters arriving at their election precincts to show identification or attest under penalty of law that they are legally able to vote in Pennsylvania.” State Rep. Malcolm Kenyatta (Philadelphia) voiced his opposition to the bill. The bill doesn’t address [] the real issues at stake. “We need to solve problems of voter irregularities and voter fraud,” he said. “We do not have demonstrable evidence that leads us to make the point that we need to address voter ID in this way. Let’s talk about the issues that do exist. This legislation doesn’t address those.” HB 1396 has a more recognizable name behind it – Speaker of the State House Joanna McClinton (Delaware/Philadelphia). State Rep. Ben Waxman (Philadelphia) complemented the Speaker, saying that the legislation is a “comprehensive approach to election reform that tries to dramatically improve the process and makes administration of elections much smoother.” Among other changes, [] House Bill 1396 would also give election workers up to a week before Election Day to prepare to count mail-in ballots, a process that has been a bottleneck for election results in parts of the state, providing fodder for election deniers. The vote-by-mail provision has been the subject of numerous lawsuits, including one now before the U.S. Supreme Court, as candidates and parties have argued over how election officials should handle irregularities, such as errors on completed ballots, that are not explicitly addressed. The bill would make clear that county election officials are required to notify voters if their mail-in ballots have been rejected for the lack of a signature and give the voter an opportunity to “cure” the error. Upcoming events As Allegheny County voters prepare for the pivotal 2025 local, municipal and judicial elections, the New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) is hosting two critical public candidate forums to give community members the chance to hear directly from the candidates shaping our schools and courts. These moderated discussions will allow voters to ask questions and learn more about each candidate’s priorities and qualifications before heading to the polls. [] Meet the candidates running for the Pittsburgh School Board and hear how they plan to impact our schools and students. Moderator: Dr. Kimeka Campbell is our Senior Director of Campaigns and Engagement. She is a recognized community activist, political strategist, and civic engagement leader. With over 15 years of experience in community organizing, political consulting, and nonprofit leadership, Dr. Campbell has been instrumental in shaping voter engagement strategies and building coalitions for progressive change. She co-founded Young Professionals of Color–Greater Harrisburg (YPOC), an organization dedicated to fostering leadership, advocacy, and professional growth for communities of color. When: Friday, May 9, 2025 | 6:00 PM – 7:30 PM Where: Hosanna House, 807 Wallace Ave, Wilkinsburg, PA 15221 RSVP [] [] Get to know the Allegheny County Common Pleas judicial candidates before you cast your ballot. Your vote shapes our justice system! Moderator: Rachel Bracken, Alliance for Justice (AFJ), Senior Regional State Courts Manager Rachel is a Senior Regional State Courts Manager with Alliance for Justice/AFJ Action. In this position, she manages AFJ/AFJ Action’s involvement in state supreme court justice elections and selection processes. Prior to joining the Justice Team, Rachel was on AFJ’s Outreach Team where she helped create AFJ’s State Court Justice Project and launch AFJ Action’s State Courts Matter initiative. She also coordinated AFJ’s networks of law professors, attorneys, and political donors to advocate around federal judicial nominations and federal courts’ impact on our constitutional rights and freedoms. When: Monday, May 12, 2025 | 6:00 PM – 8:00 PM Where: Hosanna House, 807 Wallace Ave, Wilkinsburg, PA 15221 RSVP [] Social Media of the Week [] The deadline to request your mail-in ballot for the primary election is May 13th at 5PM. Request a mail-in ballot or find your local ballot drop-off location here [vote.pa] Your county election office must receive your completed mail ballot by 8PM on May 20th. We recommend mailing it at least 5 days prior, or delivering it by hand at an official ballot drop off location. #GoVotePA SHARE ON INSTAGRAM [] [] #CourtsMatter This past Friday, Courts delivered wins for Pennsylvania voters [] . One ruling ensured that undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots will be counted, while the other rejected a bid to purge more than 277,000 from the state’s voter rolls. First, the 3rd U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals denied [] a request by Pennsylvania Attorney General Dave Sunday (R), on behalf of the state, to halt a recent federal court ruling that Pennsylvania must count undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots. The ruling stemmed from a lawsuit originally filed [] in 2022, challenging the state’s instructions to county boards of elections that any mail-in ballot that’s timely cast but either missing a date or containing the wrong date on the outer return envelopes should not be counted. After the federal judge’s ruling in March of this year, the plaintiffs appealed [] the decision to the 3rd Circuit. The appeal remains ongoing, but today’s order ensures that counties will count wrongly dated and undated ballots during this month’s primary. Second, a Pennsylvania district court judge dismissed a lawsuit by the 1789 Foundation to have hundreds of thousands of voters purged from Pennsylvania’s voter rolls.The 1789 Foundation, also known as CitizenAG, filed [] a lawsuit in October against Pennsylvania Secretary of State Al Schmidt (R) claiming Schmidt failed to maintain voter rolls in line with the National Voter Registration Act. The group wanted the court to force Pennsylvania to remove 277,000 registrants who did not respond to confirmation notices in the 2020 general election cycle removed from the voter rolls. District Court judge Robert Mariani dismissed [] the 1789 Foundation’s lawsuit on the grounds that the organization lacked standing to bring the case in the first place. In law, standing [)] is a condition that a party seeking a legal remedy must show they have, by demonstrating to the court, sufficient connection to and harm from the law or action challenged to support the party's participation in the case. In other news, this year marks the 15th anniversary of a landmark U.S. Supreme Court ruling that fundamentally reshaped the world of campaign finance. For good-government supporters, it’s a somber milestone. Find out why in Spotlight PA’s full report. [] The 2010 decision in Citizens United v. Federal Election Commission , they say, vastly expanded the role of big money in politics and ushered in an era of unprecedented — and in some cases, untraceable — spending on elections by ultra-wealthy special interests. In Pennsylvania, the court decision’s impact on races for state and local offices was muted — at least early on. That is because of Pennsylvania’s notoriously lax campaign finance laws. Some state lawmakers have sought to tighten rules around so-called “dark money” groups, but their legislation didn’t make it through the full legislature. Continue reading for more on Citizens United and its lasting impact [] . [] We are hiring! Let's provide civic education together! We're looking for people passionate about voting rights in Philadelphia, Allegheny, Erie, Lawrence, Bucks, Montgomery, Chester, and Delaware County to help us shape the future of Pennsylvania. Join our team and help us provide civic education in our communities and register folks to vote! Positions start at $20 an hour. Interested? Contact jobs@nppef.org [jobs@nppef.org] EMAIL US [jobs@nppef.org] News You Can Use There are things every one of us can do [] in our daily lives to help ensure free and fair elections. Some actions are small — so small that you might dismiss them as unimportant. Don’t. Every important journey begins with a single step, and the first is no less important than the last. 1. Educate Yourself Elections can be complicated. The rules vary from state to state — and sometimes even from county to county. These rules also change frequently due to new laws, policies or court rulings. My first recommendation: spend time each month learning what’s happening with voting laws in your state and nationally. Spotlight PA has an 2025 Election Center [] with all the information you need to be prepared to vote this year, including guides to the big races, key dates, and more. New: Which Pittsburgh mayoral candidate most aligns with you? Take our quiz [] Get answers to your questions with our AI-powered Election Assistant [] Everything you need to know about mail voting [] , becoming a poll worker [] Everything you need to know about how to vote and where to vote [] Guides to Commonwealth, Superior Courts [] , vetting judicial candidates [] Guides to State College school board [] , Reading City Council president [] Guides to voting as a college student [] En Español Guía para votar por correo [] , la presidencia del Concejo Municipal de Reading [] * Guía para quién está en la papeleta de las primarias [] , cómo convertirse en trabajador electoral el 20 de mayo [] 2. Stay Engaged To protect democracy, we all must stay engaged day in and day out. We can’t just show up right before an election and tune out afterward. If we remain committed, democracy will win. Check out a Montgomery County Commissioner’s effort to educate voters. It's now an emmy-nominated short film. “Running elections in Pennsylvania's third-most-populous county, Neil Makhija said he’s seen the slippery slope from election misinformation to political violence.” ( Philadelphia Inquirer [] ) 3. Share What You Learn Once you’ve figured out what’s going on, share that information with your networks and community. It might feel awkward to bring up voter suppression or changes in election laws when we’re not in the middle of a major election — but local elections happen in off years, and special elections are more common than you think. Now is the perfect time to start conversations about voting. Here, in Pennsylvania, we vote every year, twice a year! 4. Join or Support Pro-Democracy Organizations Is partisan politics not for you? There are numerous nonprofits and grassroots organizations working every day to protect voting rights and ensure free and fair elections. Supporting these groups with your time or resources helps these groups do their work. Check out the work New PA Project has been up to lately below. AND , check out our upcoming events listed above! 5. Become a Trained Poll Worker Many counties face a shortage of poll workers, especially during election season. Being a poll worker requires dedication, attention to detail and a willingness to be trained. It’s a meaningful way to serve your community and help protect the integrity of the voting process. Here are some resources that may be helpful: Pennsylvania Capital-Star - Advocacy groups offer training for Pa. primary poll watchers [] Spotlight PA - Election 2025: How to become a poll worker for the PA primary [] Franklin County Free Press - Poll Workers Needed for Pennsylvania’s 2025 Primary Election [] 6. Engage Your Elected Officials Call and write your elected officials at the federal, state and local levels. Tell them that protecting voting rights and ensuring free and fair elections are your top priorities. Even better, show up at public meetings. Ask them directly what they’re doing to combat voter suppression. Be informed about pending legislation and urge them to support pro-democracy initiatives. This applies no matter where you live or who represents you — Democrat or Republican. Constituent pressure always matters. Don’t let anyone tell you otherwise. 7. Vote in Every Election Yes — every election. That means local elections, primary elections and special elections. Become a super-voter. The more you vote, the more informed and engaged you become. You’ll also be better positioned to notice and report problems — like changes to polling locations or voting equipment — that could impact turnout or accessibility. Make a plan to vote in the primary election, Tuesday, May 20th. When are you voting this year? Who are you going with? How are you voting this year -- by mail or on Election Day? As a reminder, the last day to request a mail ballot is Tuesday, May 13th! Recent Events [] Solidarity for Immigrants On Monday, May 5th, also Cinco de Mayo, over 300 people, representing 18 community organizations from across Pennsylvania, gathered at the State Capitol in Harrisburg to rally for immigrant rights. We amplified voices that have been silenced, advocated against injustice, and showed that solidarity is not just a word—it is a force that creates change. This event was a declaration that Pennsylvania stands against injustice and division. [] [] [] NPPEF on the Ground [] Last Monday, May 5th was the last day to register to vote for the primary election! Our teams were out in their communities leading up to the voter registration deadline on Monday. We helped folks ensure they have the ability to exercise their right to vote in the primary on Tuesday, May 20th. Our teams are currently getting ready for GOTV - a time of year where we are on the ground connecting with our communities and mobilizing them to vote. It's our favorite time of year to build relationships with those we hope to uplift. Dauphin County [] [] Delaware County [] [] Luzerne County [] [] Super voters vote twice a year, every year ! Thank you for doing your part to stay informed and engaged! [] Last day to register to request a mail ballot: Tuesday, May 13th Primary Election: Tuesday, May 20th Last day to register to vote for the General Election: Monday, October 20th Last day to request mail-in ballot: Tuesday, October 28th General Election: Tuesday, November 4th Important Links: Voter Hotlines: PA Department of State Website [vote.pa.gov] Voter Protection Hotline: 1-866-OUR-VOTE Upcoming elections [] DOS PA Voter Hotline: 1-877-VOTESPA Mail-in and Absentee Ballot [] Spanish/English Hotline: 1-866-VE-Y-VOTA Fact-Checking Election Claims [] Asian Languages Hotline: 1-888-API-VOTE Arabic/English Hotline: 1-844-YALLA-US Paid for by the New PA Project Education Fund New PA Project Education Fund PO Box 453 West Chester, PA 19381 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: .

**When the stakes are this high, incompetence is not an option. Pete Hegseth should resign.**

My work to hold Pete Hegseth and Donald Trump accountable could make my race a top target for Republicans. And with my biggest fundraising deadline of the year just weeks away, I need your help to make sure we protect this seat and take back the Senate for Democrats.
