name: Mark Kelly
subject: A few thoughts for you, if you have a minute
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for Governor
body:
In 1990, during Desert Storm, I was stationed on the USS Midway. I flew 39 combat missions over Iraq and Kuwait. I had a missile blow up next to my airplane, I was shot at dozens of times by anti-aircraft fire.
| | |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120703535/587093016/1446759855?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk1026-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNzM2OGI0NC0yMmQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=vSONGPAvL8URNjsId0IRtcStZeNCly_Hw4JMU3w3BO0=)
Team, it’s been a weird couple weeks.
In 1990, during Desert Storm, I was stationed on the USS Midway. I flew 39 combat missions over Iraq and Kuwait. I had a missile blow up next to my airplane, I was shot at dozens of times by anti-aircraft fire. If you had told me before one of those missions that 25 years later the President of the United States would call for me to be hanged, I would have said you were crazy.
But that’s exactly what happened two weeks ago.
Sadly, this is the kind of thing we’ve come to expect from this president. He’s a person who can live through the same year we have — rising political violence, escalating tensions, assassinations — and go on to wish death upon sitting members of Congress.
Yes, it was concerning, but more than anything, it’s incredibly sad. I served 25 years in the Navy, launched into orbit 4 times, and ran for Senate because I love this country. To me, there is nothing more American than standing up for our Constitution.
Donald Trump will never understand that. He’ll never understand putting service to this country before yourself. He’s made billions of dollars off his presidency while millions of people are wondering how they’ll afford their health care, or how to feed their kids.
He just doesn’t get it, and he never will. But that makes it even more important that people who do get it — people like you — stick together. That’s how we’ll get through this.
There’s no telling what’s ahead of us. This is uncharted territory for me, for my family, for my team, and we have to be ready for anything.
I wish I didn’t need to ask you to do any more than you already have, but today, that’s what I need to do.
[**Can you split a donation between me and Michael Bennet to help raise the resources we’ll need to stand against Trump and anything he throws at us?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120703536/587093017/1446759855?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk1026-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNzM2OGI0NC0yMmQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=vSONGPAvL8URNjsId0IRtcStZeNCly_Hw4JMU3w3BO0=)
| | [Contribute](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120703537/587093045/1446759855?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk1026-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNzM2OGI0NC0yMmQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=vSONGPAvL8URNjsId0IRtcStZeNCly_Hw4JMU3w3BO0=)
---
Again, I can’t thank you enough. It really means the world to me.

Mark
| Bennet for Governor
PO Box 270117
Louisville, CO 80027
United States **Paid for by Bennet for Governor. # [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120703613/587093183/938567526?refcode=header&noskip=true&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1OGU1N2I1YS0yMmQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bOfa6C9ZhQAnf49by3c1J02jW7zHCHEd3I6KbFr19h0=)
Hi team, Robert Garcia here. This put a smile on my face:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120703614/587093184/789508428?refcode=251205_RG_headline&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1OGU1N2I1YS0yMmQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bOfa6C9ZhQAnf49by3c1J02jW7zHCHEd3I6KbFr19h0=)
_**I take that as a compliment. **_
If exposing the truth about his ties to Jeffrey Epstein...
If pushing for full public release of the Epstein files...
If standing with survivors when Trump and his allies look the other way...
— makes me an antagonist, then I’m proud to be one.
Honestly, being Trump’s “antagonist” isn’t the goal — justice is.
**But if the truth makes Trump uncomfortable, that’s his problem, not mine.**
But here’s the reality: Every time we uncover the truth, Trump’s allies in Congress push back harder. They’re desperate to stop oversight, to shut down investigations, and to keep the public in the dark.
**[I need your help to keep going. Let's keep pushing,
Robert Garcia

name: Pramila Jayapal
subject: I just introduced a bill to end ICE’s abusive immigrant incarceration
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
[ https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20251205-5683-footer&t=1001&akid=5683%2E420207%2EPxOZ_D ]Pramila Jayapal

Peter,

So far in Trump’s second term, ICE has locked up an unprecedented 66,000
people — the vast majority of whom have no criminal record at all — in
what has become the largest for-profit incarceration system in America:
the immigrant incarceration industrial complex, sometimes called immigrant
“detention.”

People are being kidnapped off the streets, disappeared into for-profit
detention centers, and shuffled from facility to facility so their
families and lawyers can’t find them. The for-profit companies that run
these jails donated tens of millions of dollars to Trump’s campaign, and
now are reaping huge profits from the unprecedented flow of $45 billion of
taxpayer dollars to expand mass detentions.

These immigrant incarceration facilities dehumanize immigrants with
subhuman unsanitary conditions, freezing temperatures and no access to
medical care or even lawyers. At least 225 judges across the country
(including 23 appointed by Trump) have already ruled that these tactics
violate due process. Don’t let anyone tell you that these are “criminals”:
ICE’s own statistics show that over 73% of those incarcerated have no
criminal record and the remaining 27% have committed minor infractions
like traffic violations. Research shows that over 170 US citizens have
even been abducted and detained.

This year alone, 23 people have died in ICE custody — a record high. One
man died with his hands and feet tied together, and ICE claimed it was a
suicide. This administration continues to lie about it all.

This has to stop.

Yesterday, I introduced the Dignity for Detained Immigrants Act along with
124 original co-sponsors and a powerful coalition of immigrant rights
advocates. My bill would finally overhaul this abusive system from the
ground up by:

* Ending the use of private, for-profit immigration detention centers
and instead utilizing alternatives to detention that have been shown
to work and don’t waste taxpayer dollars
* Prohibiting the detention of children and families
* Making it harder to detain primary caregivers and vulnerable people,
including pregnant people and people with serious medical issues
* Imposing meaningful penalties for failure to comply with civil
detention standards
* Requiring unannounced congressional inspections so ICE can’t hide
abuses from the American people

As an immigrant, as the Ranking Member on the Immigration Subcommittee,
and as someone who knows these stories could have been mine, I cannot and
will not accept the cruelty that’s happening right now as normal. That’s
why I have held five shadow hearings to draw attention to what is
happening. That’s why I developed a brand new Immigration Justice
Resistance Lab training on what regular people who hate what is happening
can do, right now, to push back.

[ https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/50886?utm_source=em20251205-5683&t=1002&akid=5683%2E420207%2EPxOZ_D ]Sign up for our next Resistance Lab on Sunday, December 14, where we’ll
be specifically focusing on immigration justice. This is a great way to
get involved in a massive nonviolent movement to protect immigrant
communities.

But I need your help now,
Peter. Building the momentum to
pass this bill will take all of us. If we’re going to overcome the
dark-money machine behind mass detention and organize a national movement
strong enough to protect people from these abuses, then I need you in the
fight with me.

[ https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20251205.xfifQP&refcode=em20251205-5683&amount=3&t=1003&akid=5683%2E420207%2EPxOZ_D ]Can I count on you to pitch in $3 — or anything you can
— to support our work to pass the Dignity for Detained Immigrants Act and
build the grassroots support we need to end these inhumane conditions?

[ https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20251205.xfifQP&amount=3&refcode=em20251205-5683&t=1022&akid=5683%2E420207%2EPxOZ_D ]Contribute $3

This is about reclaiming our values, refusing to accept cruelty as policy,
and building the people-powered movement capable of taking on the
for-profit detention industry and winning.

We can stop the lawlessness and abuse happening in our name. We can create
an immigration system rooted in dignity, justice, and humanity. But we can
only do it together.

Thank you, truly, for being part of this fight.

In solidarity,

Pramila Jayapal

Contribute to Pramila Jayapal: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20251205-5683-footer&t=1023&refcode2=5683_420207_PxOZ_D&akid=5683%2E420207%2EPxOZ_D

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe:

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.pramilaforcongress.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=5683%2E420207%2EPxOZ_D

Paid for by Pramila for Congress.

name: John Kennedy
subject: Right now, I’m madder than a wet hen!

This ain't the same Party that it was when I first entered the big leagues. It's become a paradise for Liberal hogwash and downright anti-American behavior.
Whether it's...

* Rioting in cities across America and burning Old Glory
* Storming ICE facilities, doxxing agents, and throwing sandwiches at law enforcement
* Having actual COMMUNISTS run some of our greatest cities

It sure seems like once you think the Democrats can't stoop any lower, they grab a shovel and prove you wrong... Guess they never heard the first rule of holes: if you're in one, STOP DIGGING.
_But let me tell you the God-honest truth. They have the money to do it and if we don 't get our act together, they just might put us in their place._
Right now, the Democrats have a well-oiled fundraising machine that is raking in the big bucks from its out-of-touch, elitist donors - 100 million bucks, to be exact.
After seeing that, I knew I had to step in.
**_[So my good friend Majority Leader Steve Scalise and I got to talking. clean_body: way these Democrats are acting up right now's got me madder than a wet hen in a tote sack. This ain't the same Party that it was when I first entered the big leagues. It's become a paradise for Liberal hogwash and downright anti-American behavior. Whether it's... Rioting in cities across America and burning Old Glory Storming ICE facilities, doxxing agents, and throwing sandwiches at law enforcement Having actual COMMUNISTS run some of our greatest cities It sure seems like once you think the Democrats can't stoop any lower, they grab a shovel and prove you wrong... Guess they never heard the first rule of holes: if you're in one, STOP DIGGING. But let me tell you the God-honest truth. They have the money to do it and if we don 't get our act together, they just might put us in their place. Right now, the Democrats have a well-oiled fundraising machine that is raking in the big bucks from its out-of-touch, elitist donors - 100 million bucks, to be exact. After seeing that, I knew I had to step in. Do you reckon you could be one of those Conservatives? we all step up and work together I think we'll be able to keep these Democrats at bay and continue to deliver wins for good, hard workin' Americans. But I can 't do it without you, Peter. Thanks for hearing me out, John N. Kennedy A personal note from Majority Leader Steve Scalise: I'm humbled to announce that I am your new House Majority Leader. My first order of business is checking in with our best supporters, so I just launched my new MAJORITY LEADER SURVEY for that specific reason. "I’d be honored to get your unfiltered thoughts, feedback, and opinions using this secure link." | | "RESPOND NOW →" Thank you, Steve Scalise House Majority Leader | | Paid for by TEAM SCALISE, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit SCALISE FOR CONGRESS and EYE OF THE TIGER PAC. Team Scalise is committed to restoring a Conservative House majority and electing Conservatives across the country. Thank you for being a part of our movement. This message was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com because you are a member of the Team Scalise Conservative grassroots network. Contributions to TEAM SCALISE, or any participating committee, are not deductible as charitable contributions for federal income tax purposes. Contributions from corporations, national banks, labor unions, foreign nationals without "green cards," and federal government contractors are prohibited. Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, mailing address, occupation, and employer of each individual who contributes over $200 in a calendar year. You can donate via mail by sending a check to: 320 First Street SE 2nd Floor Washington, DC 20003 |

disclaimer_text: Paid for by United for Democracy
body:
The Supreme Court just weighed in on Texas’ gerrymandered maps. | |
---
Peter, **BREAKING: The Supreme Court issued an order last night blessing Texas’ gerrymandered maps.** States across the country are locked in a redistricting battle, and Trump is placing a huge bet that the MAGA justices on the Supreme Court will step in to back him up. **That’s exactly what they did last night.** **Thanks to the Court’s use of the “emergency” docket, they gave minimal explanation and heard no arguments.** Texas’ Congressional map will officially be rigged in favor of Republicans in next year’s midterm elections. **If the Supreme Court won’t defend our voting rights, Congress must. It’s more important than ever that they step in to end Trump’s gerrymandering frenzy.** [**Rush a letter to your members of Congress now. Demand they pass the**** _Redistricting Reform Act_****in the face of the Supreme Court's blatant complicity in ripping apart fair Congressional maps.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm38jvf5GaclcQ7i_R9B-DMhJxpTJE3HIHzGke8tGHrJP6XXa640Zb7hMYvW6bxzrSWNfXYnKBR4cVCJA9k2qV7453KeO3DlJ9yCaZJZ20xf81IN24oTqUiafXyBP2EdMJNizhVpRX34q7YTaHSWnarF9L3W9sY97bvgVVc-YYOdgNALDKhyMBm_EVqSBLiOKe9IEbIvqes8sOMjETi5xfJNoHh69qYMXAY-1PKda_oqrPkUs9Ry8JKUW1Lt1nimY6eT2JKpe8iLLq1ep2CvJhCld3pFXp7IfVRULEvleBOkvQMUEsaBz8agywfpItVnYn2ciqKC6cVnnYKi-Del-DlGZ1XLSV-ovvDreSZE1x2j5Eyj8EygiV3wg5kC2kELW5y0U37zD5hrnceAXXEm8SOnLVCz2Voya47GhIKmnTQB0ptZ0SEp38YLmI2B5Q53gj-Kmiezag3ELNvMBgWMzz5-tf8N-QeUU9Iomkdgu1ntBr9omv5CpeAexmDq4Sn0vjOlzwjMDJI98M2G1RPzDz6ai/4m6/Pj1P4WpnR82rcMQD3hY1XA/h0/h001.9befk9Xy00IlUwBsl-WSH-MDr67j0pd8JoTT3rYvmAI) [SIGN & SEND](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm38jvf5GaclcQ7i_R9B-DMhJxpTJE3HIHzGke8tGHrJP6XXa640Zb7hMYvW6bxzrSWNfXYnKBR4cVCJA9k2qV7453KeO3DlJ9yCaZJZ20xf81IN24oTqUiafXyBP2EdMJNizhVpRX34q7YTaHSWnarF9L3W9sY97bvgVVc-YYOdgNALDKhyMBm_EVqSBLiOKe9IEbIvqes8sOMjETi5xfJNoHh69qYMXAY-1PKda_oqrPkUs9Ry8JKUW1Lt1nimY6eT2JKpe8iLLq1ep2CvJhCld3pFXp7IfVRULEvleBOkvQGoVXGZP-GrpesephnBVr4oVt3Dkz5Qy0ay5s3qEMb55rN5XkR4u1EYoIP0f9BihSZJ9P2MRdhAscwCJC2qF2FSehaxUMzWCD5jE4zGEZcukjm-zuvbuX4_v7hAnryFdYu_vOCtsNp6zWv3tK1WuoufI75_Xb4B_Pt8C81fq6L6MCCIIm9kBA9DoSxvDtILVkCuVeEKImcXfAdI2oPpsUibCoPbM90reIJGmTjDqZmvT/4m6/Pj1P4WpnR82rcMQD3hY1XA/h1/h001.FidQBO84vjonK5tQD1oz0djR3wqK4ZQc46JB9UWXCNc) A normal, functioning Supreme Court would have drawn the line at unconstitutional, racist gerrymandering. But this is not a normal, functioning Supreme Court. Our country doesn’t work if politicians choose their voters instead of voters choosing their politicians. It’s a race to the bottom, and we will all lose. **It’s up to us to protect our Constitution and our rights. We must pressure Congress to act before it’s too late.** [**Tell Congress: End the redistricting war by ending partisan gerrymandering. Pass the**** _Redistricting Reform Act_****today!**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm38jvf5GaclcQ7i_R9B-DMhJxpTJE3HIHzGke8tGHrJP6XXa640Zb7hMYvW6bxzrSWNfXYnKBR4cVCJA9k2qV7453KeO3DlJ9yCaZJZ20xf81IN24oTqUiafXyBP2EdMJNizhVpRX34q7YTaHSWnarF9L3W9sY97bvgVVc-YYOdgNALDKhyMBm_EVqSBLiOKe9IEbIvqes8sOMjETi5xfJNoHh69qYMXAY-1PKda_oqrPkUs9Ry8JKUW1Lt1nimY6eT2JKpe8iLLq1ep2CvJhCld3pFXp7IfVRULEvleBOkvQDHqEdOoLi7vI2iT3L8VPxLjT4J0bKHJzZLSbAssckfBWL-93Gak2XpbLpeQhWOlD3I6B0AieIaiXWkx5E1h_jWg_oN0NFtFcWW82vvJhQFqEy5nyD5JmnEfAR6RHB4xsJP2etmMRwxBnh4pnK7huQwUX0_zpaboAWFbxVuFjpUFHAlGg0jsb4njtPJSo_klsZ8OBGcxwkATGpFkdNpDXpRZdztFmvUYxIsNkEIqiMai/4m6/Pj1P4WpnR82rcMQD3hY1XA/h2/h001.vvFu5lR3jNNSB3zitOogv5inVfXFAc8LGta03u3rMeo) Thank you for fighting to protect our voting rights, Ken’Travius Coleman

name: Tobias Read
subject: Add your name: No legal authority. No transparency. No way.
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251205em-rs [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251205em-rs]
Hi, it’s Oregon Secretary of State, Tobias Read.
I’m writing because something deeply troubling is happening inside our federal government, and every voter in Oregon deserves to know the truth.
The US Department of Justice has been requesting your private voter information — including your date of birth, partial Social Security number, and driver’s license number — without legal authority, without transparency, and without any explanation of how they intend to use it.
I refused to hand over that data.
https://secure.ngpvan.com/CfMP30xwgUWZNBhKV-mlDw2 [https://secure.ngpvan.com/CfMP30xwgUWZNBhKV-mlDw2]
No legal authority. No transparency. No way.
What’s more, now we’ve learned that the USDOJ has already been sharing personal voter data with the Department of Homeland Security and funneling it into the SAVE program — a system that watchdogs have repeatedly called inaccurate, insecure, and unreliable.
This is exactly why I said no from the start. And why I’m still saying no — even as they sue me for it.
I will not allow your personal information to be misused to intimidate voters, target immigrants, or tear families apart. That will never be Oregon’s way.
Along with Colorado’s Secretary of State and eight other Secretaries of State across the country, I’ve demanded answers directly from the Attorney General and the Secretary of Homeland Security.
But the federal government needs to hear from you too.
If you agree that personal information should not be used to intimidate voters, target immigrants, or tear families apart, please add your name to my petition now: [https://secure.ngpvan.com/CfMP30xwgUWZNBhKV-mlDw2]
ADD YOUR NAME [https://secure.ngpvan.com/CfMP30xwgUWZNBhKV-mlDw2]
Oregonians — and all Americans — deserve to know how their data is being used.
We’re standing up because your privacy, your rights, and your vote matter.
Thank you for being part of this fight.
Tobias Read
Secretary of State, Oregon
P.S. You can read the press release regarding our demand for answers from US DOJ and Department of Homeland Security here [https://apps.oregon.gov/oregon-newsroom/OR/SOS/Posts/Post/secretaries-demand-answers-from-us-department-of-justice-us-department-homeland-security-voter-data] .
CONTRIBUTE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251205em-rs]
https://www.facebook.com/TobiasReadOR [https://www.facebook.com/TobiasReadOR] https://twitter.com/TobiasRead [https://twitter.com/TobiasRead] https://instagram.com/tobiasreador [https://instagram.com/tobiasreador]

Friends of Tobias Read (5208)
PO Box 42307
Portland, OR 97242
United States
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/IMahE5FQZEyEjGesjn_XSA2?unsubscribedata=wansZgehtC6UD%2FlSmPm%2BAr3Re%2Fy9u8DlVZ5NnNoI3BDZMuRa0l8TIeJYoPjGG77e4YLgG023QgH7cniFjwT%2B26NE%2BF9hsrmGpZ8X%2Bk9fZhG7Ss06gX3YRB3td9fGIQSSHKET7r6DDawbi1o39Zy2%2Fm85FtRCJkUuuSmgXP4WPpglFiXBOhn%2FqAZxbOF5%2F76Sg3X%2BvurIOEYY9qf3MCjJIEgVADnu1O0J%2BKysZ81YWJbQRwyIFv7HSROkqo58KMO88acsw8R1pZL76aQAzuCQr9aXC%2FlVOay1d0ISg1SJFJvtSI2qKxEu5Sa89XR252IZeU0e7hJIC7OCFdu3MZUzzznSsDp9kxFZixJJw59ztsAVK6XYtyAhpaqdWf12dWxVSYq%2BpRhy5Sf5QSJMIr3xEvd5S0S1znwzTe0gi6J0Mu6fcoUQemeZDGLyBHbddJlS .

name: AOC
subject: I have one of the lowest average contribution numbers in all of politics.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
I'm proud of that number: $15.
#
I have not taken a dime in corporate or special interest money since the first day of my campaign. I am 100% grassroots.
The average donation to my campaign changes, but it's about $15-16 dollars. Sometimes it gets as high as $19.
When people ask, “Why are you able to take the stances that you do?” or “Why are you able to speak the way that you do?”
A lot of it is because **I do not rely on special interests to get re-elected or to finance my campaign.**
I am financed 100% by everyday regular people and by workers.
I have probably one of the lowest average contribution numbers in all of politics, and I'm proud of that number—$15.
Together we’ve built a movement that won’t back down. Thank you for being a part of it.
[ Help us continue the fight with a grassroots contribution today.](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20251205.D-AVek&refcode=em20251205-10345-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=10345%2E2526796%2ExZ081c)
**[Contribute $5](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20251205.D-AVek&refcode=em20251205-10345-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=10345%2E2526796%2ExZ081c)**
In solidarity,
Alexandria

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

[Get the facts yourself and learn why RFK Jr is sounding the alarm about a new emerging health crisis before it’s too late.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=3bd9efe3380c4299b33f6fa93d959bf1&_e=NRqM0ot7L-jvd7zatff8J9m10edVYrPVY20YSVw20ERd8M1lNBFIBounoPJMVuHb29Rn8NsgC4nnAv3LeCRKLieZAWSY4o8RQ-w0Ap-wp19w1HG08vLPPcpE_SoKYM8tpAc9-s8iPukbv06HxhN17Sl64pZdWipfGTlAhWVZLM41tvhICQ01giI0YqUJZ93N7WI8dSCua7xSeCZpYvDY3b7sda4fBLHvjD0nR3NrnD-I22ruymeA8kdQyJnDFUY_4T4LSqFGQ0sB7EMrCuQzdiO4QpHaw1oVbYf7Nb5pza4j6dWDspGmIhbAmxxth_IcmVKuTNDR1rzlqYhpZkR-rOrfVUA5EdXXLNLWl3hRIp13Ag1l_DvZWLCxZgQRyeVRKGSDMGW1DotD6og1_xEZfyRQx-bzLTigPKv-Vil3Z7YLcleYSyEDphi_FUAIFjOVDjDpcbYZEwfvv5hOlaAJsIoIdhcEvvVXYHY_v371CyD8tFuCvoLVyHowUhVAIncfr-1xTK2mAOzruzbj6jziHxOtvkHjGNpHboIHusStvC6Cxzu6U1c88Jz3aYCy99AyYms396Ssk0vWa7Zp33DjlA%3D%3D)
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. name: Tim Holbert, American Veterans Center
subject: Celebrate 250 years of American valor with your free American flag
body:
Please read the following message from our sponsor.
* * *

| | | | [](https://click.em.dcdailyjournal.com/t/c/5492ce3fbe114add95cd94b3c349b185-156c943f68294badb2b319820807a130/63)
---
Friend,
For more than two decades, American Veterans Center has been dedicated to honoring the sacrifice and preserving the legacy of our nation’s heroes. Each year, we host the **National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C.** , bringing Americans together in a powerful display of remembrance and patriotism. We also proudly produce **“American Valor: A Salute to Our Heroes,”** a nationally televised tribute to the men and women who have served across generations.

But our work goes far beyond public events. At the heart of our mission is a commitment to **preserve the stories of veterans from every era of service** — from the final voices of World War II, to those who served in Korea, Vietnam, and the post-9/11 generation. Through these efforts, we ensure that future generations understand the cost of freedom and the courage it has taken to defend it.

This year, American Veterans Center is marking historic military milestones: the 250th anniversaries of the U.S. Army, Navy, and Marine Corps...the 80th anniversary of the victories that won World War II...and key events in the Korean War 75 years ago, and the Vietnam War which ended 50 years ago this year.
---
[](https://click.em.dcdailyjournal.com/t/c/5492ce3fbe114add95cd94b3c349b185-156c943f68294badb2b319820807a130/63)
---
**We may never have another opportunity like this to recognize their sacrifice on a national scale.**

[**To mark the occasion, I want to send you a full-size (3x5ft) American flag when you make a gift of $25, $50, $100 or more to support the American Veterans Center’s historic campaign: American Honor 2025.**](https://click.em.dcdailyjournal.com/t/c/5492ce3fbe114add95cd94b3c349b185-156c943f68294badb2b319820807a130/63)
---
[ **Claim Your FREE Flag Today**](https://click.em.dcdailyjournal.com/t/c/5492ce3fbe114add95cd94b3c349b185-156c943f68294badb2b319820807a130/63)
---
This is one of our most ambitious efforts ever, Friend — a national salute to those who fought to make and keep America free.

**Together, we’re capturing the stories of WWII veterans before it’s too late...and preserving seldom-heard accounts from those who fought in Korea, Vietnam, and the post-9/11 wars.**

And with your gift today, I’ll send you a free, full-size American flag as my thank you.

To some, it may look like just a flag.

_But to those who served, it’s a promise kept._

It flew over the beaches of Normandy.

It sailed with the Pacific fleet.

It was stitched into the sleeves of every soldier who never made it home.

**This flag reminds us of all they sacrificed, and what we’re now called to defend.**

Let it fly proudly as a reminder that freedom isn’t free — and that this generation will not forget the sacrifices of the last.

[**So please, Friend, make your gift today. Help us preserve their legacy while there's still time — and proudly fly the flag they fought for.**](https://click.em.dcdailyjournal.com/t/c/5492ce3fbe114add95cd94b3c349b185-156c943f68294badb2b319820807a130/63)
---
[ **Claim Your Free Flag Today**](https://click.em.dcdailyjournal.com/t/c/5492ce3fbe114add95cd94b3c349b185-156c943f68294badb2b319820807a130/63)
---
Friend, throughout American history, our nation’s flag has stood tall.

**I hope you’ll take this opportunity to receive your American flag and fly it proudly, today and every day as a symbol AND promise of freedom.**

Thank you for standing with America’s heroes — past, present, and future.
---
Sincerely,

Tim Holbert
President
American Veterans Center
---
[ **Support the American Veterans Center**](https://click.em.dcdailyjournal.com/t/c/5492ce3fbe114add95cd94b3c349b185-156c943f68294badb2b319820807a130/63)
---
The mission of the American Veterans Center and the World War II Veterans Committee is to preserve and promote the legacy and experiences of America's veterans and active duty service personnel from World War II through today.
A non-profit educational organization, the AVC and WWIIVC are funded solely through generous contributions from people like you. All contributions are tax-deductible.
© American Veterans Center
1100 N. Glebe Road Suite 910, Arlington, VA 22201
---

DCDailyJournal.com

name: Voter Action Project
subject: Defending our fundamental right to vote nationwide
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Whether it's these 27 different state bills, folks, or the SAVE Act in Congress, it's abundantly clear that opponents of voting rights are only ramping up their attacks on our fundamental rights . We have to put pressure on them to stop at both the state and federal level.
RUSH $25 or more right now to stand with us and fight to defend our right to vote wherever it's under attack >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=120525_2&amount=25]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
BCJ: State Bills Would Require Passport or Birth Certificate to Register to Vote [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=120525_2&amount=25]
Team,
We've told you that voter I.D. laws are unfairly biased against people of color, young people, and unmarried women. However, across the country, states are introducing bills to make these laws even MORE restrictive at an alarming rate.
The nonpartisan Brennan Center for Justice has the details: “ Lawmakers in at least 27 states have introduced...bills in 2025 that would require some or all voters to show citizenship documents to vote .” Not just when you register, but every single time you show up to cast a ballot.
While citizenship is required to vote, rigorous research shows time and again that instances of votes cast by non-citizens are extremely rare. At least 21 million citizens, however, do not have immediate access to the I.D. documents these laws would demand, including 3.1 million voters who lack documents entirely.
The suppressive SAVE Act is stalled in Congress. So opponents of voting rights have turned to the states. These bills aren’t motivated by a desire for free and fair elections – they’re just another voter suppression tactic.
We need your help to defend the right to vote in these 27 states and beyond. Will you chip in with $25 or whatever you can today to help us fight back against voter suppression and intimidation? >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=120525_2&amount=25]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=120525_2&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=120525_2&amount=25] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=120525_2&amount=50]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=120525_2&amount=100] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=120525_2&amount=250] OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=120525_2&amount=35]
The problem is not only that these bills would block millions of Americans from voting, but also that these requirements to vote are a massive burden for election officials and many voters alike.
These intimidating requirements also come alongside other suppressive policies stocked into these bills: Mandatory voter purges, reduced early voting, closing polling places, restrictions on mail-in ballots, and so much more.
We need to speak with one loud, clear voice and tell every state legislature: We won't stand for any of these suppressive policies. Not now, not ever.
Opponents of voting rights aren't going to stop their work to restrict voting until we make them stop. Fuel our efforts to resist $25 or whatever you can today >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=120525_2&amount=25]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=120525_2&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=120525_2&amount=20]

This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Emails are a crucial way to keep supporters up to date on the latest attacks on voting rights and our efforts to expand ballot access for all Americans. If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.everyaction.com/p/FQb7Rbo110qLPmSvMAn63Q2?unsubscribedata=hTIQBv9FSuM7wJZTl%2FpMEINNoHRX45QnORruDM8S38xVOkP0j5eHx9j86KdbTa7JF%2BmvyTnKR2T0eTgCSfkYy7O2C1W3Dz1BRxXfasqYUNQvQA5poNUrexdhgCoeXKYEeQNiAk1QdYjwxJOVEhtFS%2BHm%2Ff7XF%2FLuEUyy7f6YT5NkY6YPUF11%2BXUgvtzeymJDB0C9XfFUSQo5GdzLihnIY2HHaaNRmzywL4ZkawVmRkJrvNs4bZ94VZ%2F0nIaYpr%2BtRMuHVdLNUXBMjojzUkKqt9s81eXxtQ9bBxwKUQLnWbfT12Uf5cIgG9aoJOcL5lvt2NaPdY5KivkjrjMql3YVBf805VrFvNngzK7aSU%2Fi6R39dNIFq3le3qtgSR9tYOaTf5x2YYuA9OyQLD8aveBaTZTcLtVKJyHwV%2BDD5mHJA8s%3D .
clean_body: Whether it's these 27 different state bills, folks, or the SAVE Act in Congress, it's abundantly clear that opponents of voting rights are only ramping up their attacks on our fundamental rights . We have to put pressure on them to stop at both the state and federal level. RUSH $25 or more right now to stand with us and fight to defend our right to vote wherever it's under attack >> [] Voter Action Project [] BCJ: State Bills Would Require Passport or Birth Certificate to Register to Vote [] Team, We've told you that voter I.D. laws are unfairly biased against people of color, young people, and unmarried women. However, across the country, states are introducing bills to make these laws even MORE restrictive at an alarming rate. The nonpartisan Brennan Center for Justice has the details: “ Lawmakers in at least 27 states have introduced...bills in 2025 that would require some or all voters to show citizenship documents to vote .” Not just when you register, but every single time you show up to cast a ballot. While citizenship is required to vote, rigorous research shows time and again that instances of votes cast by non-citizens are extremely rare. At least 21 million citizens, however, do not have immediate access to the I.D. documents these laws would demand, including 3.1 million voters who lack documents entirely. The suppressive SAVE Act is stalled in Congress. So opponents of voting rights have turned to the states. These bills aren’t motivated by a desire for free and fair elections – they’re just another voter suppression tactic. We need your help to defend the right to vote in these 27 states and beyond. Will you chip in with $25 or whatever you can today to help us fight back against voter suppression and intimidation? >> [] If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [] GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [] OTHER AMOUNT [] The problem is not only that these bills would block millions of Americans from voting, but also that these requirements to vote are a massive burden for election officials and many voters alike. These intimidating requirements also come alongside other suppressive policies stocked into these bills: Mandatory voter purges, reduced early voting, closing polling places, restrictions on mail-in ballots, and so much more. We need to speak with one loud, clear voice and tell every state legislature: We won't stand for any of these suppressive policies. Not now, not ever. Opponents of voting rights aren't going to stop their work to restrict voting until we make them stop. Fuel our efforts to resist $25 or whatever you can today >> [] Voter Action Project [] [] [] [] [] Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country. Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities. Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [] Donate Now [] Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code. A copy of Integrity First Voter Action Project's latest financial report may be obtained by mail at Integrity First Voter Action Project, c/o Mele Brengarth & Assoc, PO Box 15845, Washington, DC 20003. name: State Representative Ryan Chamberlin
subject: Newsletter from Rep. Ryan Chamberlin
body:
Newsletter from Rep. Ryan Chamberlin | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/repryanchamberlin/newsletter-from-rep-ryan-chamberlin-12931434?e=d1c1b9864f)
---
| |
---
| | **NEWSLETTER FROM REP. RYAN CHAMBERLIN**
---
|
---
| Friends and Neighbors,

I hope everyone enjoyed a wonderful Thanksgiving with family and friends. **We have officially wrapped up committee week five,** and the pace in Tallahassee continues to build as we move closer to the start of the 2026 legislative session. Each week brings valuable discussions, productive meetings, and opportunities to advance policies that strengthen our communities.

**My fellow legislators and I remain focused on putting Florida first!** Our state continues to lead in so many areas, and it is important that we continue promoting legislation that protects freedom, encourages fiscal responsibility, and upholds our conservative values. **I look forward to sharing more updates as we make progress in the weeks ahead.**

As always, please do not hesitate to [contact my office](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=5d199eea0f&e=d1c1b9864f) if there is something we can do for you. My team and I would be more than happy to help you in any way we can.
---
| | _**Committee Meetings**_
---
|
---
| **I have the honor of serving on several important committees for the upcoming 2026 Legislative Session.** Committee week five is now complete, and these meetings continue to shape the policies and priorities we will carry into the new year.
* [Intergovernmental Affairs Subcommittee](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=5899c37a1e&e=d1c1b9864f)
* [Human Services Subcommittee](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=0bcf7b4083&e=d1c1b9864f)
* [Economic Infrastructure Subcommittee](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=32a4cd1ebb&e=d1c1b9864f)
* [Justice Budget Subcommittee](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=f35bcc4ece&e=d1c1b9864f)

---
| | _**Events & Receptions Attended in Tallahassee**_
---
|
---
| I enjoyed attending the Florida Electric Co-Ops Caucus and hearing updates on the priorities and challenges facing Florida’s electric cooperatives.
---
| | _**Meetings in Tallahassee**_
---
|
---
| Had a great meeting with **Sheriff Hardwick to discuss the priorities of the Florida Sheriffs Association and the critical issues facing our law enforcement communities** across the state.

We also spoke about the new **Athletic Diamond of Dreams** — an incredible investment in youth, community engagement, and giving local kids a safe, positive place to play and grow. Grateful for his leadership and dedication to both public safety and building stronger communities.
---
|
---
|
---
| Had the opportunity to meet with the team at **Decision Tactical** to learn more about the high-impact training they’re delivering to law enforcement agencies across our state.

Their realistic, scenario-based approach is **helping equip officers with the skills and confidence needed to make critical decisions in the field** and keep our communities safe. Grateful for the work they’re doing in support of those who serve.
---
|
---
|
---
| Met with**leaders from the City of Lauderhill** to discuss their legislative priorities, with a focus on strengthening public safety and investing in stormwater infrastructure.

These are critical issues for protecting residents, building resilience, and ensuring long-term community sustainability. **Grateful for their leadership and continued partnership.**
---
|
---
|
---
| I also met with the **Florida Association of Postsecondary Schools and Colleges** to discuss their legislative priorities surrounding nursing programs. As Florida continues to face critical workforce shortages in healthcare, **it’s essential that we support pathways that prepare qualified, compassionate nurses** to meet the growing demand.

I appreciate their collaboration and commitment to strengthening nursing education across our state.
---
|
---
|
---
| Members of the **Florida auto dismantling and recycling industry** stopped by to discuss their challenges, priorities, and the important role they play in protecting our environment and supporting local jobs.

**Conversations like this help shape better policy for our communities.** Grateful for their partnership and insight.
---
|
---
| I had the opportunity to meet with **Cielo Digital Infrastructure** and the **Florida Association of Managing Entities** during my time in Tallahassee. These meetings provided important insight into Florida’s expanding digital infrastructure needs and the vital role managing entities play in supporting behavioral health services across our communities. Hearing about their work and the impact they have in communities across Florida was incredibly valuable as we continue preparing for the upcoming legislative session.
---
| |
---
| Looking forward to closing out the final committee week next week as we prepare for the 2026 legislative session, which begins on January 13. Thank you for your continued support of my leadership in the Florida House! I look forward to sharing with you again soon.

Have a great week!

Best regards,

**Ryan Chamberlin**
******State Representative**
**District 24**
---
| | _Copyright © 2025 Ryan Chamberlin for State Representative, All rights reserved._

Paid by Ryan Chamberlin, Republican, for State Representative

**Our mailing address is:**
Ryan Chamberlin for State Representative
526 E Park Ave
Tallahassee, FL 32301-2568

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/profile?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=929c76fdea&e=d1c1b9864f&c=42f22c0630) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=929c76fdea&t=b&e=d1c1b9864f&c=42f22c0630).

---

name: Ilhan Omar
subject: Trump is unhinged
body:
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/18910?refcode=em20251205-1685-foote&t=1001&akid=1685%2E342411%2E5yBGqt ]Squad Victory Fund

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20251205-omar-split-movement.0__o8W&refcode=em20251205-1685&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=1685%2E342411%2E5yBGqt ]Tweet from Ilhan Omar: 'His obsession with me is creepy. I hope he gets
the help he desperately needs.'

Peter, Trump is unhinged.

We are watching a sad man who is desperately flailing to save his
disgraceful presidency.

The cost of living is rising thanks to his reckless tariffs.

Insurance premiums are skyrocketing.

Millions are losing their healthcare and food assistance.

The economy is losing jobs instead of adding them.

His ICE raids are inhumane and unpopular.

His approval ratings are down the drain.

He lost “bigly” in November’s elections.

And his cabinet is busy committing war crimes, helping spread measles, and
lying to Congress.

Trump needs to come after me and the Somali-American community because he
needs a scapegoat for his failures.

Today it is me and my community, but we know it won’t stop there. Trump
and his administration have already gone after some of my other colleagues
like Congresswoman Delia Ramirez for proudly claiming her
Guatemalan-American roots.

We won’t let him get away with it.

We will continue to organize, to stand together, to talk to our neighbors,
to spread our message day in and day out until the movement to defeat
Donald Trump and the far-right is completely unstoppable.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20251205-omar-split-movement.0__o8W&refcode=em20251205-1685&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1003&akid=1685%2E342411%2E5yBGqt ]So Peter — I am humbly asking you to split a contribution
between my campaign and the Squad Victory Fund today to help us create
that unstoppable movement. Despite Trump’s threats and dangerous policies,
we have The People on our side and we will not be defeated. Every dollar
helps us keep up the fight.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20251205-omar-split-movement.0__o8W&refcode=em20251205-1685&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1022&akid=1685%2E342411%2E5yBGqt ]Contribute ››

In solidarity,

Ilhan Omar

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe:

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.squadvictoryfund.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1685%2E342411%2E5yBGqt

Paid for by The Squad Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

In addition to what’s legally required, The Squad Victory Fund does not accept contributions from: (1) corporations, associations, or their PACs; (2) individuals registered as federal lobbyists or under FARA; (3) from the PACs, lobbyists, or SEC-named executives of fossil fuel companies; or (4) from the Fraternal Order of Police.

Contributions or gifts to The Squad Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

Contributions to The Squad Victory Fund will be allocated equally among: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

Any contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

After contributing to The Squad Victory Fund, you will automatically be signed up to receive email updates from Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, and Rashida Tlaib for Congress. You may of course opt-out by unsubscribing at any time.

To contribute via check, please address to The Squad Victory Fund c/o MBA Consulting Group, PO Box 15845, Washington D.C., 20003

Email us: info@squadvictoryfund.com

subject: Donald Trump is taking a sledgehammer to our justice system

body:
|
#
---
Peter, Earlier this week, the Trump administration fired eight immigration judges right here in New York City — including the assistant chief judge at 26 Federal Plaza. These judges are just a few of the nearly 100 immigration judges fired across the country this year. And you won’t be surprised to learn that these judges all have one thing in common: they followed the law and didn’t roll over to Trump’s lawless efforts to deport immigrants seeking legal pathways to come to this country. **This isn’t justice. This is corruption in real time.** And it gets even worse: Trump has only replaced 36 of them. Meanwhile, the immigration court backlog stands at 3.7 MILLION cases, with some asylum hearings now scheduled for 2029. Judges are supposed to be impartial. They hear cases, weigh evidence, and rule based on law — not political pressure. But Trump doesn't want independent judges. He wants loyalists who will greenlight his agenda without question. Lawyers are already reporting that judges are afraid to rule against ICE, worried they'll be the next one fired. But when fear replaces impartiality, you don't have equal justice under the law. You have a rubber stamp that only exists in autocracies. But I didn't come to Congress to watch an unhinged, wannabe dictator destroy our country. I came here to fight for the Constitution and the rule of law. **If we’re going to protect our democracy, we need to take back the House next year — period. Given the Republicans' complete abdication of their constitutional duty, that is the only way to put a check on Donald Trump.** But Republicans and their billionaire pals are going to do (and spend) whatever it takes to keep their grip on power, so if we’re going to fight back and win, we need grassroots Democrats like you on our team. **[So please, chip in whatever you can afford today to help us flip the House blue in 2026 and together, we can end Trump’s unchecked corruption:](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120705323/587098473/624619952?refcode=AU_DSG_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251205_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0YTVkOTZjNy0yNmQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CZByIYFFa0YSeDpm6MF-ihHIfa1WD6aL8_Apf-79sbE=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120705324/587098474/1749249761?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=AU_DSG_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251205_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0YTVkOTZjNy0yNmQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CZByIYFFa0YSeDpm6MF-ihHIfa1WD6aL8_Apf-79sbE=)

name: Dan Crenshaw
subject: Texas isn’t done fighting. And neither am I.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan Crenshaw for Congress]
body:
[Your campaign brief for December 5, 2025](https://secure.winred.com/crenshawforcongress/texasisntdonefighting)

Peter-

This week, I called out the left’s disingenuous and manufactured outrage over the lawful, deliberate strike on a Venezuelan narcoterrorist drug boat. I also called out Gavin Newsom for his brazen admission of the New Democratic Party’s ultimate goal: turning America into a failed socialist state.

And I officially filed for re-election to continue serving the 2nd Congressional District of Texas. Please take a moment to watch my campaign launch video and hear directly from constituents about my proven record of leadership for TX-02.

[TEXAS ISN'T DONE FIGHTING. AND NEITHER AM I.](https://secure.winred.com/crenshawforcongress/texasisntdonefighting)

[CLICK HERE OR ON THE IMAGE ABOVE TO WATCH](https://secure.winred.com/crenshawforcongress/texasisntdonefighting)

I’ve been called to serve for my entire life. First as a Navy SEAL, now as your Congressman. The fight is different, but the mission is the same: protect our freedom, defend the Constitution, and always put America First.

Now, more than ever, President Trump needs reinforcements in Congress to keep restoring the American Dream. Together we can finish what we started and build a stronger, freer, and more prosperous America for generations to come.

As always, thank you for your continued support of my campaign. I cannot win re-election without committed patriots like you in my corner. If you have not donated to my campaign and are able to, I would greatly appreciate any contribution you can spare so that I have the resources necessary to win my primary next March and continue fighting for you in Congress.

[CONTRIBUTE NOW >>](https://secure.winred.com/crenshawforcongress/texasisntdonefighting)

Now, here's your brief.

NO, KILLING NARCOTERRORISTS IS NOT A “WAR CRIME”

[Fox News: Crenshaw defends alleged "double tap" strike](https://x.com/DanCrenshawTX/status/1996745632287977536)

[CLICK HERE TO WATCH](https://x.com/DanCrenshawTX/status/1996745632287977536)

The left’s latest symptom of Trump Derangement Syndrome is fake outrage over a double tap drone strike that took out a Venezuelan narcoterrorist drug boat. People who have never served in the military and have ZERO knowledge about Rules of Engagement are calling it a “war crime.” Nothing could be further from the truth.

This was a lawful, deliberate strike, not a reckless rogue operation. And it’s exactly what we should expect our military to do when engaging hostile targets – like narcoterrorists who will stop at nothing to smuggle the fentanyl poison into our country that’s responsible for killing hundreds of thousands of Americans.

Our military is trained to finish the job when a threat remains. How do I know? Because I’ve been on real-world counterterrorism missions. I can’t recall any point in my time as a SEAL where we strike a group, whether that’s a building or a boat or a vehicle, and then we were, like, ‘Oh, well, there’s survivors. We have to go. We can’t kill them.’ Of course we killed them.

The outrage we’re seeing? It’s all manufactured and disingenuous. And as Marc Thiessen lays out in [this article](https://www.washingtonpost.com/opinions/2025/12/04/trump-hegseths-drug-boat-strike-obama/), even Democrat administrations routinely used this playbook. The only reason they’re suddenly “outraged” is because President Trump is in charge.

THE NEW DEMOCRATIC PARTY IS A SOCIALIST PARTY

[Gavin Newson sums up the true intent of the Democratic Party](https://x.com/DanCrenshawTX/status/1996418348179993084)

[CLICK HERE OR ON THE IMAGE ABOVE TO WATCH](https://x.com/DanCrenshawTX/status/1996418348179993084)

Gavin Newsom summed up the new Democratic Party’s brazen socialist philosophy this week: “If we don’t democratize our economy and allow for the distribution of wealth, our democracy will die.”

Let’s translate: “democratize the economy” is feel-good-liberal-speak for “government take over of the economy.” It has never worked and always leads to ruin. And redistribution? According to [most measures](https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/federal/latest-federal-income-tax-data-2025/) we have the MOST progressive tax system in the world. The top 10% pay about 72% of all federal taxes. The top 1% pay about 40%. The top 50% pay 97% of taxes.

So yeah, the wealth is already massively redistributed. Socialists will never tell you what a “fair” tax rate is, will they? They just want more and more and more. There is no limiting principle, no underlying philosophy. Just the bitter politics of resentment, designed to mislead voters and bribe them for their vote.

This is the same socialist philosophy that has ruined countless societies and lives over the past two centuries. And America will meet a similar end if Democrats ever regain control of Congress and the White House.

SOCIAL MEDIA

[There was no debate to be had here. This was a lawful strike.](https://x.com/DanCrenshawTX/status/1996998974377431515)

[It's funny that the loudest voices screaming 'authoritarianism'...](https://x.com/DanCrenshawTX/status/1994893923257356771)

[Harry Sisson: “if you don’t let people kill their babies, you’re a FASCIST” Also Harry Sisson: “pwease dont kill the tewwowists"](https://x.com/DanCrenshawTX/status/1996631999264837732)

NEWS

clean_body: Peter- This week, I called out the left’s disingenuous and manufactured outrage over the lawful, deliberate strike on a Venezuelan narcoterrorist drug boat. I also called out Gavin Newsom for his brazen admission of the New Democratic Party’s ultimate goal: turning America into a failed socialist state. And I officially filed for re-election to continue serving the 2nd Congressional District of Texas. Please take a moment to watch my campaign launch video and hear directly from constituents about my proven record of leadership for TX-02. I’ve been called to serve for my entire life. First as a Navy SEAL, now as your Congressman. The fight is different, but the mission is the same: protect our freedom, defend the Constitution, and always put America First. Now, more than ever, President Trump needs reinforcements in Congress to keep restoring the American Dream. Together we can finish what we started and build a stronger, freer, and more prosperous America for generations to come. As always, thank you for your continued support of my campaign. I cannot win re-election without committed patriots like you in my corner. If you have not donated to my campaign and are able to, I would greatly appreciate any contribution you can spare so that I have the resources necessary to win my primary next March and continue fighting for you in Congress. Now, here's your brief. NO, KILLING NARCOTERRORISTS IS NOT A “WAR CRIME” The left’s latest symptom of Trump Derangement Syndrome is fake outrage over a double tap drone strike that took out a Venezuelan narcoterrorist drug boat. People who have never served in the military and have ZERO knowledge about Rules of Engagement are calling it a “war crime.” Nothing could be further from the truth. This was a lawful, deliberate strike, not a reckless rogue operation. And it’s exactly what we should expect our military to do when engaging hostile targets – like narcoterrorists who will stop at nothing to smuggle the fentanyl poison into our country that’s responsible for killing hundreds of thousands of Americans. Our military is trained to finish the job when a threat remains. How do I know? Because I’ve been on real-world counterterrorism missions. I can’t recall any point in my time as a SEAL where we strike a group, whether that’s a building or a boat or a vehicle, and then we were, like, ‘Oh, well, there’s survivors. We have to go. We can’t kill them.’ Of course we killed them. The outrage we’re seeing? It’s all manufactured and disingenuous. And as Marc Thiessen lays out in , even Democrat administrations routinely used this playbook. The only reason they’re suddenly “outraged” is because President Trump is in charge. THE NEW DEMOCRATIC PARTY IS A SOCIALIST PARTY Gavin Newsom summed up the new Democratic Party’s brazen socialist philosophy this week: “If we don’t democratize our economy and allow for the distribution of wealth, our democracy will die.” Let’s translate: “democratize the economy” is feel-good-liberal-speak for “government take over of the economy.” It has never worked and always leads to ruin. And redistribution? According to we have the MOST progressive tax system in the world. The top 10% pay about 72% of all federal taxes. The top 1% pay about 40%. The top 50% pay 97% of taxes. So yeah, the wealth is already massively redistributed. Socialists will never tell you what a “fair” tax rate is, will they? They just want more and more and more. There is no limiting principle, no underlying philosophy. Just the bitter politics of resentment, designed to mislead voters and bribe them for their vote. This is the same socialist philosophy that has ruined countless societies and lives over the past two centuries. And America will meet a similar end if Democrats ever regain control of Congress and the White House. SOCIAL MEDIA NEWS WASHINGTON REPORTER: WSJ: FOX NEWS: That's it for this week's campaign brief. Check out my to catch up on the latest news about my campaign, learn more about where I stand on the issues, volunteer, and order a yard sign. If we fall short of our goal tonight, it will set the stage for a complete Democrat takeover. Every GOP victory in the past few weeks will be gone overnight!
